name: Stone Golem
environment: Any
description: A massive humanoid figure of stone, animated by powerful magic.

name: Water Elemental
environment: Elemental Plane of Water
description: A swirling mass of living water, surging with elemental power.

name: Mira the Shopkeeper
environment: Town
description: A friendly shopkeeper ready to buy and sell goods.
dialogue: Hello, traveler! Looking to buy, sell, or appraise? My wares are the best in town.

ai_persona.voice: Scholarly, precise, slightly distracted. Complete sentences. Rarely uses contractions. Gets animated discussing history.
ai_persona.motivation: Preserve the historical record of Custos do Aeternos and the old traditions before they are forgotten.
secrets: casual:Knows hidden passages beneath Custos do Aeternos from her years cataloguing old stock rooms
ai_persona.opening_line: Ah, a customer! Or perhaps just someone looking for conversation? Either way, you have come to the right place.
ai_persona.farewell_line: Take care, traveler. And if you find anything interesting out there, I buy at fair prices.

chat_scenario.title: The Shopkeeper's Tales
chat_scenario.opening_narration: Mira's shop smells of leather and oil. Every shelf tells a story -- the dented shield from Gatefall, the silk scarf from Tidebloom Reach.

name: Gentle Deer
environment: Temperate forests
description: A gentle deer grazes quietly at the edge of the altar, unafraid of your presence.

name: Ape
environment: Warm forests
description: A powerful omnivore resembling a gorilla, ready to defend its territory.

name: Baboon
environment: Warm forests
description: A powerful and aggressive primate, usually found in troops.

name: Badger
environment: Temperate forests
description: A furry animal with a squat, powerful body and long claws for digging.

name: Bat
environment: Temperate forests
description: A small, insectivorous bat with keen senses and quick flight.

name: Black Bear
environment: Temperate forests
description: A forest-dwelling omnivore, dangerous if threatened.

name: Brown Bear
environment: Cold forests
description: A massive carnivore, bad-tempered and territorial.

name: Polar Bear
environment: Cold forests
description: A long, lean carnivore, slightly taller than brown bears.

name: Bison
environment: Temperate plains
description: A large herd animal, can be aggressive when protecting young.

name: Boar
environment: Temperate forests
description: A wild swine, bad-tempered and usually charges intruders.

name: Camel
environment: Warm deserts
description: A dromedary camel, known for endurance in deserts.

name: Cat
environment: Temperate forests
description: A small, agile feline with sharp senses.

name: Goble Test
environment: Any
description: A curious goblin-like creature with a mischievous grin, here for testing purposes on the Street of Introspection.

name: Wolf
environment: Temperate forests
description: A lean, gray-furred wolf with alert eyes and a cautious, predatory stance.

name: Guildmaster Thorin
environment: Town
description: A weathered veteran who trains adventurers in the martial and arcane arts.
dialogue: Welcome, adventurer. I can train your skills and abilities. Use 'practice' to improve skills or 'train' to increase an ability score.

name: Banker Goldsworth
environment: Town
description: A meticulous halfling who manages the vault with exacting precision.
dialogue: Good day. I can hold your gold safely. Use 'deposit <amount>', 'withdraw <amount>', or 'balance' to manage your account.

name: Smith Ironforge
environment: Town
description: A broad-shouldered dwarf surrounded by the ring of hammer on anvil.
dialogue: Bring me your broken gear and I'll have it right as rain. Use 'repair <item>' or 'repairall' to fix your equipment.

name: Merchant Lyssa
environment: Town
description: A sharp-eyed trader with wares from across the realm. Her eyes miss nothing, and her ledgers are immaculate.
dialogue: Browse my wares, traveler! Use 'list' to see what I have, 'buy <item>' to purchase, or 'sell <item>' to sell your goods.

ai_persona.voice: Warm on the surface, calculating underneath. Laughs easily. Always knows the price of everything.
ai_persona.motivation: Run the best-stocked shop in Custos do Aeternos. Know every trade route and every merchant's weakness.
secrets: casual:Has contacts in every region who keep her informed of rare goods before they hit the market
ai_persona.opening_line: Friend! You have the look of someone who knows quality when they see it. What can Lyssa provide today?
ai_persona.farewell_line: Come back when your pockets are heavy. I will have something new for you.

chat_scenario.title: The Merchant's Eye
chat_scenario.opening_narration: Lyssa's shop is organized chaos -- crates of goods from every region stacked floor to ceiling. She looks up from her ledger with an appraising eye.

name: Abatwa
environment: The Eternal Steppe (open grasslands, anthills, rocky uplands near Warg territory)
organization: Solitary, pair, hunting band (3-8), or tribe (20-60 with 1d4 ant mounts per 10 Abatwa)
description: Crouched in the shadow of a single blade of steppe-grass, nearly invisible against the red earth, a figure no taller than a man's thumb draws a bow strung with spider-silk. Its arrow -- a splinter of bone dipped in something that smells faintly of turned soil -- is aimed with the steady confidence of a hunter who has never missed.

name: Air Elemental, Small
environment: Any windy locale; most common in the Eternal Steppe (storm-paths, open grasslands), Giant's Teeth (high passes, summit winds), and Twin Rivers (Great Forest canopy during storms)
description: The wind gathers where no wind should be. Dust rises in a spiral, leaves whip into a column, and then it is there -- a shape made of rushing air, its outline barely visible except where debris traces its form. It smells of high places: ozone, cold rain, the thin air above the mountaintops. It does not speak, but the sound it makes as it moves is like breathing drawn through a cathedral.

environment: As Small Air Elemental

name: Ankheg
environment: The Eternal Steppe (cultivated farmland and open grassland, especially along the Kinsweave border); secondary presence in Twin Rivers (tilled lowland fields near the Great River) and Tidebloom Reach (drained marshland converted to cropland near Hillmeet)
description: The wheat shivers and collapses in a line, stalks folding inward like a wound stitching itself shut. Then the earth cracks open and something huge and chitinous erupts -- six legs driving it skyward, mandibles gaping, a hiss of acrid vapor rising from its mouth. It is the color of the soil it lives in, which means you never see it until the ground beneath you gives way.

name: Ankheg
environment: The Eternal Steppe (cultivated farmland and open grassland, especially along the Kinsweave border); secondary presence in Twin Rivers (tilled lowland fields near the Great River) and Tidebloom Reach (drained marshland converted to cropland near Hillmeet)

name: Annis Hag
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- corrupted; see Society
environment: The Eternal Steppe (rocky foothills along the mountain borders, deep gullies, abandoned cave systems); secondary in Giant's Teeth foothills (Vestri territory border) and the Kinsweave (forested highland valleys)
organization: Solitary or covey (3 hags of any type)
description: She is nine feet tall and could be mistaken for a hill giant at a distance -- except that hill giants do not move like that, with the hunched, stalking patience of a thing that enjoys the chase more than the kill. Her skin is the blue-black of a deep bruise. Her fingers end in iron-hard nails the color of rust, and when she smiles -- and she will smile -- her teeth are flat, broad, and set in a jaw that could crack a horse's femur.

name: Blink Dog
environment: The Eternal Steppe (open grasslands, particularly the western steppe where Mytroan herding routes cross Warg territory); secondary presence at the Kinsweave-Steppe border
description: It is there and then it is not. A flicker of gold-brown fur, a flash of bright, intelligent eyes, and the space where the dog was standing is empty -- the air closing behind it with a faint pop of displaced pressure. Then it is behind you, wagging its tail, looking at you with an expression that manages to combine the loyalty of a hound with the smugness of a creature that knows you cannot catch it.

name: Blink Dog

name: Almas
flags: elemental_affinity:None native (Goblinoid, extraplanar origin); trace Earth attunement developing in steppe-born generations
environment: The Eternal Steppe (deep grasslands, rocky outcrops, the wild margins between Warg territory and Mytroan herding routes)
organization: Solitary, pair, family group (3-6), or band (7-15 plus 1d4 young)
description: It stands at the edge of the Warg camp's firelight, half-hidden in the long grass -- stocky, broad-shouldered, covered head to foot in reddish-brown hair matted with steppe-dust. Its face is flat and heavy-browed, its eyes small and sharp. It carries no weapon, wears no clothing, but there is nothing animal about the way it watches the camp. It is reading the fire. It is counting the Wargs. It is deciding whether tonight is a good night to trade.

name: Almas
flags: elemental_affinity:None native (Goblinoid, extraplanar origin); trace Earth attunement developing in steppe-born generations

name: Cockatrice
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- like the basilisk, the cockatrice's petrification is an expression of Earth dominance
environment: The Eternal Steppe (open grasslands, scrubby hillsides, field-margins near agricultural settlements); secondary presence in the Kinsweave lowlands and along the forest-steppe border
description: It struts out of the tall grass like it owns the steppe -- a rooster-sized creature with the body of a scrawny chicken, the wings of a bat, and a tail that ends in a scaly, serpentine point. Its feathers are patchy and iridescent, shifting between sickly green and tarnished bronze. Its eyes are beady, stupid, and mean. It looks ridiculous. Then it pecks a field-mouse and the mouse turns to stone mid-squeak, and you stop laughing.

name: Cockatrice
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- like the basilisk, the cockatrice's petrification is an expression of Earth dominance

name: Centaur
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- dual affinity; earth-rooted but wind-spirited
environment: Forest-Steppe Border (the transition zone where the Great Forests thin into grassland, particularly along the Kinsweave-Steppe frontier and the western forest-edge near the Twin Rivers)
organization: Solitary, company (5-8), troop (8-18 plus 1 leader of 2nd-5th level), or herd (20-150 adults plus 30% noncombatants plus 10 3rd-level sergeants plus 5 5th-level lieutenants plus 1 7th-level leader)
description: It comes out of the tree-line at a canter -- the lower body of a heavy steppe-horse, the upper body of a broad-chested warrior, carrying a horn-and-sinew composite bow that would take a human archer both hands to string. Its eyes scan the grassland with the alert, unhurried confidence of a creature that has never needed to run from anything. The wind shifts, and you realise there are more of them in the trees -- a dozen, perhaps more, watching you with the same calculating patience.

name: Dire Lion
flags: elemental_affinity:Fire/Earth (the sun-hunter -- dire lions carry the Lady of Fire's warmth in their blood)
environment: Eternal Steppe (the deep grasslands far from settlements, particularly the central steppe where the grass grows shoulder-high and the horizon is unbroken in every direction)
description: Large Animal

name: Dire Wolf
flags: elemental_affinity:Air/Earth (the wind-runner -- dire wolves carry the steppe wind in their stride)
environment: Forests and Eternal Steppe (the forest-steppe border, deep woodlands, and the open grasslands -- dire wolves range more widely than any other dire animal)
organization: Solitary, pair, or pack (1 dire wolf leading 5-8 normal wolves)
description: Large Animal

name: Dire Wolf

organization: Solitary, pair, or pack (1 dire wolf leading 5-8 normal wolves)
description: Large Animal

name: Gnoll
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- gnolls exist outside the elemental framework, like all non-Kin peoples
environment: Steppe Margins (the dry, broken terrain between the Eternal Steppe and the foothills of the Giant's Teeth and Scorchspires -- badlands, scrubland, and seasonal water-courses)
organization: Solitary, pair, hunting party (2-5 plus 1-2 hyenas), or band (10-100 plus 50% noncombatants plus 1 sergeant of 3rd level per 20 adults plus 1 leader of 4th-6th level plus 5-8 hyenas)
description: The hyena-headed warrior watches you from the ridge with an intelligence that has nothing to do with its animal face. It wears armour -- not well-made, not beautiful, but functional, scavenged and resized and maintained. It holds a flail in one hand with the ease of long practice. Behind it, on the reverse slope, you can hear others -- three, five, maybe more -- and the yipping laughter that is either communication or amusement or both.

name: Gnoll
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- gnolls exist outside the elemental framework, like all non-Kin peoples

name: Gorgon
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of Fire (Fire) -- the gorgon is metal incarnate, the place where stone meets fire and becomes iron
environment: Eternal Steppe (open grasslands, particularly the dry central steppe where iron-rich earth supports sparse, tough vegetation; secondary presence in the Scorchspire foothills where volcanic soils contain high metal concentrations)
description: The iron bull stands in the grassland and the grassland is afraid of it. That is the only way to describe what you see: the grass bends away from it, the insects avoid it, the air around it tastes of metal filings. It is the size of a large ox, armoured in overlapping plates of dark iron that are not armour -- they are its hide, grown from its flesh, as much a part of it as your skin is part of you. It lowers its head. Steam rises from its nostrils. The steam smells like a forge on a bad day.

name: Impundulu
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lady of Fire (Fire) -- lightning; the impundulu is the storm made predatory, the point where wind becomes fire
environment: Deep Steppe (the open grasslands of the Eternal Steppe's interior, far from mountains or forest -- flat terrain where storms build without obstruction and the impundulu can hunt unimpeded by canopy or cliff)
description: The storm comes from nowhere. The sky was clear, the wind was calm, and now the air tastes of copper and the hair on your arms stands straight. Lightning strikes the ground fifty feet away -- not from a cloud but from a bird. It is enormous, black-feathered, white-winged, riding the static charge it generates like a ship rides a wave. Its eyes are blue-white and crackling. When it screams, the sound is thunder.

name: Manticore
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of Fire (Fire) -- predatory; the dry heat of sun-baked stone, the patience of rock that waits to fall
environment: Warm Hills and Marshes (the low, scrub-covered hills between the Eternal Steppe and the Scorchspires -- the transitional terrain where grassland gives way to volcanic foothills; secondary populations along the Tidebloom Reach's drier margins)
description: The thing on the ridge has the body of a lion, the face of a man, and a tail bristling with spines the length of crossbow bolts. It has been watching your party for the better part of an hour with the patient, calculating intelligence of a predator that understands geometry. It is not attacking because the range is wrong and it knows the range is wrong. When you cross the open ground between the tree-line and the ridge -- that killing-ground of bare earth and low scrub where there is nothing t...

name: Pegasus
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- pure; the cleanest, most joyful air-signature in Oreka, flight as an expression of freedom
environment: Eternal Steppe (the open grasslands -- particularly the high-steppe regions where the horizon is unbroken and the wind runs unimpeded, far from the forest-edge and far from mountains, in the sky above the steppe's heart)
description: The herd moves across the steppe-sky at dawn -- a dozen winged horses silhouetted against the pink-gold horizon, their flight-formation as precise as any Mytroan cavalry column but achieved without riders, without commands, without anything except the collective grace of creatures for whom the sky is home. They are white, or silver, or pale gold, and they fly with a wingspan that should not support a horse-body but does, because the wind beneath them is not entirely wind. Something else holds ...

name: Skinwalker
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone / Youngest Brother (Earth/Air) -- stolen, shifting; a skinwalker's resonance is not its own -- it is a corrupted fragment of whatever creature's form it has most recently worn, earth and air twisted into something that mimics life without possessing it
environment: Eternal Steppe (the open grasslands, river-valleys, and camp-grounds of Mytroan territory -- specifically the edges of Mytroan settlements and herding-routes, where the steppe meets the dark and the boundary between human space and wild space is thinnest)
description: The Mytroan camp-fire is burning low, and the herder on watch is the last one awake. The horse at the picket-line is nervous -- stamping, pulling at its tether, nostrils flared. The herder watches the horse and does not watch the darkness behind him, and that is the mistake. Something comes out of the steppe on four legs -- a wolf, large and dark, moving with a wolf's easy lope -- and then it stops, and the wolf unfolds. The hind-legs straighten. The forelegs become arms. The spine elongates. Th...

name: Storm Wisp
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- raw; the purest, most unrefined expression of the Youngest Brother's domain, wind as playful electrical discharge, the air-element at its most mischievous and least controlled
environment: Great Forests (primary -- the forest canopy and clearings during storm season) and Eternal Steppe (secondary -- open grasslands during thunderstorms); anywhere with frequent storms and sustained wind
advancement: 4-6 HD (Tiny); 7-9 HD (Small -- transition toward Storm Herald)
description: The air crackles. A blue-white sphere of light -- no larger than a fist, but bright enough to leave afterimages -- darts between the trees, pauses, darts again. It moves like a dragonfly: sudden direction-changes, instantaneous stops, bursts of speed that defy any creature with mass. Your arm-hair stands up. The taste of metal fills your mouth. The Pasua scout beside you grins. 'Wisp,' she says. 'Don't touch. Don't touch anything metal.'

name: Storm Wisp
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- raw; the purest, most unrefined expression of the Youngest Brother's domain, wind as playful electrical discharge, the air-element at its most mischievous and least controlled
environment: Great Forests (primary -- the forest canopy and clearings during storm season) and Eternal Steppe (secondary -- open grasslands during thunderstorms); anywhere with frequent storms and sustained wind

name: Worg
flags: elemental_affinity:Wild Static -- worgs are intelligent non-Kin creatures that register on Kin-sense as unreadable presence, the same wild-static signature shared by all non-Kin sapients (goblins, goblinoids, Wargs); recognisable, alive, but not elementally identifiable
environment: Any (worgs range across all of Oreka's temperate and cold regions -- Eternal Steppe, Great Forest edges, mountain foothills, and the margins of every settled area)
description: The wolf is too large, too intelligent, and looking at you with an expression that wolves do not have. Wolves assess threats. This creature is assessing you -- your armour, your weapons, the way you hold your sword. It understands equipment. It understands threat-hierarchy. And when it speaks -- growling, accented Common -- it says: 'Move. Or I move you.'

name: Wolf
flags: elemental_affinity:Species Harmony -- wolves carry the species-specific resonance of their kind, distinct from elemental Kin-sense but part of the world's ecological web
environment: Forests / Steppe (the Great Forests' edges and the Eternal Steppe -- anywhere pack-hunting in open or semi-open terrain is viable)
description: Medium Animal

name: Wolf

environment: Forests / Steppe (the Great Forests' edges and the Eternal Steppe -- anywhere pack-hunting in open or semi-open terrain is viable)
description: Medium Animal

name: Winter Wolf
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother / Lady of the Sea (Air/Water) -- winter predator; the resonance of cold wind and frozen water combined, the two elements meeting in the winter-state that produces ice, frost, and the killing cold of the northern steppe
environment: Northern Steppe (the cold grasslands and snow-covered plains of the Eternal Steppe's northern reaches -- the border-zone between the steppe and the northern mountain-barrier where winters are long and wolves are large)
description: The wolf on the ridge is white -- white as the snow-field it stands on, invisible except for the eyes (amber, steady) and the breath (which is not breath but a thin, constant stream of ice-fog that curls from its jaws like smoke from a forge). It is larger than any wolf should be -- shoulder-height to a tall man -- and it watches you with an intelligence that wolves do not possess. When it speaks -- and it will speak, because winter wolves speak -- the voice is a low, grinding rasp, and the words ...

name: Tokoloshe
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- mischief-weight; a small, dense, heavy earth-resonance that feels disproportionately solid for the creature's size -- the Lord of Stone's domain expressed as something that should not be as present as it is
environment: Warg Camps (the semi-permanent camp-grounds and seasonal gathering-sites of Warg pack territories on the Eternal Steppe -- specifically the margins of Warg settlements where food-stores, sleeping-shelters, and goblin-quarter boundaries create the edges and in-between spaces that tokoloshe inhabit)
description: The Warg camp is quiet -- the kind of quiet that happens after midnight when the fires are low and the pack-guards are watching the perimeter. Something small moves between the sleeping-shelters. It is knee-height, dark-skinned, hairless, and it walks with a rolling gait that suggests its legs are not quite the right length for its body. It carries a stone in one hand -- a smooth, river-polished stone -- and it is heading for the food-stores with the determined, focused tread of a creature that ...

name: Yer-Sub
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- deep-soil guardian; the resonance of the bedrock layer beneath the steppe, the Lord of Stone's domain in its nurturing, foundation-providing aspect -- the earth that supports all growth, all roots, all life on the surface
environment: Eternal Steppe (Mytroan) -- the deep grasslands, seasonal migration routes, and sacred earth-sites where the steppe's bedrock approaches the surface; yer-sub manifest at locations of geological significance: aquifer convergence-points, natural springs, and places where the stone-root is strongest
advancement: 11-15 HD (Large); 16-20 HD (Huge -- the deepest, oldest yer-sub, whose forms incorporate fossil-stone and aquifer-crystal)
description: The steppe splits. Not violently -- there is no earthquake, no crack of breaking stone -- but a seam appears in the earth as if the ground were opening a door. From within rises a figure of compressed soil, clay, and living stone: humanoid in outline but faceless, broad as a wall, and rooted to the earth by tendrils of bedrock that trail from its legs like a gown. It smells of rain-wet soil and deep stone. When it moves, the steppe moves with it -- grass leans toward it, stones shift, and for a ...

name: Poludnitsa
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) -- noon-blaze; the killing heat of midday sun, fire expressed not as flame but as radiance so intense it stops the heart, the Lady of Fire's destructive aspect turned to punishment
environment: Volcanic Lowlands (the cultivated fields, terrace-farms, and open ground of Rarozhki agricultural territory in the Scorchspire lowlands -- specifically the exposed, sun-baked areas where field-workers labour during the growing season and where noon-heat is most intense)
description: Noon in the volcanic lowlands is a thing that has weight. The sun does not simply shine -- it presses, flattens, bakes the ground until the air above the dark soil shimmers and bends. Nothing moves at noon. The Rarozhki field-workers have retreated to the shade-shelters, the goats stand motionless under the rock-overhangs, and even the insects have gone quiet. Into this silence, something walks. A woman, tall, dressed in white, moving across the open field with a step that leaves no mark on th...

name: Thunderbird
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother / Lady of the Sea (Air/Water) -- living storm; the resonance of a thunderstorm given physical form -- not merely wind or rain or lightning but the entire atmospheric system compressed into a body of feather, talon, and fury
environment: Eternal Steppe (the vast open grasslands of the Mytroan -- specifically the storm-corridors where continental weather-systems cross the steppe, the atmospheric zones where the Youngest Brother and the Lady of the Sea's domains interact most violently)
description: The steppe-sky darkens -- not the gradual darkening of a storm approaching but the sudden, absolute darkening of something vast passing between you and the sun. The shadow covers acres. The wingbeat that follows is not wind -- it is thunder, a detonation of compressed air that flattens the grass for a hundred yards in every direction. The Mytroan wind-caller beside you drops to one knee, presses his forehead to the steppe-grass, and speaks a name. The name is old. The thing above you is older. ...

flags: elemental_affinity:None -- skeletons are animated objects, not elemental beings; they have no resonance, no signature, and no connection to the elemental system -- they are bones made to move by magic, nothing more
environment: Any (wherever the dead are raised -- battlefields, crypts, Dómnathar tunnels, necromantic workshops, cursed sites where ambient death-energy animates remains without a spellcaster's intervention)

name: Zombie
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- a zombie's resonance is absence, like a wight's, but less -- a wight hungers, a zombie does not even have that; it is the absolute minimum of undeath, meat animated by the thinnest thread of necromantic energy, registering on Kin-sense as 'dead thing moving' with no further complexity
environment: Any (zombies appear wherever necromantic contamination meets preserved corpses -- battlefields, mass graves, Dómnathar-influenced zones, and locations where the boundary between life and death has been thinned by catastrophe or dark magic)
description: It walks. That is the horror of it -- not the rot, not the smell, not the exposed bone or the grey-green skin or the blank, staring eyes. The horror is that it walks. It was a person. You can see the person it was -- the build, the clothing, the face. And now it walks toward you with its arms out and its mouth open and no thought behind the eyes at all. Nothing. Not hunger, not malice, not pain. Nothing. It is meat that remembers how to move and has forgotten everything else.

name: Wight
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- a wight is elemental absence, the dead animated by hunger without any of the elemental identity that defined them in life
environment: Any (barrows, crypts, battlefields, and sealed burial-chambers across Oreka -- wherever the dead were interred without proper rites or where necromantic contamination has reached preserved corpses)
description: The barrow opens into a chamber that has not been touched by light since it was sealed. The figure inside is standing -- not lying, not seated, but standing against the back wall with its arms at its sides and its eyes open. It was Kin once. You can tell by the bone-structure, the remnants of clothing, the corroded weapon still gripped in one hand. But the eyes are wrong -- flat, lightless, and lit from within by something that is not life. It sees you. It steps forward. The temperature drops.

name: Allip
flags: elemental_affinity:Fragmented -- distorted remnant of original Kin's elemental identity
environment: Kinsweave (border-kingdom ruins, abandoned guild halls, fallen court chambers); Tidebloom Reach (drowned villages near Hillmeet and Foxfen, waterlogged libraries). Rare in the Infinite Desert near collapsed Giant record-halls.
description: It was a scholar, once. You can tell by the way it moves its hands -- still tracing letters on invisible pages, still turning invisible leaves. Its mouth opens and closes ceaselessly, pouring out a stream of whispered words that resolve into nothing, loop back on themselves, and begin again. The sound is not language. It is the sound of a mind that broke so completely that death could not stop it from trying to finish the thought.

name: Banshee
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- distorted; the banshee's wail is wind corrupted by grief
environment: The Kinsweave (border-kingdom ruins, fallen courts, abandoned keeps along the contested frontiers); secondary presence along the Lament Road and in the ruins of the Twelve Kingdoms
description: You hear her before you see her -- a sound that begins as a woman weeping, rises to a scream, and then passes beyond screaming into a frequency that lives behind your teeth and in the bones of your skull. The air turns cold. And then she is there, translucent and grey, her mouth open wider than a living woman's mouth could open, her hair streaming upward as though she were drowning in reverse.

name: Banshee
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- distorted; the banshee's wail is wind corrupted by grief

name: Aranea
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- dual affinity, with Air dominant
environment: Twin Rivers and Kinsweave (Great Forests, deep canopy; especially common in the Hasura heartlands and the old-growth Deepwood border)
description: It looks like a spider -- a hand-sized spider, mottled brown and grey, building a web between two branches with the unhurried patience of a craftsman who has all day. Then you notice the web's pattern: not random, not instinctive, but geometric -- a mandala of concentric shapes that no spider should know how to make. And then it turns, and the cluster of eyes that looks back at you has something behind them that spiders' eyes do not have.

name: Sand Wraith
flags: elemental_affinity:All four Elemental Lords (fragmented); strongest resonance with the Lord of Stone -- a Sand Wraith's elemental signature is the shattered remnant of a Giant's full four-element harmony, broken and looping like a record stuck in a groove
special_qualities: elemental resonance (all four - fragmented Giant echo)
environment: The Infinite Desert (Giant ruins, dune-seas; thickest concentration at the Sunken Thrones, Tharok's Pillar, and the deep dune-seas; rare sightings at the Apelian Sea's submerged Giant piers in the Deepwater Marches)
description: The ruin ahead is half-buried -- the upper columns of what was once a Giant plaza project from the dune like the ribs of a vast skeleton, and the sand between them moves in patterns that have nothing to do with the wind. A shape gathers in the air above the columns: tall, vaguely humanoid, composed entirely of swirling sand and grit. Dim amber light burns where its eyes should be. The sound it produces is less a voice than a sustained, low howl of desert wind forced through a space too narrow ...

name: Mohrg
environment: Kinsweave Ruins (the abandoned border-settlements, burned villages, and War-era fortifications of the Kinsweave -- the region where the northern Deceiver host's passage left the densest concentration of unburied dead and corrupted ground)
description: The ruin smelled wrong before you entered it -- a sickly-sweet rot that no amount of wind would carry away, layered over the cold-stone scent of the Kinsweave's abandoned border-settlements. Inside, the bodies are arranged. Not piled, not scattered -- arranged, in a manner that suggests intelligence, purpose, and a deeply unpleasant sense of humour. In the centre of the arrangement, the thing that made it stands upright: a skeleton wrapped in its own entrails, its ribcage open like a birdcage, ...

name: Mummy
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- undead; but the body was once Giant, and traces of the all-four-element resonance linger in the preservation-runes like an echo in a sealed room
environment: Giant Tombs (the sealed burial-complexes and mausoleum-vaults of Giant-era cities throughout the Infinite Desert -- the Sunken Thrones, the Dust-Vaults beneath Tharok's Pillar, and the deep tomb-complexes of the Giant's Teeth foothills)
description: The sarcophagus is not dwarven. It is Giant -- carved from a single block of basalt in proportions that no Kin hands shaped, covered in rune-script that predates written Dwarven by millennia. The lid has been moved. From inside, the smell of preservation-resin and dust and something else -- something dry and electric that makes the hair on your arms stand up. The thing that climbs out does not rush. It has been waiting for longer than your civilisation has existed, and it has the patience of st...

name: Shadow
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- Dómnathar void; a shadow registers on Kin-sense as active absence, the characteristic void-signature of the Dómnathar ecosystem -- not the natural emptiness of a dead zone but the hungry, purposeful nothing of the False Silence
environment: Dómnathar Zones (the periphery of Dómnathar tunnel-networks, Silence Breach zones, abandoned Dómnathar outposts, and any location where the False Silence has left a permanent residue -- shadows are the undead by-product of Dómnathar presence, forming where elemental resonance has been suppressed long enough for the void itself to develop hunger)
description: The torch dies. Not gutters, not flickers -- dies, snuffed as completely as if it had been plunged into water. The darkness that follows is not normal darkness. It has weight. It has direction. It moves toward you, and where it touches the wall, the stone loses its colour -- grey bleaching to white, moisture evaporating, warmth draining away. Something is in the dark, and it is not absence. It is presence -- the presence of nothing, the active malice of a void that is hungry.

name: Shadow
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- Dómnathar void; a shadow registers on Kin-sense as active absence, the characteristic void-signature of the Dómnathar ecosystem -- not the natural emptiness of a dead zone but the hungry, purposeful nothing of the False Silence

name: Svartálfar Shade
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- fading; the resonance of Vestri earth-attunement diminished by death but not extinguished -- a candle's afterglow where a forge-fire once burned, recognisably dwarven, recognisably diminished
environment: Abandoned Dwarf Halls (the sealed, evacuated, or forgotten mining-halls and forge-complexes of the Vestri Dwarven infrastructure -- specifically the halls abandoned during the centuries of contraction following the Fall of Aldenheim, where Vestri dead were left in memorial-chambers when the living withdrew)
description: The dwarf-hall is empty. It has been empty for a long time -- the forge-fires are cold, the tool-racks bare, the sleeping-quarters stripped of everything portable. The Vestri who abandoned this hall took everything they could carry and left everything they could not, and what they could not carry included their dead. The shade drifts through the doorway of the memorial-chamber with the slow, deliberate movement of something that is still performing a duty it was assigned a very long time ago. ...

name: Monstrous Spider
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- faint; the quiet attunement of small creatures that live in dark places close to stone
environment: Any (monstrous spiders are found throughout Oreka -- Great Forests, underground cave-systems, Giant ruins, Vestri mines, Dómnathar tunnel-fringes, and any enclosed environment where webs can be anchored; specific subspecies favour specific environments)
description: The web is not visible until you are in it. The silk is the colour of shadow and it clings to everything -- armour, hair, skin -- with an adhesive strength that is entirely out of proportion to its apparent fragility. Something moves at the edge of your torchlight. It has too many legs and too many eyes and it is larger than you want it to be.

name: Wraith
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- a wraith's resonance is burned absence, the elemental signature of fire-death -- not the extinguished-fire of the Ashen Hollow spectres but the burned-away identity of creatures that died in fire, their elemental connections consumed along with their bodies
environment: Burned Cities (the fire-ruined settlements, razed towns, and burned-out strongholds scattered across Oreka -- specifically the Reckoning-era burned cities of the Kinsweave and Tidebloom Reach, where the Dómnathar's surface campaigns used fire as a primary destruction-tool, and where the mass fire-death produced concentrations of wraith-spawning energy)
organization: Solitary, pair, or gang (3-5 wraiths, sometimes led by 1 dread wraith)
description: The burned city is dark -- dark in the way that fire-ruins are dark, the charred beams absorbing torchlight, the collapsed roofs creating pockets of absolute blackness that no flame seems to penetrate. Something moves in the blackness -- not a shadow (shadows are passive, projected by light) but something that moves as darkness, a shape of concentrated absence that slides between the ruins with deliberate, predatory intent. Where it passes, the air goes cold, and the coldness is not temperature...

name: Dryad
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) -- root and sap; the dryad is bound to the land and the water that sustains it
environment: Great Forests (old-growth oak groves, ancient forest, anywhere a single tree has stood long enough to develop fey resonance -- primarily Hasura territory, Twin Rivers old-growth, and the deeper Pasua forest-margins)
description: She is standing inside the oak. Not behind it -- inside it, her body half-merged with the bark, her skin the colour of heartwood, her hair a cascade of leaves that shift with a breeze that does not touch any other tree. Her eyes are green. Not metaphorically -- green the way chlorophyll is green, the green of photosynthesis, the green of something that feeds on light. She watches you with an expression that is not human curiosity. It is the expression of a tree noticing that something is standi...

environment: Any (wherever the dead are raised -- battlefields, crypts, Dómnathar tunnels, necromantic workshops, cursed sites where ambient death-energy animates remains without a spellcaster's intervention)

name: Dread Wraith
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- a dread wraith is elemental negation, a wound in the fabric where resonance used to exist
environment: Any (found wherever sufficient death-energy has accumulated -- the Lament of Kings, major battlefields of the Deceivers' War, the ruins of Hillmeet and Foxfen, and deep Giant tombs where the Thalorien's grief pooled into darkness)
organization: Solitary or gang (2-5 wraiths led by 1 dread wraith)
description: It comes through the wall without opening the wall. One moment the corridor is empty; the next, a shape of absolute darkness occupies the space where the torchlight ends -- not shadow, because shadow is the absence of light, and this is the presence of something else. It is cold the way a blade is sharp: not a quality of the air but a quality of the thing itself, radiating outward, turning breath to frost at thirty feet. Its face -- if the darkness where a face should be can be called a face -- ...

name: Earth Elemental, Large
description: The ground buckles. A section of stone floor -- ten feet across, solid granite -- rises, cracks, and reshapes itself into something upright, something that has arms and legs and a suggestion of a face carved into what might be a head. It smells of deep stone. It sounds like a quarry. When it moves, the ground shakes.

name: Draugr
flags: elemental_affinity:Corrupted -- the draugr carries a distorted echo of the Vestri Earth/Fire resonance, broken by death and further warped by the Dómnathar raising that failed to hold
environment: Tidebloom Reach and northern Kinsweave (barrow-mounds, battlefield graves, and ruined fortifications along the route the Deceiver host marched from Hillmeet to Aldenheim -- the 'Dead Road'); secondary presence in the Lament of Kings perimeter
description: The barrow-mound splits open. It has not been opened by tools -- the stone capstone has been pushed aside from within, scraped along the ground by hands that are still strong after three hundred years. What climbs out is wearing the remains of Vestri plate-armour -- Mithral Legion insignia still visible beneath the corrosion, the breastplate cracked where the killing blow landed. Its eyes glow the dull orange of banked forge-coals. Its beard, once braided in the Vestri warrior tradition, hangs ...

name: Green Hag
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- corrupted; the green hag is the Lady's domain gone stagnant and poisonous
environment: Tidebloom Marshes (the tidal marshlands of the Tidebloom Reach, particularly the deep-marsh zones where freshwater rivers meet saltwater tidal intrusions and the water is neither one thing nor the other)
organization: Solitary or covey (3 hags of any kind plus other creatures)
description: She lives where the water goes bad. Not where the water is dangerous -- that is different, honest danger, the kind you can see. She lives where the water is wrong: where the marsh-pools turn the wrong colour, where the fish float belly-up without visible cause, where the air tastes of sweetness that your body tells you means rot. She is there, somewhere in the murk, watching you with yellow eyes that she has not had to disguise in decades because no one comes here anymore.

name: Hydra
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- hydras are creatures of the deep marsh, saturated with stagnant water-energy
environment: Marshes (the Tidebloom Reach, the Deepwater Marches wetlands, and the marshy lowlands where the Great River spreads into seasonal flood-plains -- any warm, wet, low-lying terrain with sufficient prey)
advancement: 6-7 HD (five heads, Huge); 8-10 HD (six to seven heads, Huge); 11-14 HD (eight to ten heads, Gargantuan)
description: Five heads. Five necks. One body. The body is the size of a horse and scaled in dark, wet green; the necks are each ten feet long and independently mobile, weaving and striking like snakes operated by a single furious brain. When one head lunges, the others cover it. When one head is hurt, the others strike at the attacker. The creature does not think -- five heads do not produce five times the intelligence, they produce five times the aggression.

name: Each-Uisge
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- deep, cold, and patient; the Each-Uisge is the Lady's domain at its most predatory
environment: Great Forests (deep forest pools, still lakes, rain-fed tarns surrounded by old-growth trees -- specifically the deep interior of Pasua territory, where standing water collects in ancient glacial depressions beneath the canopy)
description: The horse stands at the lake's edge, knee-deep in black water, white as birch-bark and perfectly still. It is the most beautiful horse you have ever seen -- long-necked, deep-chested, with a mane that streams in a breeze you cannot feel. Its eyes are dark and liquid and fixed on you with an intensity that is not equine. The water around its legs does not ripple. It waits, patient as a lure, for you to come close enough to touch it. Every Pasua child knows what happens next. You touch it. Your ...

name: Assassin Vine
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) -- dual affinity reflecting root-depth and water dependence
environment: Great Forests (Deepwood, Twin Rivers old-growth, dense undergrowth near streams and springs); secondary presence in Tidebloom Reach (tidal forests) and Deepwater Marches (jungle undergrowth)
description: The trail narrows between two stands of old-growth trees, their canopy so thick that the forest floor is dim even at midday. Moss covers everything -- the tree trunks, the stones, the trail itself. There is no wind, but something moves at the edge of your vision: a vine, thick as a man's forearm, sliding across the path with a slow, purposeful motion that has nothing to do with the breeze. By the time you notice that the vines are moving on every side of you, they are already too close.

name: Assassin Vine
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) -- dual affinity reflecting root-depth and water dependence

name: Nuckelavee
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- inverted; the absolute negation of water's nurturing principle, corruption given physical form
environment: Tidebloom Reach (the salt-marshes, tidal flats, and coastal wetlands of the Tidebloom Reach -- the border-zone where brackish water meets the Apelian Sea, particularly the areas where the tides carry pollution and decay inland)
description: The Tidebloom stinks of rot. Not the clean rot of marshland decomposition -- something chemical, something that burns the sinuses and leaves a taste like copper and bile on the back of your tongue. The water has turned the colour of old blood. The fish are floating belly-up in the shallows, and the reed-beds are black and dying in concentric rings radiating from a point you cannot yet see. Then you see it -- rising from the fouled water like something being born from a wound. Horse and rider, f...

name: Changeling
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- fey affinity aligned with the forest (Wind through branches, Earth in roots)
environment: Great Forests (deep forest, near Kin settlements at the forest edge; also found in Twin Rivers forest-villages and the southern Kinsweave where forest extends into the plains)
organization: Solitary (always when placed in a Kin household; a small clutch of 2-5 may exist in the deep forest in natural form before dispersal)
description: The child in the cradle is wrong. The parents know it. The neighbours know it. The child itself -- if it is a child -- knows it, and watches the household with eyes that are too old and too amused for its face. It is the right size, the right shape, and it eats when fed, cries when wet, and sleeps when held. But it is not the child that was there yesterday. Something in the forest took the real one and left this in its place.

name: Ettercap
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- faint; the ettercap is a creature of the under-canopy, the dark spaces close to the ground
environment: Great Forests (dense mid-canopy and undergrowth, particularly in humid lowland forest where spider populations are highest -- southern Deepwood, Tidebloom Reach tidal forests, and the jungle-margins of the Deepwater Marches)
organization: Solitary, pair, or nest (3-6 plus 2-8 monstrous spiders)
description: The web is wrong. Normal spider-webs are symmetrical, elegant, efficient -- this one is a sprawling mess of thick, ropy strands strung between trees at head-height, and it is not empty. Something crouches in the upper canopy where the anchor-lines converge: humanoid enough to have arms, legs, and a recognisable posture, but wrong in the details -- too many joints in the fingers, spinnerets where a chin should be, and eyes that are flat, black, and arranged in a cluster rather than a pair.

name: Owlbear
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- fused; a dual resonance that reads as two signals imperfectly merged, as though something forced bear-earth and owl-air together and the result never fully integrated
environment: Great Forests (the mid-depth Great Forests -- deeper than the forest-edge where Pasua patrol, shallower than the deep interior where oni and dire tigers dominate -- the broad middle-zone of mixed deciduous and coniferous forest that is the Great Forests' most common terrain)
description: The thing about the owlbear is that you hear both halves before you see either. The hoot comes first -- deep, resonant, wrong, because it comes from a throat the size of a barrel rather than a bird's syrinx. Then the growl beneath the hoot, the ursine rumble that tells your hindbrain a very large bear is nearby. Then it comes through the undergrowth like a landslide with feathers, and the two sounds become one creature: eight feet of muscle, beak, claws, and bad intentions, moving with the spe...

name: Shambling Mound
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone / Lady of the Sea (Earth/Water) -- marsh-growth; the resonance of waterlogged earth producing uncontrolled growth, the border-zone where soil meets water and everything that falls in is consumed and converted to more growth
environment: Forest-Marsh Border (the transitional wetlands between the Great Forests and the Deepwater Marches -- specifically the zones where standing water meets forest undergrowth, creating the dense, decaying vegetation that shambling mounds require)
description: The marsh-edge is dense with vegetation -- cattails, sedge-grass, moss-draped willows -- and the shapes in the undergrowth are the shapes of rotting logs and mounded compost. You have been walking past them for ten minutes. One of them stands up. It is fifteen feet tall, roughly humanoid, composed entirely of rotting vegetation -- vines and roots and decaying plant-matter compressed into a body that smells of compost and moves with a slow, deliberate power that belies its shambling gait. It has ...

name: Storm Wisp
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- raw; the purest, most unrefined expression of the Youngest Brother's domain, wind as playful electrical discharge, the air-element at its most mischievous and least controlled
environment: Great Forests (primary -- the forest canopy and clearings during storm season) and Eternal Steppe (secondary -- open grasslands during thunderstorms); anywhere with frequent storms and sustained wind
advancement: 4-6 HD (Tiny); 7-9 HD (Small -- transition toward Storm Herald)
description: The air crackles. A blue-white sphere of light -- no larger than a fist, but bright enough to leave afterimages -- darts between the trees, pauses, darts again. It moves like a dragonfly: sudden direction-changes, instantaneous stops, bursts of speed that defy any creature with mass. Your arm-hair stands up. The taste of metal fills your mouth. The Pasua scout beside you grins. 'Wisp,' she says. 'Don't touch. Don't touch anything metal.'

name: Ponaturi
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- drowned dark; water in enclosed spaces, water that has not seen sunlight, the cold black water that fills tombs and basements and the spaces beneath the world
environment: Flooded Giant Ruins (the submerged and partially-submerged Giant-era structures along the Kovaka coast and in the lowland forests where rising water-tables have flooded ancient architecture -- specifically the great columned halls, step-pyramids, and underground cisterns that the Giants built before the Kin and that now sit beneath metres of dark water)
description: The flooded ruin is deeper than it looks. The Giant-era columns rise from water that should be waist-deep but isn't -- the floor drops away somewhere between the entrance and the first colonnade, and by the time you realise you're swimming rather than wading, the shapes are already rising from below. They are humanoid, pale as drowned flesh, with wide flat eyes that reflect your torchlight back at you with a greenish luminescence. They are smiling. Every single one of them is smiling -- not the...

name: Ponaturi
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- drowned dark; water in enclosed spaces, water that has not seen sunlight, the cold black water that fills tombs and basements and the spaces beneath the world
environment: Flooded Giant Ruins (the submerged and partially-submerged Giant-era structures along the Kovaka coast and in the lowland forests where rising water-tables have flooded ancient architecture -- specifically the great columned halls, step-pyramids, and underground cisterns that the Giants built before the Kin and that now sit beneath metres of dark water)
description: The flooded ruin is deeper than it looks. The Giant-era columns rise from water that should be waist-deep but isn't -- the floor drops away somewhere between the entrance and the first colonnade, and by the time you realise you're swimming rather than wading, the shapes are already rising from below. They are humanoid, pale as drowned flesh, with wide flat eyes that reflect your torchlight back at you with a greenish luminescence. They are smiling. Every single one of them is smiling -- not the...

name: Skeleton
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- skeletons are animated objects, not elemental beings; they have no resonance, no signature, and no connection to the elemental system -- they are bones made to move by magic, nothing more
environment: Any (wherever the dead are raised -- battlefields, crypts, Dómnathar tunnels, necromantic workshops, cursed sites where ambient death-energy animates remains without a spellcaster's intervention)
description: Medium Undead

flags: elemental_affinity:None -- skeletons are animated objects, not elemental beings; they have no resonance, no signature, and no connection to the elemental system -- they are bones made to move by magic, nothing more

description: Medium Undead

name: Zombie
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- a zombie's resonance is absence, like a wight's, but less -- a wight hungers, a zombie does not even have that; it is the absolute minimum of undeath, meat animated by the thinnest thread of necromantic energy, registering on Kin-sense as 'dead thing moving' with no further complexity
environment: Any (zombies appear wherever necromantic contamination meets preserved corpses -- battlefields, mass graves, Dómnathar-influenced zones, and locations where the boundary between life and death has been thinned by catastrophe or dark magic)
description: It walks. That is the horror of it -- not the rot, not the smell, not the exposed bone or the grey-green skin or the blank, staring eyes. The horror is that it walks. It was a person. You can see the person it was -- the build, the clothing, the face. And now it walks toward you with its arms out and its mouth open and no thought behind the eyes at all. Nothing. Not hunger, not malice, not pain. Nothing. It is meat that remembers how to move and has forgotten everything else.

name: Zombie
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- a zombie's resonance is absence, like a wight's, but less -- a wight hungers, a zombie does not even have that; it is the absolute minimum of undeath, meat animated by the thinnest thread of necromantic energy, registering on Kin-sense as 'dead thing moving' with no further complexity
environment: Any (zombies appear wherever necromantic contamination meets preserved corpses -- battlefields, mass graves, Dómnathar-influenced zones, and locations where the boundary between life and death has been thinned by catastrophe or dark magic)
