You can see the person it was -- the build, the clothing, the face. And now it walks toward you with its arms out and its mouth open and no thought behind the eyes at all. Nothing. Not hunger, not malice, not pain. Nothing. It is meat that remembers how to move and has forgotten everything else.

name: Nang Tani
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) -- nurturing; the dark soil of cultivated groves, the patient water that feeds roots, growth expressed as care
environment: Halfling Orchards (the fruit-groves and crop-orchards of the Deepwater Marches and Tidebloom Reach halfling communities -- particularly old-growth banana, mango, and breadfruit groves that have been tended for generations)
description: The banana grove is older than the halfling settlement that tends it. The trees are tall and thick-trunked, their broad leaves casting the kind of shade that turns midday into twilight, and the air beneath them is sweet with the smell of ripening fruit and damp earth. You notice one tree at the grove's heart that is different -- not larger, but present in a way the others are not. A green silk ribbon is tied around its trunk, and at its base, a scattering of fresh flowers, a small clay cup of ...

name: Spider Eater
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- canopy-predator; the resonance of the Great Forest's upper airspace, wind filtered through leaves and channelled between tree-trunks
environment: Great Forests (the mid-canopy and upper-canopy zones of the Great Forests -- specifically the zones where giant spider populations are dense enough to support spider eater breeding cycles)
description: The thing drops from the canopy -- thirty feet of membranous wing spanning between a body like a wasp's, a head like a horse's, and a stinger the length of your forearm. It is not falling. It is diving, and the target is not you but the giant spider in the web between the oaks. The stinger drives into the spider's abdomen with surgical precision, deposits something, and the creature is airborne again before the spider can react. In three days, the spider's body will burst open. The larva insid...

name: Grig
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- the grig is the playful overlap of earth and air; the cricket in the grass, the music on the breeze
environment: Great Forests (forest clearings, meadow-margins, stream-banks, and the sun-dappled edges of the canopy -- wherever the forest is light and warm enough for music)
description: You hear the fiddle first. Something is playing a fiddle in the undergrowth -- playing it well, playing it fast, playing a reel that makes your feet want to move before your brain can object. You part the ferns and see the musician: a creature the size of your hand, cricket-legged, fairy-winged, grinning like a lunatic, sawing away at a fiddle the size of a matchstick with a bow the size of a needle. It sees you seeing it and plays faster.

name: Kodama
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- the deep, slow earth-resonance of a living tree's connection to the soil
environment: Great Forests (the interior of the oldest forests -- wherever a tree has stood long enough and grown deep enough to develop a kodama; never in young growth, plantations, or recently-cleared areas)
organization: Solitary (one per tree, always) or grove (3-20, one per tree in a kodama-grove)
advancement: 1-3 HD (Tiny -- larger kodama inhabit larger, older trees)
description: The sound is not a sound. It is a vibration -- a resonance that comes from the tree itself, from the ring of wood inside the bark, from the sap moving through channels that have been open for five hundred years. If you press your ear to the trunk and listen with more than your ears, you hear it: a pulse, slow and patient, like a heartbeat that measures its time in seasons rather than seconds. The kodama is not in the tree. The kodama is the tree's awareness of itself.

name: Nixie
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- gentle; the clear water of shallow lakes, sunlight on the surface, the warmth of still pools
environment: Rivers and Lakes (freshwater lakes, slow rivers, mill-ponds, and spring-fed pools throughout the Great River system and the Deepwater Marches -- anywhere that still, clear freshwater collects in sufficient depth)
description: The lake is clear and shallow at the edges, the kind of place where children wade and fishermen cast from the bank. You see the light first -- a flicker of green-gold beneath the surface, like sunlight catching a fish-scale, except the light moves with purpose. A head breaks the surface: small, angular, green-skinned, with large dark eyes and hair the colour of waterweed. She treads water effortlessly and regards you with the frank curiosity of something that owns this lake and considers you a...

name: Pixie
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- mischievous; the lightest, most playful expression of air-resonance, wind as laughter, breeze as practical joke
environment: Great Forests (the mid-canopy and understory of Oreka's oldest forest zones -- particularly the groves where sunlight filters through the canopy in dappled patterns, creating the shifting light-and-shadow that pixies prefer; common in Tanuki groves, Pasua patrol-ranges, and the forest-edges bordering the Eternal Steppe)
description: You hear the laughter before you see anything -- high, silvery, coming from somewhere above your left ear and slightly to the right and also behind you and possibly from inside the hollow log you just stepped over. Then a mote of light drifts past your face, and you feel a tug at your belt-pouch, and when you look down, your coins are gone. When you look up, a creature the size of your hand is sitting on a branch at eye-level, counting your money with an expression of delighted concentration. ...

name: Unicorn
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- forest-heart; the resonance of the deepest, purest forest-earth -- soil that has never been ploughed, turned, or contaminated, the Lord of Stone's domain expressed as the foundation of untouched wilderness
environment: Great Forests (the deepest, most pristine zones of the Great Forests -- specifically the old-growth interiors where Kin settlements have never reached, where the forest-floor has been undisturbed for millennia, and where the elemental purity of the earth is absolute)
description: The forest clearing is lit from within -- not by sunlight (the canopy is too thick) but by something else, a quality of light that has no visible source and no shadow. The creature standing in the clearing is a horse -- or it looks like a horse, the way a cathedral looks like a building. It is white, impossibly white, the white of fresh snow before anyone has walked on it, and its mane falls like water. The horn is the colour of old ivory, spiralling from the centre of its forehead with the mat...

name: Water Elemental, Medium
environment: As Small Water Elemental
description: The river rises. Not floods -- the water level does not change -- but a column of river-water pulls itself upright, shaping limbs and a torso and something that might be a head from the current itself. It stands on the river's surface like a person stands on a floor, and within its translucent body you can see river-debris turning slowly: pebbles, weeds, a small fish swimming in confused circles. When it moves, the sound is rain on stone.

name: Dragon Turtle
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) / Lady of Fire (Fire) -- dual affinity; the dragon turtle is the Lady of the Sea's domain armoured in the Lady of Fire's heat
environment: Kailian Sea (deep channels, submerged seamounts, and the warmer currents of the eastern Kailian basin); secondary presence in the deep Apelian Sea (rare) and at the Great River's mouth where freshwater meets salt
description: The sea ahead goes flat. Not calm -- flat, as though the water itself has been pressed down by something enormous beneath the surface. Then the shell rises: a ridge of stone and coral the size of a small island, streaming water, trailing kelp gardens that have been growing on its back for decades. The head emerges last -- a dragon's head on a turtle's neck, armoured in scales the colour of deep ocean, with eyes like amber lanterns. The mouth opens. Steam pours out. The fishing boats turn and run.

name: Bunyip
environment: Apelian Sea (coastal shallows, river-mouths, tidal pools, and sheltered coves along the Deepwater Marches coastline); secondary presence at the Sea Falls and in the brackish estuaries where the Great River meets the Apelian basin
description: The water at the edge of the sea-lake goes still -- not the stillness of calm water, but the stillness of water holding its breath. Then a shape surfaces: seal-dark, heavy-shouldered, with a head that seems to shift between dog and crocodile depending on the angle. Its mouth opens and it produces a sound -- not a roar, not a bark, but something between the two, a bellowing cry that reverberates off the cliff face and turns the blood in your arms to ice water. The fisherfolk are already running....

name: Kappa
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- kappa are water-spirits given physical, mischievous form
environment: Apelian Shoals (the shallow coastal waters, tidal pools, and estuaries of the Apelian Sea -- particularly the sheltered coves and reef-shallows along the Pekakarlik and Na'wasua coast)
description: The thing in the shallows looks like a turtle that decided to be a child. It is three feet tall, green-skinned, shell-backed, with a beak where its mouth should be and a shallow depression in the top of its skull filled with water. It is watching you from behind a rock with an expression that combines curiosity, malice, and hunger in roughly equal measure. The Pekakarlik fisherman who warned you about this stretch of coast said two things: 'they're strong enough to drown a horse' and 'if one ...

name: Hakuturi
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) -- the living forest; root-water and dark soil, growth expressed as patience
environment: Great Forests (the old-growth stands of the Kovaka territories -- the southern and island-fringe forests where ancient trees have stood for millennia undisturbed; never found in young growth, managed forest, or areas cleared within living memory)
description: The tree is old. Older than the Kovaka settlement at its base, older than the trail that leads to it, older than anything human memory can account for. You came to cut a branch -- a single branch, for a canoe-rib -- and now you cannot lift the axe. Not because something is stopping you. Because the tree is looking at you. The bark has rearranged itself into a face that was always there and always watching, and the face is not angry. It is patient. It is waiting for you to understand why you sho...

name: Tipua
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone / Lady of the Sea (Earth/Water) -- landmark spirit; the resonance of specific geographical features that have persisted long enough to develop awareness -- a boulder, a spring, a tree, a cliff-face -- the elemental system acknowledging places that matter
environment: Kovaka Landmarks (the named geographical features of Kovaka coastal-forest territory -- ancient trees, distinctive rock-formations, hot springs, river-forks, and cliff-edges that have served as navigational reference-points and cultural markers for centuries)
organization: Solitary (always bound to a specific landmark)
advancement: 8-10 HD (Medium); 11-14 HD (Large -- the spirits of the most significant landmarks)
description: The landmark has always been here -- the split boulder at the river-fork, the ancient pohutukawa tree at the cliff-edge, the hot spring where the valley narrows. The Kovaka have names for each one, and the names are older than the settlements. Today the landmark is different. The split boulder has shifted -- not moved, but the gap between the halves is wider, and in the gap stands a woman who was not there a moment ago. She is beautiful, dark-haired, barefoot, and her eyes are the colour of the...

name: Nymph
flags: elemental_affinity:All four Elemental Lords -- pristine; the unbroken elemental harmony of the world as it was meant to be, expressed through living beauty
environment: Pristine Groves (isolated pockets of elemental perfection scattered across Oreka -- old-growth hollows, mountain springs, forest-clearings, and lakeside meadows where the elemental balance has never been disrupted, usually far from Kin settlement and often concealed by natural terrain)
description: The grove is a place that should not exist. You are in the foothills of the Giant's Teeth, where the forest is thin and the soil is rocky and nothing grows taller than scrub-oak -- but here, in this hollow between two ridges, there is a grove of ancient trees surrounding a pool of water so clear it looks like a window into the earth. The air is different -- warmer, sweeter, thick with the scent of flowers that have no business blooming at this altitude. Everything here is more alive than it sho...

name: Kitsune
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- mutable; the kitsune's air-resonance shifts and changes like the wind, never quite the same twice, reflecting the fey's shapeshifting nature
environment: Great Forests (the deciduous and mixed forests of Hasura territory -- forest-edges, shrine-clearings, ancient game-trails, and the borderlands where managed forest meets wild wood)
organization: Solitary or skulk (2-4, always related -- a parent and offspring, or siblings)
advancement: 7-12 HD (Medium -- each additional HD represents a new tail, to a maximum of nine)
description: The fox was there a moment ago. You saw it -- a red fox, large, sitting at the tree-line with its tail curled around its feet, watching you with an intelligence that foxes should not have. You blinked. Now it is a woman -- or was a woman, or is becoming a woman, or is something in between that your brain is trying to resolve into one shape or the other and failing. She smiles at you with the fox's eyes in a woman's face, and you understand that the shape was never the point.

name: Leshy
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- ancient; root-deep and canopy-high, the living forest from soil to sky
environment: Great Forests (the deepest interior of the Hasura canopy and Pasua ground-level forests -- ancient-growth stands where the trees are old enough to have names; rare sightings in Kovaka coastal forests and the forest-steppe border)
organization: Solitary or moot (2-4, extremely rare -- leshiye gather only to resolve territorial disputes)
description: The forest is watching you. Not in the way that forests always watch -- the incurious attention of birds and insects and the slow awareness of old trees. This is focused. This is personal. You have been walking for three hours and you are back where you started, though you have not turned once. The blaze-marks you cut into the bark an hour ago are gone. The path you were following does not exist. Somewhere ahead, in the green shadow between the oaks, something enormous shifts its weight and th...

name: Androsphinx
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- dual affinity
environment: The Infinite Desert (Giant ruins, carved canyon approaches, exposed plateau-tops near the Sunken Thrones and Tharok's Pillar); secondary presence in the Glass Wastes (ridgelines overlooking the sand-flats)
description: It sits on a slab of carved stone that was once a throne-room lintel, its lion's body perfectly still, its human face turned toward the horizon as though watching for something that will never come. Sand drifts against its flanks. Its mane, thick as a horse's tail, lifts in the desert wind. When it speaks -- and it will speak, if it judges you worthy of words -- its voice resonates in the chest like a struck bell, and the sand around it trembles.

name: Amphisbaena
environment: The Infinite Desert (sandy gullies, rocky scree-fields, ruin-margins, canyon floors); secondary presence in the Glass Wastes and the arid foothills between the Giant's Teeth and the Scorchspires
description: Something moves in the sand ahead of you -- a ripple, like water over a submerged log. Then two heads emerge, one at each end of a body the colour of dried bone, and both heads open their mouths simultaneously, each displaying a set of thin, curved fangs wet with venom. The creature does not coil; it holds itself in a taut S-curve between the two heads, ready to strike in either direction without turning around.

name: Amphisbaena
environment: The Infinite Desert (sandy gullies, rocky scree-fields, ruin-margins, canyon floors); secondary presence in the Glass Wastes and the arid foothills between the Giant's Teeth and the Scorchspires
description: Something moves in the sand ahead of you -- a ripple, like water over a submerged log. Then two heads emerge, one at each end of a body the colour of dried bone, and both heads open their mouths simultaneously, each displaying a set of thin, curved fangs wet with venom. The creature does not coil; it holds itself in a taut S-curve between the two heads, ready to strike in either direction without turning around.

name: Basilisk
environment: The Infinite Desert (rocky gullies, canyon approaches, ruins with exposed stone foundations); secondary presence in the Glass Wastes (ridgeline scree-fields) and Scorchspires foothills (dry ravines)
description: It moves like a lizard that has never needed to learn haste -- a low, heavy-bodied crawler, its scales the grey-brown of sunbaked stone, its eight legs spreading its weight across the sand like a creature designed by someone who did not want it to sink. Its eyes are the problem. They are open, wet, and the colour of old amber, and everything they look at dies -- or wishes it had.

name: Basilisk
environment: The Infinite Desert (rocky gullies, canyon approaches, ruins with exposed stone foundations); secondary presence in the Glass Wastes (ridgeline scree-fields) and Scorchspires foothills (dry ravines)
description: It moves like a lizard that has never needed to learn haste -- a low, heavy-bodied crawler, its scales the grey-brown of sunbaked stone, its eight legs spreading its weight across the sand like a creature designed by someone who did not want it to sink. Its eyes are the problem. They are open, wet, and the colour of old amber, and everything they look at dies -- or wishes it had.

name: Behir
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lady of Fire (Fire) -- dual affinity; lightning is where wind meets heat
environment: The Infinite Desert (deep canyons, slot canyons, cliff-face lairs between the Sunken Thrones and the Glass Wastes); secondary presence in Giant's Teeth foothills (steep ravines)
description: The canyon wall moves. What you took for a ridge of dark stone uncoils -- twelve legs unfold from its sides, a serpentine body longer than a merchant barge stretches across the gap between the cliff faces, and a head like a crocodile's opens to reveal a throat lit from within by crackling blue-white light. Lightning arcs between its teeth. The air smells of ozone and hot sand.

name: Bulette
environment: The Infinite Desert (deep sand-seas, dune-fields between the Sunken Thrones and the Glass Wastes); secondary presence in the Eternal Steppe (deep-soil grasslands, particularly along the Kinsweave border where soil is loosest)
description: The ground heaves. A ridge of displaced earth rises like a wave in the sand -- six feet high, forty feet long, moving faster than a horse at gallop. It crests. The sand explodes upward, and something armored and enormous breaches the surface like a whale breaching water: a body the size of a wagon, plated in blue-grey chitin harder than castle stone, with a head that is mostly jaw and a jaw that is mostly teeth. It hangs in the air for one terrible second, blotting out the sun. Then it comes d...

name: Dust Mephit
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- minor elemental; dust is where wind meets dry earth
environment: The Infinite Desert (sandstorms, dust-devils, ruin interiors, dry canyon systems); secondary presence wherever arid conditions meet high winds (Scorchspire valleys, Glass Wastes, Ashen Crown leeward slopes)
description: It materialises from the air itself -- or rather, from the dust in the air, drawing particles together into a shape roughly the size and temperament of an angry cat with wings. Its body is gritty, compacted sand and dust that shifts and hisses when it moves. Its eyes are two sparks of dull amber. Its voice, when it speaks (and it will speak -- dust mephits never shut up), sounds like sand pouring through a crack.

name: Dust Mephit
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- minor elemental; dust is where wind meets dry earth
environment: The Infinite Desert (sandstorms, dust-devils, ruin interiors, dry canyon systems); secondary presence wherever arid conditions meet high winds (Scorchspire valleys, Glass Wastes, Ashen Crown leeward slopes)
description: It materialises from the air itself -- or rather, from the dust in the air, drawing particles together into a shape roughly the size and temperament of an angry cat with wings. Its body is gritty, compacted sand and dust that shifts and hisses when it moves. Its eyes are two sparks of dull amber. Its voice, when it speaks (and it will speak -- dust mephits never shut up), sounds like sand pouring through a crack.

name: Gynosphinx
flags: elemental_affinity:All Four Elemental Lords (fragmented) -- the gynosphinx carries the same four-element signature as the vanished Giants, but refracted, partial, and distorted through a bestial lens
environment: Glass Wastes (the heat-fused sand expanses of the Infinite Desert's western margins, where the sand has been melted into glass by ancient elemental forces -- a landscape of razor-edged glass ridges and reflective plains that the sphinxes have claimed as their own)
description: She is waiting for you. She has been waiting for you since before you entered the desert, since before you decided to take this route, since before you were born. She reclines on the sand-polished stone with the patience of a monument and the attention of a predator, and her face -- a woman's face, beautiful, ageless, and utterly without mercy -- watches you approach with an expression that might be curiosity or might be appetite. They are the same thing, to her.

name: Hieracosphinx
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- faint; the hieracosphinx carries a diluted version of the four-element sphinx resonance, stripped to a single attenuated air-note
environment: Infinite Desert (the Glass Wastes and surrounding dune-seas -- the same general region as gynosphinxes, but occupying the less desirable territories that the greater sphinxes do not claim)
description: It has a hawk's head and a lion's body and none of the intelligence of its gynosphinx cousins. It does not pose riddles. It does not bargain. It sits on a sand-scoured ridge in the Glass Wastes and watches the desert below with eyes that calculate nothing except distance, angle, and speed.

name: Lamia
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of Fire (Fire) -- corrupted; desert heat twisted inward, consuming rather than warming
environment: Infinite Desert (the deep dune-seas and abandoned oases between Pekakarlik waystations -- particularly the Glass Wastes, where the ruins offer hiding-places and the oases draw prey; rare sightings along the Scorchspire foothills' desert edge)
description: The woman standing at the oasis edge is beautiful. You notice that first -- the dark hair, the amber eyes, the confident stillness with which she watches your approach. You notice second that she is alone in the deep desert, a day's ride from the nearest Pekakarlik waystation, with no supplies, no mount, and no footprints leading to where she stands. You notice third -- and this is the last useful thing you will notice, because by now the Wisdom is already draining from behind your eyes -- that ...

name: Sand Wraith
flags: elemental_affinity:All four Elemental Lords (fragmented); strongest resonance with the Lord of Stone -- a Sand Wraith's elemental signature is the shattered remnant of a Giant's full four-element harmony, broken and looping like a record stuck in a groove
special_qualities: elemental resonance (all four - fragmented Giant echo)
environment: The Infinite Desert (Giant ruins, dune-seas; thickest concentration at the Sunken Thrones, Tharok's Pillar, and the deep dune-seas; rare sightings at the Apelian Sea's submerged Giant piers in the Deepwater Marches)
description: The ruin ahead is half-buried -- the upper columns of what was once a Giant plaza project from the dune like the ribs of a vast skeleton, and the sand between them moves in patterns that have nothing to do with the wind. A shape gathers in the air above the columns: tall, vaguely humanoid, composed entirely of swirling sand and grit. Dim amber light burns where its eyes should be. The sound it produces is less a voice than a sustained, low howl of desert wind forced through a space too narrow ...

name: Sand Wraith
flags: elemental_affinity:All four Elemental Lords (fragmented); strongest resonance with the Lord of Stone -- a Sand Wraith's elemental signature is the shattered remnant of a Giant's full four-element harmony, broken and looping like a record stuck in a groove
special_qualities: elemental resonance (all four - fragmented Giant echo)
environment: The Infinite Desert (Giant ruins, dune-seas; thickest concentration at the Sunken Thrones, Tharok's Pillar, and the deep dune-seas; rare sightings at the Apelian Sea's submerged Giant piers in the Deepwater Marches)
description: The ruin ahead is half-buried -- the upper columns of what was once a Giant plaza project from the dune like the ribs of a vast skeleton, and the sand between them moves in patterns that have nothing to do with the wind. A shape gathers in the air above the columns: tall, vaguely humanoid, composed entirely of swirling sand and grit. Dim amber light burns where its eyes should be. The sound it produces is less a voice than a sustained, low howl of desert wind forced through a space too narrow ...

name: Olgoi-Khorkhoi
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- deep, sick; a churning, acidic earth-resonance that reads as 'the ground digesting itself'
environment: Infinite Desert (the deep hardpan flats between dune-fields -- sun-baked clay-pan stretches where nothing grows, where the earth is cracked and dry, and where the olgoi-khorkhoi burrows beneath the surface waiting for vibrations)
description: The Mytroan scout dismounts fifty paces from the depression in the hardpan. He kneels and presses his palm flat against the baked earth -- a gesture you recognise from steppe-rider tradition, the earth-reading that Mytroan scouts use to detect burrowing threats. His face goes white. He stands, backs toward his horse without turning around, mounts, and says one word: khorkhoi. The other riders wheel their horses immediately. No one argues. No one asks questions. Everyone rides.

name: Remorhaz
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- subsurface inferno; the resonance of superheated rock, of magma channels too deep for even the Rarozhki to mine, of the earth's internal fire expressed through a living body
environment: Infinite Desert Hardpan (the baked, compressed substrata beneath the Infinite Desert's dune-fields -- the hardpan layer that lies between the surface sand and the deep rock, where temperatures are extreme and the geological pressure creates conditions that no surface creature can tolerate)
description: The hardpan cracks. Not like drought-cracking -- like something pressing up from beneath, a line of fractures racing across the baked earth faster than a horse can run. The Pekakarlik scout is already moving, already running, already screaming the word that every desert-traveller learns before any other: 'Below!' The ground splits open along the fracture-line and something erupts -- something enormous, segmented, centipede-shaped, its body radiating heat so intense that the air above it shimmer...

name: Purple Worm
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- tectonic; the resonance of geological movement, of continental plates grinding against each other, of the earth itself shifting -- not the quiet patience of mountain-stone but the active, destructive energy of the earth in motion
environment: Deep Desert (the Infinite Desert's deepest hardpan zones, the substrata beneath the dune-fields, and the buried rock-layers that underlie the Glass Wastes -- the worm moves through the desert's bones, not its surface)
description: The desert does not move. That is the first thing every Pekakarlik caravan-guide learns and the last thing every dead caravan-guide forgot. The desert does not move -- and if it does, if the dunes shift when there is no wind, if the hardpan cracks along a line that is too straight and too long to be natural, if the ground beneath your feet begins to vibrate with a frequency that your bones recognise before your brain does, then you have exactly the amount of time it takes to scream before the ...

name: Lamassu
flags: elemental_affinity:All four Elemental Lords -- primordial; the unbroken harmony of the Giant age, carried forward in living stone and wind
environment: Giant Ruins (the great ruin-complexes of the Infinite Desert -- the Sunken Thrones, Tharok's Pillar, and the partially-collapsed temple-cities of the Glass Wastes' edge; rare sightings at Giant-era bridges and gate-complexes in the Giant's Teeth foothills)
description: The ruin is silent -- that particular silence of places where someone carved stone with purpose ten thousand years ago and the stone remembers. You round the fallen pillar and something is watching you from the colonnade's shadow. A body like a bull cast in granite, shoulders broad enough to block the passage, wings folded tight against its flanks -- eagle's wings, massive, the feathers carved from living bronze. And the face. The face is not a bull's face. It is older than anything you have me...

name: Medusa
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- corrupted; petrification as a perverted expression of earth-affinity, turning living flesh to stone against its will
environment: Ruins and Marshes (abandoned structures throughout Oreka -- Giant ruins in the Infinite Desert, ruined border-settlements in the Kinsweave, crumbling watchtowers in the Tidebloom Reach; always in built environments where stone is plentiful and prey can be lured into enclosed spaces)
description: The gallery is a sculpture-garden -- or you thought it was. The figures are too detailed, too varied, too individual to be carved. The dwarf in the corner has a crack in his breastplate that no sculptor would include. The elf at the entrance has an expression of dawning recognition on her face that no art could capture. The woman at the centre of the gallery is veiled. She asks if you would like to see her collection. She asks very politely.

name: Salt Mephit
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- crystalline desiccation; the resonance of salt-deposits, of water removed from earth until only the mineral skeleton remains, earth at its driest and most hostile to life
environment: Desert Salt Flats (the vast salt-pan expanses of the Infinite Desert -- specifically the dried lake-beds and mineral-flat zones where ancient water-sources evaporated millennia ago, leaving crystalline wastelands)
description: The salt flat shimmers in the heat. The crust is thin here -- thin enough that your boot punches through with each step, revealing the damp brine beneath. Something crunches to your left, and a shape rises from the salt-crust like a man rising from shallow water: small, angular, encrusted with crystalline deposits, with a face that is all sharp edges and a grin that contains too many teeth. It smells like a low-tide beach on a hot day. It is looking at your waterskin with the focused intensity...

name: Mushussu
flags: elemental_affinity:All four Elemental Lords -- fading; the all-element resonance of the Giant age, attenuated by millennia of isolation, guttering like a candle in its last hours
environment: Desert Ruins (the gate-complexes, processional avenues, and entrance-ways of the most intact Giant-era ruin-cities in the Infinite Desert -- the Sunken Thrones, Tharok's Pillar, and the partially-buried temple-complexes of the Glass Wastes)
organization: Solitary, pair, or gate-vigil (3-5 at a major gate-complex)
description: The ruin-gate is guarded. The guardian is not Giant -- it is something the Giants made, or something that lived with the Giants and stayed after they were gone. It has the body of a scaled serpent, the forelegs of a lion, the hind-legs of an eagle, and a neck that arches upward like a crane's, capped with a horned head whose eyes glow with a faint, steady amber light. It has been sitting in front of this gate for ten thousand years. The bones at its feet suggest that it takes its duties seriou...

name: Mummy
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- undead; but the body was once Giant, and traces of the all-four-element resonance linger in the preservation-runes like an echo in a sealed room
environment: Giant Tombs (the sealed burial-complexes and mausoleum-vaults of Giant-era cities throughout the Infinite Desert -- the Sunken Thrones, the Dust-Vaults beneath Tharok's Pillar, and the deep tomb-complexes of the Giant's Teeth foothills)
description: The sarcophagus is not dwarven. It is Giant -- carved from a single block of basalt in proportions that no Kin hands shaped, covered in rune-script that predates written Dwarven by millennia. The lid has been moved. From inside, the smell of preservation-resin and dust and something else -- something dry and electric that makes the hair on your arms stand up. The thing that climbs out does not rush. It has been waiting for longer than your civilisation has existed, and it has the patience of st...

name: Skeleton
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- skeletons are animated objects, not elemental beings; they have no resonance, no signature, and no connection to the elemental system -- they are bones made to move by magic, nothing more
environment: Any (wherever the dead are raised -- battlefields, crypts, Dómnathar tunnels, necromantic workshops, cursed sites where ambient death-energy animates remains without a spellcaster's intervention)
description: Medium Undead

name: Zombie
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- a zombie's resonance is absence, like a wight's, but less -- a wight hungers, a zombie does not even have that; it is the absolute minimum of undeath, meat animated by the thinnest thread of necromantic energy, registering on Kin-sense as 'dead thing moving' with no further complexity
environment: Any (zombies appear wherever necromantic contamination meets preserved corpses -- battlefields, mass graves, Dómnathar-influenced zones, and locations where the boundary between life and death has been thinned by catastrophe or dark magic)
description: It walks. That is the horror of it -- not the rot, not the smell, not the exposed bone or the grey-green skin or the blank, staring eyes. The horror is that it walks. It was a person. You can see the person it was -- the build, the clothing, the face. And now it walks toward you with its arms out and its mouth open and no thought behind the eyes at all. Nothing. Not hunger, not malice, not pain. Nothing. It is meat that remembers how to move and has forgotten everything else.

name: Vampire Spawn
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- a vampire spawn has no elemental resonance; it is dead matter animated by hunger, a body that feeds to sustain a semblance of existence without possessing any of the elemental connections that defined it in life
environment: Giant Tomb Complexes (the sealed burial-chambers, sarcophagus-halls, and crypt-networks of the Giant-era tomb complexes scattered through the Giant's Teeth and Infinite Desert -- places where the original Giants entombed their dead with rituals that preserved the bodies in conditions that, when combined with subsequent necromantic contamination, produced vampiric undead)
description: The tomb-air is dry and still and old, and the thing that steps out of the sarcophagus-shadow is not what you expected. You expected bone. You expected rot. What you get is a person -- pale, sharp-featured, dressed in the remnants of burial-finery that has held together through centuries of absolute darkness. Its eyes are open and aware and utterly, coldly hungry, and when it smiles, the canines are longer than they should be, and the smile does not contain a single molecule of warmth. It is n...

name: Peri
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire -- sacred; not the destructive fire of the Scorchspires but the illuminating, purifying fire of dawn, the fire that reveals truth and burns only corruption
environment: Glass Wastes / Tharok's Pillar (the heat-blasted ruins of the Infinite Desert's most ancient Giant cities -- particularly the sacred sites where the Lady of Fire's influence is strongest, where the sand has been fused to glass by heat so old it predates memory)
description: The Glass Wastes shimmer at midday, heat-haze turning the ruined columns of Tharok's Pillar into dancing shapes that could be anything -- pilgrims, mirage-beasts, or the melting edges of the world itself. And then one of the shimmering shapes steps out of the haze, and it is neither mirage nor heat-dream. It is a woman made of fire that does not burn -- golden-skinned, amber-eyed, with wings of flame that fold and unfold like a heartbeat. The air around her is warm, not hot. The light she produ...

name: Humbaba
flags: elemental_affinity:All Four Elemental Lords -- integrated; the humbaba was built with the Firstborn's four-element mastery, its elemental core containing bound fragments of all four domains
environment: Giant Ruins (the interior of the best-preserved Giant-era structures -- specifically guarding doorways, halls, treasure-vaults, and sacred chambers; found in ruins across the Infinite Desert, Giant's Teeth, and Kinsweave deep-sites)
organization: Solitary or pair (one per doorway, sometimes two flanking a major entrance)
description: The door opens and the guardian is there. It has been there for seven thousand years. It is fifteen feet tall, built of stone and bronze and elemental fire bound into a framework that the Giants designed to last forever, and it has. Its face is a lion's face carved in basalt. Its body is a humanoid chassis of interlocking stone plates over a skeleton of Giant-forged bronze. Its eyes -- two points of white-hot elemental fire -- track you from the doorway to the threshold, and the voice that emer...

name: Cherufe
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- dual affinity, Fire dominant; the cherufe is molten earth, the place where the Lady of Fire's domain and the Lord of Stone's domain merge
environment: Scorchspires (active calderas, magma chambers, volcanic vent systems, and the deep volcanic core of the Scorchspire mountain range)
description: The caldera glows. Not from below -- from across, from the thing that sits in the centre of the lava lake like a man sitting in a bath. It is made of stone. It is on fire. The stone is not burning -- the fire is coming from inside, pouring through cracks in a body of black basalt that has the approximate shape of a giant, if a giant were designed by someone who thought 'humanoid' was a loose suggestion rather than a requirement. Its eyes are magma. When it moves, the caldera trembles.

name: Ember Drake
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) -- pure, undiluted fire-affinity; the ember drake is the Lady's domain in its most accessible form
environment: Scorchspires (volcanic vents, lava tubes, caldera edges, and Rarozhki forge-halls); secondary presence in the Emberfang Mountains, Ashen Crown, and volcanic vents in the Infinite Desert
advancement: 5-8 HD (Small); see Elder Drake and Emberfang Wyrm for mature forms
description: It sits on the forge-rim like a cat sits on a warm hearth -- compact, content, and radiating heat that makes the air shimmer. It is the size of a large dog, scaled in overlapping plates of orange-red that shift to white-hot at the edges, and its eyes glow like coals. A thin trail of smoke curls from each nostril. The Rarozhki smith working at the forge does not look up. The drake has been here longer than he has. It was here when his grandfather lit this forge for the first time, and it will b...

name: Ember Drake
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) -- pure, undiluted fire-affinity; the ember drake is the Lady's domain in its most accessible form
environment: Scorchspires (volcanic vents, lava tubes, caldera edges, and Rarozhki forge-halls); secondary presence in the Emberfang Mountains, Ashen Crown, and volcanic vents in the Infinite Desert
advancement: 5-8 HD (Small); see Elder Drake and Emberfang Wyrm for mature forms
description: It sits on the forge-rim like a cat sits on a warm hearth -- compact, content, and radiating heat that makes the air shimmer. It is the size of a large dog, scaled in overlapping plates of orange-red that shift to white-hot at the edges, and its eyes glow like coals. A thin trail of smoke curls from each nostril. The Rarozhki smith working at the forge does not look up. The drake has been here longer than he has. It was here when his grandfather lit this forge for the first time, and it will b...

name: Magma Mephit
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- volcanic; liquid stone made malicious, the Scorchspires' smallest and most irritating ambassadors
environment: Scorchspire Caldera (the active volcanic vents, lava tubes, and caldera rims of the Scorchspires -- particularly the Rarozhki-adjacent vents where elemental activity is highest)
description: The caldera vent cracks open and something small climbs out, dripping with molten rock that cools on its body in streaks of black and red. It is two feet tall, vaguely humanoid, and made of cooling magma -- each movement cracking its skin to reveal the orange glow beneath. It grins at you with a mouth full of obsidian teeth and speaks in a voice like a campfire settling. Behind it, three more are climbing out.

name: Magmin
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) -- pure; the elemental flame given form, innocent and incendiary
environment: Scorchspires (active lava-tubes, volcanic fissures, and the deep caldera floors where molten rock is accessible -- always near active magma sources)
description: The lava-tube glows from within -- not the dull red of cooling rock, but the bright, shifting orange of something moving. A shape detaches itself from the wall: small, humanoid, and burning. Not on fire -- made of fire, given form by a thin shell of cooling rock that cracks and reforms with every step. Where it walks, the stone beneath its feet softens and glows. It looks at you with eyes that are holes into something incandescent, and you understand instantly that this creature does not know i...

name: Thoqqua
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire / Lord of Stone (Fire/Earth) -- magma-worm; the resonance of rock melted by internal fire, the intersection of the Lady and the Lord's domains expressed as a living geological process
environment: Scorchspires (the active volcanic zones, lava-tube networks, and geothermal fissure-fields of the Scorchspire range -- anywhere the boundary between fire and earth is thinnest)
description: The volcanic fissure hisses -- a sound like water on hot iron -- and something worm-shaped pushes through the crack in the rock. It is three feet long, segmented, and glowing a dull orange-red, and the stone it touches melts. Not chars, not blackens -- melts, flowing away from the creature's body like candle-wax from a flame. It is a living drill-bit, and the volcanic vent it just emerged from was not here yesterday. The thoqqua made it.

name: Earth Elemental, Small
description: The ground buckles. A section of stone floor -- ten feet across, solid granite -- rises, cracks, and reshapes itself into something upright, something that has arms and legs and a suggestion of a face carved into what might be a head. It smells of deep stone. It sounds like a quarry. When it moves, the ground shakes.

name: Criosphinx
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- dual affinity reflecting the forge-heat and volcanic stone of the Scorchspires
environment: Scorchspire Foothills (rocky ridgelines, dry valleys, and exposed plateaus at the transition between the Scorchspires' volcanic slopes and the lowland desert or steppe); secondary presence in the Giant's Teeth foothills where dry, warm conditions prevail
description: The creature on the ridgeline has the body of a lion -- massive, tawny, heavy with muscle -- and the head of a ram, curling horns spiralling out from a skull broader than a man's chest. Its wings are folded against its flanks, eagle-feathered and enormous. It watches you approach with an expression that is neither hostile nor welcoming but evaluative, as though it is calculating something about you that you are not aware of.

name: Salamander, Average
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) -- forge-bound; fire with purpose, heat directed toward creation, the Lady of Fire's domain expressed as the transformative power of the forge
environment: Scorchspire Vents (the deepest volcanic vents and magma-channels of the Scorchspire range, where the Material Plane and the Elemental Plane of Fire overlap -- specifically the forge-vent complexes where salamander colonies maintain permanent workshops)
description: The heat hits you like a wall -- not the dry heat of desert air but the wet, aggressive heat of a forge-room with the bellows running. The vent ahead glows orange, and something is moving in the glow: a creature whose upper body is roughly humanoid -- muscular, dark-skinned, with eyes like embers -- and whose lower body is a coiled serpent-trunk of living fire, leaving scorched stone in its wake. It carries a spear that glows white-hot, and it is looking at you the way a blacksmith looks at unwo...

name: Popobawa
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- shadow outsider; the popobawa exists in the space between elemental resonances, a predator of the gap, registering on Kin-sense as concentrated dread -- not absence (like Dómnathar void) but active malice, the feeling of being watched by something that feeds on fear
environment: Scorchspire Settlements (the stone-built villages, farming-terraces, and valley-floor communities of Taraf-Imro territory in the Scorchspire foothills -- specifically the isolated settlements at volcanic-valley edges where darkness is absolute at night and where the gap between houses creates shadows deep enough for a predator to hide)
organization: Solitary (always -- popobawa are aggressively solitary and territorial)
description: The settlement goes quiet after dark -- not the comfortable quiet of a village at rest, but the held-breath quiet of prey that knows the predator is already inside the pen. The Taraf-Imro warrior on the roof has not moved in twenty minutes. He is watching the shadows between the stone houses, watching for the thing that does not have a fixed shape, that moves through walls without opening doors, that smells of sulphur and bat-musk and something worse -- the sweet-rot stink of a creature that fe...

name: Rast
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) -- vent-heat; the raw, undirected thermal energy of volcanic fissures, fire as geological process rather than element, heat without light
environment: Scorchspires (the volcanic vents, fumaroles, and sulphur-fields of the Scorchspire range -- specifically the active geothermal zones where the boundary between the Material Plane and the Elemental Plane of Fire is thinnest)
description: The Scorchspire vent is breathing. Not metaphorically -- the sulphurous air pulses in and out of the fissure with a rhythm that is too regular to be geological. Something is in there, something that breathes fire the way you breathe air, and when it emerges -- crawling out of the crack on too many legs, its body the colour of cooling magma, its mouth already open and already glowing -- you realise that the heat you felt from twenty feet away is not the vent. It is the creature. The vent is merel...

name: Dragonne
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- dual affinity; the dragonne is a warm-stone predator, fire in a lion's body
environment: Warm Mountains and Foothills (Scorchspire foothills, southern Giant's Teeth, dry ridgelines between the Infinite Desert and the Eternal Steppe)
description: It crouches on the ridgeline like something that could not decide whether to be a lion or a dragon and chose both. Its body is a lion's -- massive, tawny, thick with muscle -- but its head is elongated, half-snouted, with brass-coloured scales running from brow to shoulders. Enormous bat-leather wings fold along its flanks, and when it opens its mouth to roar, the sound that comes out is not a lion's roar and not a dragon's bellow but something between: a brassy, resonant blast that shakes the ...

name: Vodyanoy
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- river-lord; the resonance of a specific stretch of river claimed by an intelligence that manages it -- not corrupted water (like a spirit naga's domain) and not shadowed water (like an uthikoloshe's crossing) but governed water, river that has a master who keeps it healthy, productive, and under absolute personal control
environment: Great River / Kailian Deep (the deep pools, bridge-hollows, and river-bends of the Great River and its major tributaries, and the freshwater deeps of the Kailian Sea coast -- specifically the pools beneath bridges, dams, and river-structures where the water is deep enough and permanent enough to sustain a vodyanoy's territorial claim)
organization: Solitary or court (1 vodyanoy plus 2-4 water fey attendants -- lasa, water mephits, or small water elementals)
advancement: 11-15 HD (Large); 16-20 HD (Huge -- the eldest river-lords of the deepest pools)
description: The river-pool beneath the bridge is deep -- deeper than the river should be at this point, the bottom invisible even in clear water. Something lives in the depth, and the Eruskan rivermen know what it is because the bridge has been here for four hundred years and the thing below the bridge has been here longer. It surfaces at dusk. It is man-shaped -- roughly -- with a face like a catfish given human proportions: wide-mouthed, bearded with trailing whisker-tendrils, and green-skinned, covered i...

name: Piasa
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) -- riverine; the resonance of river-clay, deep-bank mud, and the slow patient erosion of stone by water over centuries
environment: Steppe-River Border (the river-crossings, fords, and bluff-lines where the Great River and its tributaries cut through the Eternal Steppe's edge -- specifically the transitional zone where open grassland meets river-valley, where travellers are funnelled through narrow crossing-points)
organization: Solitary (always -- piasa are aggressively territorial against their own kind)
description: The thing above the river is not a bird. You knew that before your eyes confirmed it -- the wingbeats are wrong, too heavy, displaced air moving in a pattern that no bird produces. It is a dragon, but not like the dragons the scholars describe: no chromatic scales, no metallic sheen, no alignment to the elemental framework your tutors taught you. It is the colour of river-clay and old blood, its wings are feathered like an eagle's rather than membraned like a drake's, its face carries antlers ...

name: Phi Tai Hong
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- violent death imprint; a phi tai hong is the psychic scar of a violent or unjust death burned into a location, grief and rage so concentrated it persists after the body is gone
alignment: Always chaotic evil (the original person's alignment is irrelevant -- the phi tai hong is pure concentrated anguish, not a continuation of the deceased's personality)
environment: Great River Gorge (the narrow cliff-walled sections of the Great River where the water cuts through ancient stone -- particularly the deep gorges between Pekakarlik river-towns where the current is swift, the walls are high, and deaths by drowning, falling, or ambush are most common)
organization: Solitary (always -- each phi tai hong is a unique death)
advancement: 7-10 HD (Medium); 11-14 HD (Large -- phi tai hong fed by additional violent deaths at the same site)
description: The gorge narrows where the Great River cuts through the old rock, and in the narrowing the air changes. Not temperature -- something else. The Pekakarlik boatman has gone quiet. His pole rests against the gunwale, untouched, and the barge drifts on the current alone. He is watching the cliff-face, specifically the ledge thirty feet above the waterline where the wildflowers grow too thick and too bright for a place that gets no sunlight. 'Someone died there,' he says. 'Badly.' You look again. ...

name: Charun
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of Fire (Fire) -- dual affinity; stone shaped by heat, the forge-gate, the threshold between states
environment: Great River (river-gates, bridge-arches, canal locks, and portals along the Great River and its major tributaries -- always at a point of transition, where water passes from one state or region to another); most commonly encountered at Eruskan-maintained river-gates
description: The river barge drifts toward the stone gate where the Great River narrows between two carved pillars -- remnants of Giant architecture, now maintained by Eruskan hands. Standing on the west bank is a figure that should not be there: tall, heavy-framed, grey-skinned, with a face like weathered stone and eyes like embers in a dead fire. It carries a hammer. Not a weapon -- or not only a weapon. A tool. The kind of tool used to shape stone, break locks, and open things that were meant to stay clo...

name: Culsans
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lady of the Sea (Water) -- dual affinity; the culsans embodies the meeting of currents, the point where rivers converge and winds cross
environment: Great River (river-forks, confluence points, canal junctions, and crossroads along the Great River and its major tributaries -- always at a point where two paths, two currents, or two directions meet); most commonly encountered at Eruskan-maintained river-forks in the Twin Rivers region
description: The gate stands at the river-fork where the Twin Rivers meet -- two pillars of Giant-cut stone, the carvings worn smooth by centuries of water and wind. Between the pillars, a figure stands facing both directions at once. It has two faces -- one looking upstream, one looking downstream -- set on a single head that rotates slowly, continuously, like the hands of a clock that measures something other than time. Its body is carved from the same stone as the gate, or perhaps the gate was carved from...

name: Kinnari
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- the kinnari is the waterfall-song, the place where water and air create music
environment: Great River and Sea Falls (the major waterfalls of Oreka -- the Sea Falls along the Pekakarlik coast, the Grand Falls on the Great River, and the cascading river-systems of the Giant's Teeth; always at waterfalls, never in still water or open sky)
description: The music comes from the waterfall's mist. Not from behind the waterfall, not from above it -- from the mist itself, from the place where falling water becomes air. The voice is singing, and the voice is the most beautiful thing you have ever heard. It is a woman's voice, high and clear and resonant in a way that no mortal throat produces, and when the mist parts, you see her: bird-legged, bird-winged, woman-bodied, hovering in the spray-column with effortless grace, her feathers iridescent wi...

name: Rust Monster
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- corroded; the resonance of oxidation, of metal returning to ore, of the earth reclaiming what was taken from it -- entropy given form and appetite
environment: Vestri Mining Tunnels (the active and abandoned mine-shafts, ore-processing tunnels, and metal-storage facilities of the Vestri Dwarven mining complex in the Giant's Teeth -- specifically wherever metal is mined, stored, transported, or worked)
description: The thing is about the size of a large dog, with a body like an armadillo's, antennae that wave independently of each other like a dowser's rods, and a long, flat tail that it uses for balance as it scurries toward you with a speed that is frankly alarming for a creature of its build. But you are not alarmed by the creature itself. You are alarmed by the expression on the Vestri miner's face -- the miner who has faced olgoi-khorkhoi and cave-spiders and once punched a grick -- because the miner...

name: Minotaur
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- deep; the resonance of underground stone, of passages carved by creatures that live where light does not reach
environment: Underground Labyrinths (the interconnected tunnel-systems beneath the Giant's Teeth, the Scorchspires, and the Kinsweave -- natural cave networks expanded and modified by minotaur habitation into deliberate mazes; some populations inhabit Giant-era sub-surface complexes that were designed as labyrinths)
description: The labyrinth was not built for you. It was built for something larger, something that moves through these corridors with the confidence of a creature that has memorised every turn. You have been lost for two hours. You hear it breathe -- a deep, slow rasp of something big drawing air through a bovine muzzle -- and then the sound of hooves on stone, accelerating.

name: Roper
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- living stone; the resonance of mineral deposits that have developed awareness, stone that has been stone so long it has learned to hunger
environment: Deep Caverns (the deepest natural cave-systems of the Giant's Teeth, the Scorchspires, and the Ashen Crown -- well below Vestri mining-depth, in the eternal-dark zones where no light has ever reached and the only sounds are the drip of groundwater and the occasional groan of settling rock)
description: The stalagmite has an eye. You do not see it immediately -- you have been walking through the cavern for twenty minutes, and the stalagmites here are tall and thick and densely packed, and your attention is on the ground (uneven, slick, treacherous). But the Vestri miner leading you has stopped, and he is looking at a specific stalagmite with an expression that has gone very still, very controlled, and when you follow his gaze you see it: a single yellow eye, lidless and unblinking, set in the...

name: Lindworm
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- deep; the slow pressure of mountains, the heat of the world's bones beneath the crust
environment: Giant's Teeth Deep Shafts (the deepest mine-shafts, natural fissures, and geothermal vents below Vestri mining depth -- particularly the Undershafts beneath Stoneheart, the flooded deep-tunnels of Ironwell, and the volcanic fissures of the inner Giant's Teeth)
description: The shaft goes deeper than the Vestri mapped it. The stonework ended three levels up -- down here, the walls are raw mountain, the seams of ore glinting in your lantern-light like veins in a living body. The air is warm and wet and smells of acid. You hear it before you see it: a vast, slow scraping, like a ship's hull dragging across rock, and the sound of something breathing through a mouth full of venom. The lindworm fills the tunnel. Its body is serpentine and armoured in scales the colour...

name: Zmey Gorynych
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) -- volcanic intelligence; the resonance of fire at its most calculating, the Lady's domain in its predatory, thinking aspect -- not the mindless burn of a wildfire or the nurturing warmth of a forge-bond but the deliberate, patient, strategic heat of magma that chooses where to flow
environment: Scorchspires (Rarozhki) -- the deep volcanic valleys, caldera-rims, and magma-lake caverns of the Scorchspires' inner range -- specifically the zones too hot for any creature without fire immunity, where the zmey gorynych lairs in comfort while everything else is kept at bay by temperature alone
advancement: 16-20 HD (Huge); 21-30 HD (Gargantuan -- the eldest, whose three breath weapons can devastate an army)
description: Three heads. That is what the Rarozhki scouts reported, and three heads is what you see: three serpentine necks rising from a body built like a fortress of red-black scales, each head armoured, horned, and breathing independently. The left head watches you. The centre head watches the sky. The right head watches the tunnel behind it. Nothing approaches a zmey gorynych from a single direction, because the zmey gorynych is watching every direction simultaneously. Each mouth trails smoke. Each e...

name: Adze
flags: elemental_affinity:None (Extraplanar -- summoned from the Dómnathar's home plane)
environment: Deepwater Marches (halfling settlements, marsh villages, river-trading posts)
description: The lamp at the window gutters. A firefly drifts in through the gap beneath the door -- just one, glowing faintly amber in the dark. It lands on the sleeping child's arm. By morning, the child is pale, feverish, and will not wake. The firefly is gone. There is no wound to find.

name: Aboleth
environment: Deep Apelian Sea (abyssal trenches, drowned enclaves below the Na'wasua shelf)
organization: Solitary, pair, or enclave (3-5 plus 2d10 enslaved thralls of various races)
description: The water darkens without cause. Far below, where the light of the surface is a rumour, something vast turns in the silt -- a shape like a beached whale wrapped in mucus, its three vertical eyes glowing with a cold intelligence that did not originate in this world. Tentacles trail behind it like the trains of a drowned king's robe. It came here with the Deceivers. It chose to stay. And it has been building ever since.

name: Bugbear
flags: elemental_affinity:None native -- Goblinoid (extraplanar origin, arrived with the Dómnathar host); free bugbears in contact with Oreka's elemental environment for centuries show trace Earth attunement
alignment: Usually chaotic evil (Deceiver-aligned) or usually neutral (free)
environment: Temperate mountains and forested highlands (Giant's Teeth foothills, Gatefall Reach ridgelines, Kinsweave hill-country); Deceiver-aligned bugbears are found in Dómnathar tunnel networks and underground strongholds
organization: Solitary, gang (2-4), or warband (5-12 plus 1-3 hobgoblin officers [Deceiver-aligned]) or hunting lodge (5-12 plus 1 elder [free])
description: It is bigger than you expected -- half again the height of a man, broad-shouldered, covered in coarse dark fur that smells of wet leather and something else, something sharp and wrong that prickles at the back of your Kin-sense. It moves through the forest with a silence that should be impossible for something its size, and when it looks at you from the underbrush, its yellow eyes hold an intelligence that is not malicious -- just utterly, carefully calculating whether you are worth the effort.

name: Bugbear
flags: elemental_affinity:None native -- Goblinoid (extraplanar origin, arrived with the Dómnathar host); free bugbears in contact with Oreka's elemental environment for centuries show trace Earth attunement
alignment: Usually chaotic evil (Deceiver-aligned) or usually neutral (free)

name: Goblin
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- goblinoids exist outside the elemental framework
environment: Any (goblins are the most widespread non-Kin people in Oreka -- found in every region, in every terrain, in every conceivable ecological niche from Deepwood caves to desert ruins to mountain tunnels to steppe-margin camps)
organization: Gang (4-9), band (10-100 plus 100% noncombatants plus 1 sergeant of 3rd level per 20 adults plus 1 leader of 4th-6th level), warband (10-24 with worg mounts), or settlement (40-400 plus 100% noncombatants plus 1 sergeant of 3rd level per 20 adults plus 1-2 lieutenants of 4th-5th level plus 1 leader of 6th-8th level plus 10-24 worg mounts plus 2-4 dire wolves)
description: It is three feet tall, green-skinned, loud, and there are always more of them than you expected. The one crouching behind the rock has a knife. The one in the tree has a shortbow. The one you did not see has just stolen your waterskin. Goblins are not dangerous individually -- a single goblin against a single armed Kin is a mismatch that ends quickly. But goblins are never single, never alone, and never out of ideas.

name: Goblin
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- goblinoids exist outside the elemental framework
environment: Any (goblins are the most widespread non-Kin people in Oreka -- found in every region, in every terrain, in every conceivable ecological niche from Deepwood caves to desert ruins to mountain tunnels to steppe-margin camps)

name: Girallon
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- the canopy and the trunk; girallons are creatures of the vertical forest
environment: Deepwood (the densest interior of the Great Forests -- specifically the mid-to-upper canopy zone, 60-120 feet above the forest floor, where the branches are thick enough to support their weight and the undergrowth is too dense for ground-travel)
description: Four arms. That is what you notice first -- not the size (though it is massive, nine feet at the shoulder), not the white fur (though it is matted and streaked with old blood), not the fangs (though they are longer than your fingers). Four arms, each one thick as a man's thigh, each one ending in a hand that could crush a helmet like an egg. The creature drops from the canopy on four of those arms and lands in front of you on two legs, and the remaining four spread wide, and every hand is open...

name: Dire Ape
flags: elemental_affinity:Earth/Air (canopy and trunk -- the Deepwood Paragon Ape is an expression of forest verticality)
environment: Deepwood Canopy (the highest, densest sections of the Great Forests, particularly the southern Deepwood where the canopy forms an unbroken ceiling 100+ feet above the forest floor)
organization: Solitary or company (2-4, siblings from the same Paragon siring)
description: Large Animal

name: Harpy
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- corrupted; the harpy is the wind made cruel, the Youngest Brother's domain twisted into a lure
environment: Cliffs and Marshes (coastal cliffs along the Apelian Sea, the marshy fringes of the Tidebloom Reach, and the ruin-studded cliffsides of the Kinsweave -- anywhere that combines elevation with nearby prey-populations)
description: The singing starts before you see her. It comes from above -- from the cliff-face, from the marsh-edge, from the ruin's upper story -- and it is beautiful. Not just pleasant. Beautiful in the way that makes you stop walking, stop thinking, stop being afraid. Your legs move toward the sound before your brain can object. By the time you see what is singing -- the matted feathers, the human face twisted by cruelty, the taloned feet -- you are already too close.

name: Krasue
environment: Deepwater Marches (the wetland interior of the Deepwater Marches -- stagnant pools, reed-beds, and the foggy marsh-paths that halfling fishing-communities use to navigate between settlements)
description: The light in the marsh is not a lantern. It moves wrong -- bobbing, drifting, hovering at head-height with the lazy motion of something that is not subject to wind. When it moves closer, you see what is carrying the light: a woman's head. Just the head -- floating, trailing a mass of viscera beneath it like a jellyfish trails its tentacles. The organs glow faintly from within, producing the light that lured you here. The face is beautiful. The face is always beautiful. The face is smiling.

name: Mapinguari
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- corrupted; ancient, sick, the resonance of soil that has turned toxic
environment: Deepwater Marches (the dense interior jungle of the Marches -- the oldest-growth, most inaccessible regions where goblin settlements are sparse and the canopy blocks all sunlight at ground level)
description: The jungle went quiet three days ago. Not the quiet of nightfall or the quiet of a predator passing through -- the quiet of everything leaving. The birds are gone. The monkeys are gone. The insects are muted. Something enormous is moving through the Deepwater canopy, and the stench reached you before the sound did: a rotting, animal musk so powerful it burns the back of your throat. You see it through the trees -- bipedal, shaggy, taller than a man on horseback, with one milky eye set in the ce...

name: Phi Krahang
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- corrupted marsh; the stagnant water of a swamp that has curdled, the marsh at its worst, decay given predatory purpose
environment: Deepwater Marshes (the outer edges of the Deepwater Marches -- the deep-marsh zones where mangrove gives way to open swamp, far from halfling settlements, active at night)
description: The marsh-lights are wrong tonight. The halfling fishers of the outer Deepwater Marches know their marsh -- they know the will-o'-wisps, the phosphorescent fungi, the bioluminescent insects that make the swamp glow after dark. These lights are different. They move against the wind, in formation, and they are approaching the fishing-platforms at a speed that no natural light-source produces. Then the figure emerges from the tree-line -- a man, or something shaped like a man, riding two rice-winn...

name: Phi Pop
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- undead; a parasitic undead resonance that masks itself beneath the ambient signal of its host community
environment: Deepwater Marches (halfling settlements and their surrounding marsh-communities -- not in the deep swamp but in the settlements, hiding among the living)
description: The halfling village is dying. Not suddenly -- slowly, from the inside, the way a tree dies when something is eating its roots. The strongest men sicken. The healthiest children pale and thin. The healers find nothing -- no disease, no poison, no curse that their remedies can identify. But at night, when the village sleeps, something moves through the houses. Not through the doors -- through the walls, through the floors, through the spaces between. It feeds while they sleep, and what it feeds o...

name: Shocker Lizard
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- bioelectric; the resonance of living electricity, the air-element expressed through biological discharge rather than atmospheric phenomena
environment: Deepwater Marches (the warm freshwater marshes, swamp-pools, and reed-beds of the Deepwater Marches -- specifically the shallow, still-water zones where shocker lizard colonies can establish overlapping electrical fields)
description: The marsh-pool ahead is shallow and murky, and the thing sitting on the half-submerged log is about the size of a house cat -- blue-grey, smooth-skinned, with a wide flat head and eyes that track your movement with reptilian precision. It looks harmless. Then the air crackles, your arm-hairs stand up, and the log lights up -- blue-white sparks arcing between the lizard's flanks and the water's surface. The second lizard surfaces to your left. The third is behind you. The water between them begi...

name: Shocker Lizard
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- bioelectric; the resonance of living electricity, the air-element expressed through biological discharge rather than atmospheric phenomena
environment: Deepwater Marches (the warm freshwater marshes, swamp-pools, and reed-beds of the Deepwater Marches -- specifically the shallow, still-water zones where shocker lizard colonies can establish overlapping electrical fields)
description: The marsh-pool ahead is shallow and murky, and the thing sitting on the half-submerged log is about the size of a house cat -- blue-grey, smooth-skinned, with a wide flat head and eyes that track your movement with reptilian precision. It looks harmless. Then the air crackles, your arm-hairs stand up, and the log lights up -- blue-white sparks arcing between the lizard's flanks and the water's surface. The second lizard surfaces to your left. The third is behind you. The water between them begi...

name: Stirge
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- stirges have no elemental resonance; they are blood-feeding parasites that register on Kin-sense as barely-there biological noise, too small and too simple to carry elemental identity
environment: Deepwater Marches (the warm, humid marshlands and swamp-forests of the Deepwater Marches -- specifically the stagnant pools, reed-beds, and dense undergrowth where stirge swarms breed in standing water)
description: The first one lands on your neck before you feel it. That is the thing about stirges -- they are small, they are quiet, and they latch on before you know they are there. The proboscis is thin as a needle and slides into your skin with a precision that a surgeon would envy. By the time you slap at it, the first pint is already gone. The second stirge lands on your arm. The third is on the horse. There are more in the air -- a dozen, two dozen, darting and hovering like oversized mosquitoes with ...

name: Stirge
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- stirges have no elemental resonance; they are blood-feeding parasites that register on Kin-sense as barely-there biological noise, too small and too simple to carry elemental identity
environment: Deepwater Marches (the warm, humid marshlands and swamp-forests of the Deepwater Marches -- specifically the stagnant pools, reed-beds, and dense undergrowth where stirge swarms breed in standing water)
description: The first one lands on your neck before you feel it. That is the thing about stirges -- they are small, they are quiet, and they latch on before you know they are there. The proboscis is thin as a needle and slides into your skin with a precision that a surgeon would envy. By the time you slap at it, the first pint is already gone. The second stirge lands on your arm. The third is on the horse. There are more in the air -- a dozen, two dozen, darting and hovering like oversized mosquitoes with ...

name: Will-O'-Wisp
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- deceptive; the resonance of air that misleads, the Youngest Brother's domain at its most treacherous -- not the honest wind of the steppe but the false light of the marsh, air that lies
environment: Deepwater Marches (the deep-bog zones, reed-mazes, and flooded forests of the Deepwater Marches -- specifically the treacherous areas where solid ground and bottomless bog are indistinguishable and where a single wrong step means drowning)
description: The marsh-light appears at dusk -- a pale, bobbing globe of blue-white luminescence that drifts between the reed-beds with a movement that looks deliberate. It looks like a lantern. It looks like a signal. It looks like someone is ahead of you, navigating the marsh, showing you the safe path. Follow it. Follow it deeper into the bog, where the ground becomes water and the water becomes mud and the mud closes over your ankles, your knees, your waist. The light stops. It turns. And you understan...

name: Sea Cat
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- coastal; the resonance of shallow-water hunting-grounds, of the turbulent zone where wave meets reef and the sea's predatory energy is most concentrated
environment: Apelian Sea (the coastal shallows, reef-systems, and island-chains of the Apelian basin -- specifically the warm-water zones where fish-stocks are dense enough to support a large apex predator; secondary presence along the Kailian coast)
description: The Apelian shallows are not empty -- you knew this -- but you were watching for serpents and sharks, and the thing that surfaces thirty feet from the boat is neither. It has the forelimbs, mane, and head of a great lion, and the hind-body and tail of a fish, and it moves through the water with a power and grace that a lion on land cannot match. Its roar carries across the water -- not the deep bass of a terrestrial lion but a sharper, higher sound, pitched to carry over waves -- and the fishing-...

name: Sea Hag
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- drowned malice; the resonance of water as instrument of death, not the Lady's nurturing aspect but her cold, drowning, killing aspect -- the sea that takes
environment: Kailian Coast (the rocky, tide-battered stretches of coastline where the Kailian meets the Apelian basin -- sea-caves, tidal pools, half-drowned grottoes, and the fog-shrouded coves where ships run aground and survivors wash ashore into worse)
organization: Solitary or coven (3 hags of any kind)
description: The woman on the rock is not beautiful, though the quality of her ugliness has a kind of terrible fascination. She is hunched, dripping, with kelp-tangled hair and skin the colour of a drowned corpse. Her eyes are the worst part -- they are aware, they are watching you, and behind the milky surface there is a cruel intelligence that is evaluating whether you are worth the effort. She opens her mouth. Her teeth are black. 'Come closer,' she says, and her voice is the sound of waves pulling a bo...

name: Encantado
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- the encantado is the Lady's domain expressed as charm rather than force
environment: Apelian Sea and Deepwater Marches coast (warm coastal shallows, river-mouths, tidal lagoons; in dolphin form, ranges across the full Apelian basin; in humanoid form, visits coastal settlements, particularly halfling fishing villages and the smaller Deepwater Marches trading-posts)
description: The stranger walks out of the sea at dusk, barefoot on the wet sand, wearing a hat pulled low over their forehead. They are beautiful -- unnaturally, painfully beautiful -- and they move with a liquid grace that makes every step look like swimming. They smile at the fishing village and the fishing village smiles back, because it has been a long time since anyone beautiful came to these shores. The hat stays on. It always stays on. Beneath the hat, the blowhole is still wet.

name: Mud Menders
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- constructive; the intersection of water and earth as building material, the quiet industry of riverbanks repairing themselves
environment: Marshes and River Deltas (the Deepwater Marches' mudflats, the Great River's flood-plains, and the tidal estuaries where fresh water meets the Apelian -- anywhere that river-mud accumulates in sufficient depth and elemental water-earth energy is concentrated)
description: The riverbank is repairing itself.
