Where the spring flood tore a section away, leaving raw earth and exposed roots, something is working -- dozens of small, wet, vaguely humanoid shapes made of river-mud and clay, moving with the busy purposefulness of ants. They are packing mud into the wound, weaving roots through the fill, and smoothing the surface with hands that are more suggestion than anatomy. One of them notices you watching. It stops, tilts its featureless head, and extends a hand tha...

name: Tendriculos
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- ravenous growth; the resonance of earth-fertility pushed to destructive excess, soil so rich and so hungry that everything planted in it grows beyond control and consumes everything around it
environment: Deepwood (the densest, darkest zones of the Great Forests -- the old-growth interiors where the canopy blocks nearly all light and the undergrowth is so thick that navigation is by sound and touch rather than sight)
description: The Deepwood is dense -- dense enough that sunlight reaches the forest floor only in thin, scattered beams, and the undergrowth is a tangle of moss, root, and fallen branch. The mound ahead looks like a compost-heap, green with new growth. It is not a compost-heap. The vines that compose it are moving, slowly, independently, and the opening at the mound's centre -- the one that looks like a hollow in the vegetation -- is lined with thorns the length of daggers, and it is a mouth.

name: Troll
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- feral vitality; the resonance of earth-as-regeneration, the Lord of Stone's domain expressed as the relentless, unkillable persistence of stone -- break it and it reforms, chip it and it grows back, erode it and it replaces itself
environment: Cold Mountains / Marshes (the northern Giant's Teeth foothills, the cold marshes bordering the Eternal Steppe's northern reaches, and the fog-wrapped mountain-valleys where trolls establish lairs -- anywhere cold, wet, and remote enough to support a large predator that eats constantly)
description: The thing comes out of the marsh at a lope -- six limbs of disproportionate length, a body like a collection of angles that someone forgot to soften, and a face that is all mouth. It is green, in the way that rot is green, and it smells like a compost-heap in summer, and it is very fast for something its size. But the thing about the troll -- the thing that every survivor mentions first -- is not the speed or the claws or the jaw. It is the arm you cut off that grows back. It is the wound that c...

name: Locathah
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- steady; deep-current and thermal warmth, the patient rhythm of tidal cycles
environment: Deep Apelian Trenches (the geothermal vent communities along the Apelian Sea's deepest fissures -- particularly the submerged Giant-era aqueducts and flooded temple-complexes that provide structure for locathah settlements; smaller communities in the Kailian Sea's northern deep-water shelves)
organization: Company (2-4), patrol (11-20 plus 2 3rd-level sergeants and 1 leader of 3rd-6th level), or tribe (30-100 plus 100% noncombatants plus 1 3rd-level sergeant per 10 adults plus 5 5th-level lieutenants plus 3 7th-level captains)
description: The water is warm here -- the deep Apelian currents rising through thermal vents in the trench floor, carrying minerals and heat into the crushing dark. Your water breathing spell holds, but the pressure aches behind your eyes. Movement below: a dozen figures rising from the coral-crusted ledge of a Giant-era aqueduct, their bodies sleek and scaled, their eyes reflecting your magelight with a flat, inhuman calm. They carry spears of sharpened coral tipped with Giant-ruin bronze, and they move ...

name: Merfolk
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- tidal; the steady pull of coastal currents, salt and warmth and sunlight through shallow water
environment: Apelian Sea (the shallow coastal waters, tidal flats, coral reefs, and estuaries of the Apelian Sea -- particularly the warmer southern reaches where coral growth provides shelter and the shallows are productive)
organization: Company (2-4), patrol (11-20 plus 2 3rd-level sergeants and 1 leader of 3rd-6th level), or community (20-80 plus 100% noncombatants plus 1 3rd-level sergeant per 10 adults plus 1 leader of 6th-8th level)
description: The water is warm and shallow here -- the Apelian tidal flats, where the sea meets the coral shelf and the light reaches the bottom. A face breaks the surface ten yards out, watching you with dark, calm eyes. Then another. Then six more. They are not threatening -- they are simply there, the way the sea is there, with the absolute confidence of creatures in their element. Their upper bodies are Kin-like -- close enough to human that you could mistake them at a distance -- and their lower bodies a...

name: Chuul
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- chuuls are alien to Oreka's elemental framework; see Breach Resonance
environment: Apelian Sea (coastal shallows, tidal caves, submerged ruins along the Apelian basin); secondary presence in Tidebloom Reach (brackish tidal pools, flooded Giant ruins at Hillmeet and Foxfen)
description: It rises from the shallows like a nightmare designed by committee -- lobster pincers the size of shields, a segmented carapace slick with brine, and a mass of writhing tentacles where its mouth should be. Its eyes, set on short stalks, swivel independently, tracking two different targets at once. It does not look like it belongs in this water. It does not look like it belongs anywhere. It looks like something that crawled out of a place that has different rules about what shapes are acceptable.

name: Leviathan-Shard
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- alien; so ancient and so deep that it reads as something from before the Lady shaped the waters
environment: Any coastal or wetland (Leviathan-shards wash ashore on the Apelian Sea coast and are found in free goblin camps throughout the Deepwater Marches; specimens have been transported as far as the Tidebloom Reach by goblin traders)
organization: Solitary or clutch (2-4, usually in a goblin camp)
description: You smell it before you see it -- brine and depth and something older, a pressure-smell like the deep ocean forced into a space too small to contain it. The goblin camp ahead has arranged its tents in a wide semicircle around a pit lined with wet stones, and in the pit there is a thing. A mass of translucent flesh, pale blue and shot through with veins of bioluminescence that pulse in a slow rhythm unrelated to anything on land. It is the size of a cart-horse, and it is alive in a way that mak...

name: Mamlambo
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- corrupted; stagnant, hungry, the patience of water that has forgotten how to flow
environment: River-Steppe Border (the wide, slow-moving river crossings where the Great River's tributaries meet the Eternal Steppe -- the fording-points, gravel shallows, and seasonal flood-pools used by steppe herds and Mytroan caravans)
description: The river crossing has been safe for a decade. The Warg packs ford here every season and the Mytroan riders use it as a watering-point on the steppe-circuit. Then the horses stopped drinking. The Wargs crossed upstream, three miles out of their way, and when asked why, the pack-leader said only: 'The river has teeth now.' You crouch at the bank and the water looks normal -- brown, shallow, running smooth over gravel. You cannot see what is lying on the bottom, because the thing on the bottom h...

name: Nak
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- brackish; the murky, salt-tinged water where river meets sea, neither fully fresh nor fully salt
environment: Pekakarlik Docks (the timber-pylon dock-complexes of Pekakarlik coastal and estuarine trading-posts -- the pilings, jetties, and harbour structures where river-barges meet sea-going vessels along the Apelian coast)
description: The dock is old Pekakarlik construction -- heavy timber pylons driven into the estuary-mud, planked with salt-treated oak, worn smooth by a century of barge-keels and bare feet. At the dock's end, where the pylons meet the water, something lives between the pilings. The children see it first -- they always do -- a crocodilian shape the size of a large dog, long-snouted and dark-scaled, watching the dock-traffic from the shadows between the timbers with bright, unblinking eyes. It is not a crocod...

name: Inkanyamba
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- storm-water; the inkanyamba commands the intersection of water and wind, generating weather through elemental will
environment: Inland Lakes (the large, deep freshwater lakes of the Taraf-Imro territories in the southern mountains and volcanic lowlands -- specifically the caldera-lakes and tectonic rift-lakes that are deep enough to support a Huge aquatic predator year-round)
description: The lake has been wrong for three days. The fishermen noticed first -- the water-level rising when it should be falling, the currents running backward, the fish moving to the shallows in panicked schools. Then the storms started. Not regular storms -- targeted storms, centred on the lake, rotating with a focus that suggests intelligence rather than weather. Something beneath the surface is angry, and whatever it is, it is large enough to control the water above it.

name: Nasnas
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- aberrant; a creature split from the elemental framework, registering on Kin-sense as half a signature, a broken signal that never resolves
environment: Na'wasua Groves (the tidal-forest groves and mangrove-islands of Na'wasua sea-elf territory along the Apelian coast -- particularly the remote outer groves where the forest meets the sea and the boundary between land and water is ambiguous)
description: The Na'wasua grove is wrong. The sea-elves noticed it first -- the resonance shifted, the water in the tidal-pool tastes sour, and something is moving at the edge of the tree-line with a gait that is not animal, not Kin, and not right. It hops. That is the first thing you notice -- it hops, because it has one leg. One arm. Half a face, split vertically, so the left side is smooth flesh and the right side is exposed muscle and bone. It is fast -- faster than anything with one leg should be -- and ...

name: Shedim
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- liminal; shedim exist in the twilight between elemental signatures, in the perceptual gaps where Kin-sense transitions between readings and cannot resolve clearly
environment: Twilight Zones (the liminal spaces at the edges of goblin settlements -- the grey areas between day and night, between forest and clearing, between the wild static of goblin communities and the blank absence of uninhabited wilderness; shedim manifest where the world is between states)
description: The twilight is wrong. You have walked through twilight a hundred times -- the grey hour between day and night when the world is neither lit nor dark, when shadows have no source and distance becomes unreliable. This twilight does not end. It has been grey for twenty minutes, and the sun is not setting and has not risen, and the goblin settlement ahead is quiet in a way that goblin settlements are never quiet. Something stands in the gap between the last house and the treeline -- a shape that i...

name: Mazzikin
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- mischievous; the air that steals your hat, the wind that snuffs your candle
environment: Any (mazzikin are found wherever goblins gather -- goblin camps, trading-posts, and caravans in the Deepwater Marches, but also goblin-Kin border-communities, Eruskan market-towns with goblin populations, and isolated settlements where the boundary between fey and mortal is thin)
description: You cannot see them. That is the point -- mazzikin are invisible, bodiless, and everywhere, and the only evidence of their presence is the mischief. Your bootlaces are tied together. Your waterskin is empty though you filled it an hour ago. The goblin trader's pack has been rearranged so that the heavy items are on top and the fragile items are on the bottom. From somewhere near the ceiling, something giggles -- a high, musical laugh that sounds like a child who has never been told no.

name: Klatterkin
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- construct; breach resonance indicates extra-dimensional origin of animating principle
environment: Any (wherever goblins are -- klatterkin attach themselves to goblin communities and travel with them; found in goblin settlements, camps, and warbands across every region of Oreka)
description: The goblin warband's camp is unusually well-equipped. That is the first thing the scout notices -- not scavenged armour and stolen weapons, but purpose-built equipment: a ballista assembled from scavenged parts that somehow works better than it should, a camp-perimeter alarm made of bones and wire that triggers with mechanical precision, and in the centre of the camp, tended by two goblins with the careful hands of acolytes, a contraption. It is three feet tall, made of wood and metal and teet...

name: Dybbuk
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- goblinoid dead carry the same absence of elemental resonance as living goblinoids, but twisted by undeath into something colder
environment: Any area with goblinoid populations (Deepwater Marches free goblin settlements, Eternal Steppe Warg communities that harbour goblins, Dómnathar tunnel fringes where goblin auxiliaries are stationed)
description: The goblin stops mid-sentence. Its companions notice the silence first -- Krikkit never stops talking, has not stopped talking since the day it learned Common from a halfling trader -- and then they notice the eyes. Krikkit's eyes have gone flat. Not dead. Not vacant. Full, the way a cup is full when someone has poured something different into it. The goblin's mouth opens and a voice comes out that is not Krikkit's voice. It is older, heavier, and it speaks Goblin with an accent that hasn't bee...

name: Qareen
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- personal; the resonance of one person's relationship with the sea, intimate and specific, like the sound of a wave that has only ever broken on one stretch of shore
alignment: Usually neutral (matches bonded partner's alignment tendency)
environment: Na'wasua Groves (the tidal grove-settlements, sea-caves, and reef-shelters of Na'wasua territory along the Apelian coast -- specifically the places where Na'wasua community life happens, where the boundary between land and sea is most permeable and the Lady of the Sea's presence is most intimate)
advancement: 5-8 HD (Small); 9-12 HD (Medium -- qareen whose bonded partner reaches great age or spiritual depth)
description: The Na'wasua diver does not swim alone. You understand this intellectually -- you have been told this, multiple times, by people who know -- but you do not believe it until you see it. She dives from the canoe into water that is twenty fathoms deep and very dark, and as she descends, something descends with her. It is not visible, not exactly -- more like a shimmer in the water where a shimmer has no business being, a shape that the current defines by flowing around rather than through. It moves...

name: Si'Lāt
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- false intimacy; a si'lāt generates water-resonance that mimics the personal, intimate quality of a qareen bond, creating a false sense of trust and familiarity that exploits the Na'wasua's deepest emotional connections
environment: Na'wasua Groves (the tidal grove-settlements, reef-caves, and lagoon-shelters of Na'wasua territory -- specifically the locations where Na'wasua community life is most intimate and where the boundary between welcomed guest and infiltrating predator is thinnest)
description: The Na'wasua grove is quiet -- the natural quiet of a reef-sheltered lagoon at low tide. The woman walking through the shallows ahead of you is beautiful in the way that Na'wasua sea-elves are beautiful -- long-limbed, dark-skinned, silver-haired, moving through the water with the unconscious grace of someone born to it. She turns. She smiles. And then your qareen -- the companion-spirit you have had since birth, the water-presence that has never left your side -- goes cold. Not distant. Cold. Th...

name: Rakshasa
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- false; a rakshasa generates a convincing but artificial elemental resonance that matches whatever identity it is currently wearing, constructed through sheer force of will and centuries of practice; the false resonance is detectable as fake only on a DC 25 Kin-sense check
environment: Warm Settlements (the river-cities of the Eruskan, the oasis-towns of the Pekakarlik, the larger trading-posts of the Kinsweave border-kingdoms -- anywhere commerce creates wealth, complexity creates cover, and population density creates prey)
description: The merchant is perfect. His smile is warm, his voice is cultured, his robes are exactly the quality a man of his station would wear -- not ostentatious, not modest, the precise median. His hands are soft and well-manicured. His knowledge of the spice-trade is encyclopaedic. He has been a fixture of this Eruskan river-city for eleven years, and every person who knows him considers him reliable, generous, and utterly trustworthy. The only thing wrong with him -- the only thing -- is that his hand...

name: Uthikoloshe
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- shadow-current; the resonance of water in shadow, river-current that flows through the darkest places, the Lady of the Sea's domain where her light does not reach -- not corrupted water (like a spirit naga's domain) but shadowed water, water that has become a medium for darkness
special_qualities: vulnerability to sunlight on dry land
environment: Warg Territory (the rivers, streams, and water-crossings of Warg pack territory on the Eternal Steppe -- specifically the fords, shallow crossings, and river-bends where Warg packs cross between seasonal grazing-grounds, where the water is accessible and the uthikoloshe can ambush wading creatures)
description: The river-crossing is shallow -- knee-deep, cold, and fast-moving. The Warg pack-scout wades in without hesitation and stops. The water around his ankles has darkened -- not with mud but with something else, a shadow that has no source and no shape, a presence in the water that is cold in a way that river-water in summer should not be. The scout backs out slowly. 'Uthikoloshe,' he says to the pack-leader. His voice is flat. 'In the water. We go around.' The pack-leader looks at the river -- the ...

name: Tengu
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- canopy-wind; the resonance of wind in the highest forest, air channelled through branch-architecture into currents that only canopy-dwellers know, the Youngest Brother's domain at its most refined and most playful
environment: High Canopy (the upper canopy of the Great Forests -- specifically the Hasura canopy-settlement zones, where the forest-architecture of branch-platforms and vine-bridges creates an aerial environment that tengu navigate with natural ease)
description: The Hasura canopy-platform is a hundred feet above the forest floor, and the creature standing at the edge of the platform should not be able to stand there -- the branch is too narrow for anything larger than a squirrel. But the tengu stands with the casual balance of something born to the air, its taloned feet gripping the bark with an ease that makes the Hasura sentinel beside you look clumsy by comparison. It is man-shaped -- roughly -- with a raven's head, a raven's beak, and wings folded a...

name: Ooze Mephit
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- stagnant; the unmoving, brackish water of marshes where nothing flows, things decay, and the water itself seems to have given up
environment: Apelian Marshes (the coastal wetlands and tidal marshes where the Apelian Sea meets the Deepwater Marches -- brackish pools, reed-beds, mangrove-shallows, and anywhere that stagnant water accumulates in sufficient volume)
description: The marsh is the wrong colour. Not the usual brown-green of Apelian wetland -- a sickly, iridescent sheen like oil on water, and it moves, shifting across the surface in patterns that are too regular to be current and too slow to be wind. A bubble rises from the muck and pops with a sound like a wet cough. Something climbs out of the bubble -- small, dripping, translucent, with a body that seems unable to decide whether it is liquid or solid. It has eyes, of a sort -- dark spots in the gelatinou...

name: Water Elemental, Large
environment: As Small Water Elemental
description: The river rises. Not floods -- the water level does not change -- but a column of river-water pulls itself upright, shaping limbs and a torso and something that might be a head from the current itself. It stands on the river's surface like a person stands on a floor, and within its translucent body you can see river-debris turning slowly: pebbles, weeds, a small fish swimming in confused circles. When it moves, the sound is rain on stone.

name: Tepegöz
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- Dómnathar void; like all Dómnathar-forged creatures, a tepegöz registers as active absence on Kin-sense, the characteristic hollow signature of beings manufactured rather than born
environment: Dómnathar Tunnels (the wider passage-networks and excavation-chambers of active Dómnathar tunnel-systems -- specifically the zones where tunnel construction has created spaces large enough for a 12-foot creature to move freely, or where the tepegöz has widened the tunnels itself through decades of habitation)
description: The tunnel is wider here -- wider than Dómnathar standard, wider than Vestri bore-gauge, and the walls are scored with claw-marks that are too large and too deep for any creature you've encountered underground. The smell hits you next: rancid meat, old blood, and something underneath that is neither -- the cold, mineral stink of something that has been living underground for a very long time without ever seeing sunlight. The thing that fills the tunnel ahead is twelve feet tall, broad as a cart...

name: Supay
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- sovereign depth; the resonance of the deepest earth, the stone that has never seen light, the Lord of Stone's domain at its most absolute and most ancient -- not the worked-stone of mines or the living-stone of mountains but the primordial substrate beneath everything
environment: Deep Caverns (the deepest natural cave-systems beneath the Giant's Teeth foothills -- specifically the ancient mine-shafts and subterranean chambers that Orean mountain-communities have mapped and sealed, where the earth is old enough to have its own ruler)
organization: Solitary (always -- a supay claims a domain and rules it alone)
description: The mine-shaft is old -- older than the Vestri tunnels above it, older than the Orean settlements in the foothills, old enough that the tool-marks on the walls were made by hands larger than any Kin's. The air at the bottom of the shaft is not stale. It is warm, the wrong kind of warm -- not geothermal warmth but the warmth of a presence, of something breathing in the dark. The Orean miners who mapped this shaft went no further than this landing, and the reason is carved into the wall in Old Or...

name: Dire Tiger
flags: elemental_affinity:Earth/Water (the Deepwood hunter -- shadow, patience, and the water-courses that thread the forest floor)
environment: Deepwood (the densest interior of the Great Forests, along river-courses and near forest pools where large prey comes to drink)
description: Large Animal

name: Oni
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- dark, primal; deep-forest earth, the resonance of soil that has been dark since the world began, where the forest-floor is older than the trees growing on it
environment: Deep Forests (Hasura territory and beyond -- the oldest, densest interior of the Great Forests where the canopy blocks all direct sunlight and the undergrowth is primordial)
description: The deep forest changes character past the Hasura border-markers. The trees are older, the canopy is lower, and the undergrowth is thick enough to hide a horse. The Hasura rangers who guided you this far have stopped. They will not go further. They point to a mark on a tree -- a claw-mark at nine feet, old and deep -- and speak the name quietly, as though the creature might hear: oni. In the forest beyond the marker, something laughs. It is a deep laugh, rich with amusement, and it comes from t...

name: Troglodyte
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- shallow-cave; the resonance of limestone and mineral-earth near the surface, cool and damp, the Lord of Stone's domain at its most accessible and least imposing
environment: Shallow Caves (the upper cave-systems, rock-shelters, and limestone karst formations found across Oreka -- specifically the shallow cave-zones above Vestri mining-depth, where natural cave-systems provide shelter, moisture, and protection from surface weather)
organization: Clutch (2-5), squad (6-11 plus 1-2 monitors of 3 HD), or band (20-80 plus 20% noncombatants plus 3-13 monitors of 3 HD plus 1 chieftain of 3-6 HD)
description: The cave-mouth smells before you reach it. The stench is organic, chemical, and deliberately weaponised -- a musk so thick it coats the back of your throat and makes your eyes water at thirty feet. The creatures that emerge from the smell are reptilian: bipedal, grey-green, scaled, with flat faces and cold eyes that regard you with a hostility that is not personal but absolute. You are warm-blooded. You do not belong in their caves. The stench intensifies. They are doing that on purpose.

name: Grimlock
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- grimlocks exist outside the elemental framework, like all non-Kin peoples
environment: Deep Underground (natural cave systems below the depth of Vestri mining, below Dómnathar corridors, in the deep strata where light has never reached and the concept of 'surface' is an abstraction)
organization: Gang (2-4), pack (10-20), or tribe (20-40 plus 1-3 leaders of 3rd-5th level plus 2-5 dire bats)
description: It has no eyes. That is the first thing you notice -- the flat, smooth expanse of face where eyes should be, unbroken by sockets, lids, or any concession to sight. It does not need eyes. It navigates by sound, by scent, by the vibration of your footsteps through the stone, and by a sense you cannot name that tells it exactly where you are, how large you are, and where to put the axe.

name: Bodak
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- bodaks are breach-warped; they exist outside the elemental system entirely
environment: Gatefall Reach (Silence Breach zones, dead-Kin-sense areas near Dorrach Kael); secondary presence at the Lament of Kings (unstable resonance zones) and in deep Dómnathar tunnel networks where planar barriers are thin
description: It was alive once -- you can tell because it is shaped like something that was alive. Two arms, two legs, a head. But the flesh is the color of burnt tallow, stretched tight over a frame that seems to have been emptied from the inside. The eyes are the worst: pale, featureless white, like two holes punched through the face into a void behind it. It does not breathe. It does not blink. When it looks at you, something behind your sternum goes cold and stops.

name: Phase Spider
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- between; phase spiders exist partially outside the elemental framework, their resonance flickering between present and absent as they shift between planes
environment: Gatefall Reach Forests (the mixed deciduous forests of the Gatefall Reach -- particularly the border-zone near the Silence Breach where the planar membrane is thinnest and phase-shifting is easiest)
description: The web is invisible. Not hidden -- invisible. The spider spins its web on the Ethereal Plane and waits there, watching the Material Plane through the membrane between worlds, until a creature walks into the space that corresponds to the web's location. Then it shifts -- phases -- from the Ethereal to the Material, materialising directly behind its prey with fangs already extended. The attack lasts less than a second. Then the spider phases back, taking its venom and its victim's screams with it...

name: Jorogumo
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- aberration; breach resonance, deliberately masked
environment: Gatefall Reach (the waterfall-basins, pool-edges, and cliff-hollows of the Gatefall -- the great chain of waterfalls in Hasura territory where the river-systems descend from the highland forests to the lowland valleys; specifically the remote, infrequently-patrolled falls where prey can be taken without immediate discovery)
advancement: 8-10 HD (Medium); 11-14 HD (Large -- true spider form only)
description: She is beautiful. She is always beautiful -- that is the point, that is the design, that is the trap. She sits at the edge of the Gatefall, where the forest canopy overhangs the waterfall-pool, and she combs her hair with her fingers and watches the water and waits for someone to find her. The Hasura scouts found the cocoons first -- three of them, wrapped in silk the colour of moonlight, hanging from the underside of the cliff-ledge behind the falls. Inside each cocoon, a Hasura elf. Two were ...

name: Cadi Kuşu
flags: elemental_affinity:Corrupted -- the Cadı Kuşu retains traces of its original Kin affinity, but the signature is cracked and wrong, contaminated by stolen Deceiver magic
environment: Gatefall Reach (settlements near the Silence Breach perimeter, abandoned farmsteads, forest edges); secondary presence in the Kinsweave border-kingdoms and Tidebloom Reach where Dómnathar influence reaches furthest
description: The bird perches on the rooftop at dusk -- dark-feathered, crow-sized, with eyes that catch the light wrong. It watches the village with a patience that no animal possesses. When it opens its beak, the sound that emerges is not a birdcall but something like a woman humming through a mouthful of glass. The villagers bar their doors. They know what a Cadı Kuşu is. They know what it used to be.

name: Manananggal
environment: Silence Breach Zones (the Kin-sense dead zones near Dómnathar surface-breach points -- particularly in the Gatefall Reach and the outer Kinsweave, where the residual metaphysical damage of the Deceivers' War entry-point creates conditions that sustain this specific form of undead)
description: You find the lower half first. It is standing upright in a Dómnathar-ruined basement, legs together, trunk severed cleanly at the waist, and it is warm. Blood pools slowly at the separation point but does not spill -- as though the body is waiting for something to return. The cellar smells of vinegar and old blood and the particular sterile absence of a place where Kin-sense dies. You hear wings above you -- heavy, wet, membranous wings -- and something laughs in the dark with a voice that used ...

name: Spectre
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) -- extinguished; the resonance of fire that has gone out, heat that has been removed, the Lady of Fire's domain inverted into its opposite -- not cold (which is the absence of heat) but the memory of heat, the agonising awareness of warmth that no longer exists
environment: Ashen Hollows (the dead volcanic valleys of the Scorchspire foothills -- places where volcanic activity ceased, where the Lady of Fire's presence once burned and now does not, leaving a residue of elemental grief that produces the most powerful incorporeal undead in the region)
description: The Ashen Hollows are the places where fire died -- the volcanic valleys of the Scorchspire foothills where eruptions once poured lava and now pour nothing, where the ground is cold and grey and the trees are petrified stumps. Nothing lives here. That is the first sign that something dead does. The shape that rises from the grey earth is not smoke -- it is darker than smoke, more defined, and it moves with the deliberate purpose of something that remembers being alive and hates you for still be...

name: Wight
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- a wight is elemental absence, the dead animated by hunger without any of the elemental identity that defined them in life
environment: Any (barrows, crypts, battlefields, and sealed burial-chambers across Oreka -- wherever the dead were interred without proper rites or where necromantic contamination has reached preserved corpses)
description: The barrow opens into a chamber that has not been touched by light since it was sealed. The figure inside is standing -- not lying, not seated, but standing against the back wall with its arms at its sides and its eyes open. It was Kin once. You can tell by the bone-structure, the remnants of clothing, the corroded weapon still gripped in one hand. But the eyes are wrong -- flat, lightless, and lit from within by something that is not life. It sees you. It steps forward. The temperature drops.

name: Wight
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- a wight is elemental absence, the dead animated by hunger without any of the elemental identity that defined them in life
environment: Any (barrows, crypts, battlefields, and sealed burial-chambers across Oreka -- wherever the dead were interred without proper rites or where necromantic contamination has reached preserved corpses)
description: The barrow opens into a chamber that has not been touched by light since it was sealed. The figure inside is standing -- not lying, not seated, but standing against the back wall with its arms at its sides and its eyes open. It was Kin once. You can tell by the bone-structure, the remnants of clothing, the corroded weapon still gripped in one hand. But the eyes are wrong -- flat, lightless, and lit from within by something that is not life. It sees you. It steps forward. The temperature drops.

name: Dire Boar
flags: elemental_affinity:Earth (the rooter, the ground-breaker -- dire boars are earth made aggressive)
environment: Forest-Steppe Border (the transition zone between the Great Forests and the Eternal Steppe, where undergrowth and grassland overlap)
organization: Solitary or herd (2-4 with mundane boars)
description: Large Animal

name: Wendigo
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- frozen, corrupted; the resonance of wind stripped of warmth, the Youngest Brother's playful domain twisted into predatory cold -- not the natural cold of winter but the hungry cold of something that has consumed all warmth in its vicinity and still wants more
environment: Gatefall / Kinsweave Forests (the deep, cold forests of Gatefall Reach and the northern Kinsweave border-kingdoms -- specifically the zones where Dómnathar influence has thinned the elemental fabric and winter persists longer than it should, where the boundary between natural cold and supernatural hunger is thinnest)
advancement: 13-18 HD (Large); 19-24 HD (Huge -- the eldest and most terrible)
description: The hunger comes before the creature does. You feel it first in your stomach -- a hollow, gnawing emptiness that has nothing to do with whether you've eaten -- and then in your mind, where it becomes something worse: the certainty that nothing will ever fill you, that the emptiness is permanent, and that the only thing that might -- might -- ease it is the warmth of another living body, broken open. The trees above creak. Something enormous moves through the canopy with a speed that should be imp...

name: Dullahan
flags: elemental_affinity:Corrupted -- the dullahan carries a twisted echo of the Far Rider's air-earth resonance (wind-over-grassland), broken by death and scarred by Dómnathar raising
environment: Great Forests and Eternal Steppe (the Dead Road and the old Far Rider patrol routes that stretch from the forest-steppe border through the Tidebloom Reach to the edges of the Lament of Kings; occasionally found in deep Pasua territory where Far Rider outposts once stood)
organization: Solitary or patrol (2-4 dullahan riding together)
description: The rider comes down the Dead Road at a gallop, and the horse beneath it is wrong -- too thin, too dark, its hooves striking sparks that glow the colour of old blood. The rider sits tall in the saddle, armoured in the remnants of Far Rider scout-leather, a sword in one hand and nothing on its shoulders. Nothing. The head is gone. Where the neck ends, a ragged stump of old flesh and exposed bone meets the open air, and the air around the stump shimmers with a faint, sick light that is neither f...

name: Púca
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- crossroads; the resonance of the place where road meets road, where earth and air mix at the boundary between tame and wild, cultivated and untamed, known and unknown
environment: Forest-Steppe Border (the crossroads, forest-edges, river-fords, and boundary-points where the Great Forests meet the Eternal Steppe -- specifically the transitional zones where no single Kin culture claims authority and where the rules of civilisation begin to fray)
advancement: 6-8 HD (Large); 9-12 HD (Huge -- rare, very old specimens)
description: The horse is standing at the crossroads where the forest meets the steppe, and it is the most beautiful horse you have ever seen -- midnight-black, tall, with eyes that gleam like copper coins in the last light of day. It is watching you with an intelligence that horses do not possess, and its ears are pricked forward with an expression you would describe as amused. The Pasua ranger beside you says, very quietly, 'Don't get on.' You were not planning to get on. But now that she's said it, you ...

name: Orc
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- wild static; orcs, like goblins, register on Kin-sense as static rather than a clean elemental signature, though orc static is coarser and more aggressive than goblin static
environment: Any (hill-country, cave-systems, ruins, forest-edges, and the margins between Kin territories -- anywhere that provides shelter, defensible ground, and access to raiding targets)
organization: Gang (2-4), squad (11-20 plus 2 3rd-level sergeants and 1 leader of 4th-6th level), or band (30-100 plus 150% noncombatants plus 1 3rd-level sergeant per 10 adults, 5 5th-level lieutenants, and 3 7th-level captains)
description: The raiders come out of the ravine at a run -- fast, organised, and angry. There are a dozen of them, grey-skinned and heavy-jawed, wearing patchwork armour assembled from three different cultures' discarded kit. The one in front carries an axe that was Orean-forged before someone reground the edge to a crueler angle. They do not shout. They do not threaten. They come in silent, trained, and focused, which means these are not the disorganised raiders the border-patrols warned you about. These ...

name: Hippogriff
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- similar to griffons but lighter, more air-dominant; the hippogriff is wind-over-hill rather than wind-over-mountain
environment: Giant's Teeth Foothills (the low, grassy hills and upland meadows of the Giant's Teeth margins -- lower elevation than griffon territory, in terrain where grassland meets slope and thermal-updrafts are strong)
description: The horse has wings. That is the simplest, most reductive description, and it is wrong -- the creature is not a horse with wings any more than a griffon is an eagle with a lion's body. It is its own thing: part raptor, part equine, shaped by the same elemental forces that produced griffons but with a different emphasis. Where the griffon is all predator, the hippogriff is half-wild, half-willing, and that willingness is what makes it valuable.

name: Cù Sìth
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- the border between wind and root, the place where forest meets grassland
environment: Forest-Steppe Border (the transitional zone where the Great Forests thin into grassland -- specifically the Pasua-claimed stretches of the western and southern forest-edge, the Kinsweave-Steppe frontier, and the wooded margins of the Twin Rivers region)
description: The dog is too large. That is the first thing -- it stands shoulder-height to a horse, its body lean and deep-chested, built for endurance rather than speed. Its fur is dark green, the colour of moss on old stone, and it moves through the undergrowth without disturbing a single branch. Its eyes are gold and ancient and watching you with an intelligence that no dog should possess. It does not bark. It does not growl. It simply appears at the tree-line, considers you, and waits to see what you w...

name: Yuki-Onna
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- a yuki-onna's resonance is frozen grief, undeath shaped by cold, the elemental connections of the original person iced over and preserved but inaccessible, like a voice heard through a frozen windowpane
alignment: Usually neutral evil (but see below -- some yuki-onna retain fragments of their living personality and may spare travellers under specific conditions)
environment: Northern Forests (Hasura) -- the snow-deep forests and mountain-passes of the Hasura homelands, where winter is long and the dead do not always stay buried beneath the snow -- specifically the high passes, blizzard-routes, and deep-winter trails where travellers are most vulnerable to exposure
advancement: 7-9 HD (Medium); 10-12 HD (Medium -- the eldest, most powerful, whose frozen embrace can kill in a single round)
description: The snow falls harder after midnight, and the trail disappears. You are not lost -- you know exactly where you are -- but the cold has reached the place inside your chest where decisions live, and you cannot quite remember why you were walking. You should sit down. You should rest. The snow is soft. The cold is not painful anymore; it is becoming warm, which you know -- in the part of your mind that is still thinking -- means you are dying. And then she is there: standing in the snow without foot...

name: Karakoncolos
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- the karakoncolos exists in the gap between elemental signatures, in the dead zones where Kin-sense cannot reach
environment: Kin-Sense Dead Zones (areas where elemental resonance has been drained, suppressed, or corrupted -- typically near Dómnathar surface-breach points, in the ruins of Dómnathar outposts, and at the edges of False Silence fields)
description: The dead zone is wrong. Not dead -- dead zones are common in Oreka, places where Kin-sense returns nothing, where the elemental resonance has been drained or suppressed or was never there. Those are empty. This place is not empty. This place is full, and you cannot hear what is filling it. Something stands in the silence where your Kin-sense should be, and it is looking at you through the gap in the world.

name: Goreham (Brute)
environment: Temperate Hills (the low foothills and rolling terrain between the Giant's Teeth and the settled lowlands -- lower elevation than Hrokk territory, further from mountain peaks than Fogwrack lairs, occupying the most accessible terrain where Brutes can sustain themselves on livestock, game, and stolen food)
organization: Solitary, pair, gang (3-4), or mob (5-8, rare -- a degraded War-era formation, less cohesive than Hrokk warbands)
description: It is ten feet tall and most of that is gut. Where the Fogwrack is a mockery of Giant grace and the Hrokk is a mockery of Giant efficiency, the Goreham is a mockery of Giant endurance -- a lumbering, thick-skinned, dim-witted slab of Dómnathar-manufactured flesh that exists to absorb damage and keep moving forward. It is not fast. It is not clever. It is not frightening in the way the Fogwrack is frightening. It is simply, relentlessly, stupidly present, and it will not stop coming until it is...

name: Maero (Brute)
environment: Mountain Valleys (the densely-forested mountain valleys of Kovaka coastal territory -- the steep-walled river-valleys, old-growth ravines, and fog-bound coastal forests of the southwestern Great Forests)
organization: Solitary, pair, or band (3-5, rare -- degraded War-era formation)
description: The Kovaka call them te tangata mohoao -- the wild people -- but there is nothing of a person in what steps out of the tree-line. It is eight feet of matted hair and raw-boned muscle, its arms too long for its body, its hands thick-fingered and blunt as mallets. The face is wrong in the particular way that all Brute faces are wrong: close enough to Kin to trigger recognition, far enough from Kin to trigger revulsion. It smells of rotting bark and swamp-water and something chemical beneath both ...

name: Cockatrice
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- like the basilisk, the cockatrice's petrification is an expression of Earth dominance
environment: The Eternal Steppe (open grasslands, scrubby hillsides, field-margins near agricultural settlements); secondary presence in the Kinsweave lowlands and along the forest-steppe border
description: It struts out of the tall grass like it owns the steppe -- a rooster-sized creature with the body of a scrawny chicken, the wings of a bat, and a tail that ends in a scaly, serpentine point. Its feathers are patchy and iridescent, shifting between sickly green and tarnished bronze. Its eyes are beady, stupid, and mean. It looks ridiculous. Then it pecks a field-mouse and the mouse turns to stone mid-squeak, and you stop laughing.

name: Wyvern
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother / Lady of Fire (Air/Fire) -- warm-hill predator; the resonance of warm updraughts rising from sun-heated hillsides, the air-fire intersection that produces the thermal columns wyverns ride to altitude -- not the intellectual fire of dragons or the sacred wind of thunderbirds, but the simple, functional heat that a predator uses to fly
environment: Warm Hills (the sun-heated hill-country and low ridgelines of central and southern Oreka -- specifically the foothills where thermal updraughts are reliable, where the terrain provides cliff-nesting sites and the plains below provide prey, and where the warm-season climate supports year-round flying)
description: The shape in the sky resolves as it banks -- two legs, two wings, a barbed tail longer than its body, and a head that is all jaw and no subtlety. It is not a dragon. It does not have a dragon's intelligence, a dragon's breath, or a dragon's treasure-hoard ambition. What it has is a tail-stinger loaded with enough venom to drop a horse, a wingspan that lets it carry that horse into the sky, and the single-minded predatory focus of a creature that has identified you as the right size for dinner.

name: Owlbear
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- fused; a dual resonance that reads as two signals imperfectly merged, as though something forced bear-earth and owl-air together and the result never fully integrated
environment: Great Forests (the mid-depth Great Forests -- deeper than the forest-edge where Pasua patrol, shallower than the deep interior where oni and dire tigers dominate -- the broad middle-zone of mixed deciduous and coniferous forest that is the Great Forests' most common terrain)
description: The thing about the owlbear is that you hear both halves before you see either. The hoot comes first -- deep, resonant, wrong, because it comes from a throat the size of a barrel rather than a bird's syrinx. Then the growl beneath the hoot, the ursine rumble that tells your hindbrain a very large bear is nearby. Then it comes through the undergrowth like a landslide with feathers, and the two sounds become one creature: eight feet of muscle, beak, claws, and bad intentions, moving with the spe...

name: Aranea
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- dual affinity, with Air dominant
environment: Twin Rivers and Kinsweave (Great Forests, deep canopy; especially common in the Hasura heartlands and the old-growth Deepwood border)
description: It looks like a spider -- a hand-sized spider, mottled brown and grey, building a web between two branches with the unhurried patience of a craftsman who has all day. Then you notice the web's pattern: not random, not instinctive, but geometric -- a mandala of concentric shapes that no spider should know how to make. And then it turns, and the cluster of eyes that looks back at you has something behind them that spiders' eyes do not have.

name: Bakeneko
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- dual affinity, Air dominant
environment: Twin Rivers (Great Forests, Tanuki groves and longhouse settlements; occasionally in Hasura border-villages where forest meets cultivated land)
description: The cat sits on the rooftop of a Tanuki longhouse, washing its face with one paw. It has been sitting there since morning. It was sitting there yesterday, too. The Tanuki children wave to it; the Tanuki elders do not. They have noticed that the cat's shadow does not match its posture, and that its tail -- if you watch long enough -- splits at the tip and then mends itself when it realises you are looking.

name: Darkmantle
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- faint, ambient; the darkmantle is a cave creature through and through
environment: Underground (natural cavern systems, Giant ruin undercrofts, Dómnathar tunnel fringes); found beneath any region with cave networks, most commonly reported in Vestri mining territories and the tunnels beneath the Infinite Desert
description: The stalactite opens its eyes. That is what it looks like -- a cone of wet stone hanging from the cavern ceiling suddenly sprouts two tiny, reflective eyes and unfurls a membrane of leathery skin that was folded so tightly against its body that it was invisible. Then it drops.

name: Lasa
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- gentle; river-current and willow-shade, the slow music of water over stone
environment: Great River (the quiet bends, willow-pools, and sacred confluences of the Great River system -- particularly the Eruskan stretches between Twin Rivers and the Sea Falls, where the river widens and the current slows)
organization: Solitary, pair, or confluence (3-8 at a major river junction)
description: The river is quieter here -- the current softens where the willow-roots meet the water, and the evening light turns the surface to hammered copper. You see her sitting on the stones at the water's edge, bare feet in the current, combing her hair with a comb made of river-shell. She is not quite solid. The light passes through her edges. When she turns to look at you, her eyes are the colour of deep water, and the song she has been humming settles into your chest like a second heartbeat. The Gr...

name: Lasa
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- gentle; river-current and willow-shade, the slow music of water over stone
environment: Great River (the quiet bends, willow-pools, and sacred confluences of the Great River system -- particularly the Eruskan stretches between Twin Rivers and the Sea Falls, where the river widens and the current slows)
organization: Solitary, pair, or confluence (3-8 at a major river junction)
description: The river is quieter here -- the current softens where the willow-roots meet the water, and the evening light turns the surface to hammered copper. You see her sitting on the stones at the water's edge, bare feet in the current, combing her hair with a comb made of river-shell. She is not quite solid. The light passes through her edges. When she turns to look at you, her eyes are the colour of deep water, and the song she has been humming settles into your chest like a second heartbeat. The Gr...

name: Huldra
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- the forest-river border; where water meets earth and both are alive
environment: Forest-River Borders (the banks and shallows of rivers that flow through the Great Forests -- specifically the transition zone where forest canopy overhangs running water, and the worlds of wood and water overlap)
description: She is standing in the river. The water is waist-deep and moving fast, and she is standing in it as though it were a warm bath -- barefoot on the stones, her back to you, her long hair trailing in the current. She is beautiful. You can tell that even from behind, from the line of her shoulders and the way the water parts around her as though it likes her. Then she turns, and you see her face, and she is more beautiful than you expected. And then you see her back -- or rather, you see that her b...

name: Monstrous Spider
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- faint; the quiet attunement of small creatures that live in dark places close to stone
environment: Any (monstrous spiders are found throughout Oreka -- Great Forests, underground cave-systems, Giant ruins, Vestri mines, Dómnathar tunnel-fringes, and any enclosed environment where webs can be anchored; specific subspecies favour specific environments)
description: The web is not visible until you are in it. The silk is the colour of shadow and it clings to everything -- armour, hair, skin -- with an adhesive strength that is entirely out of proportion to its apparent fragility. Something moves at the edge of your torchlight. It has too many legs and too many eyes and it is larger than you want it to be.

name: Satyr
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone / Youngest Brother (Earth/Air) -- forest-edge; the resonance of where cultivated land meets wildwood, earth-under-leaves meeting wind-through-branches, the boundary between the tamed and the untamed expressed as music
environment: Great Forests (the mid-forest zones between deep canopy and forest-edge -- specifically the clearings, stream-banks, and meadow-openings where sunlight reaches the ground and the forest is at its most welcoming and most deceptive)
description: The music reaches you before the musician does. It is coming from deep in the forest -- a pipe-melody that winds between the trees like smoke, changing key with each turn of the path, and you find yourself walking toward it without having decided to. The undergrowth parts. The clearing opens. He is sitting on a fallen oak, legs crossed -- the legs of a goat, furred and hooved, below the bare-chested torso of a man who looks like he has never done anything more strenuous than smile. He is playin...

name: Grig
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- the grig is the playful overlap of earth and air; the cricket in the grass, the music on the breeze
environment: Great Forests (forest clearings, meadow-margins, stream-banks, and the sun-dappled edges of the canopy -- wherever the forest is light and warm enough for music)
description: You hear the fiddle first. Something is playing a fiddle in the undergrowth -- playing it well, playing it fast, playing a reel that makes your feet want to move before your brain can object. You part the ferns and see the musician: a creature the size of your hand, cricket-legged, fairy-winged, grinning like a lunatic, sawing away at a fiddle the size of a matchstick with a bow the size of a needle. It sees you seeing it and plays faster.

name: Nixie
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- gentle; the clear water of shallow lakes, sunlight on the surface, the warmth of still pools
environment: Rivers and Lakes (freshwater lakes, slow rivers, mill-ponds, and spring-fed pools throughout the Great River system and the Deepwater Marches -- anywhere that still, clear freshwater collects in sufficient depth)
description: The lake is clear and shallow at the edges, the kind of place where children wade and fishermen cast from the bank. You see the light first -- a flicker of green-gold beneath the surface, like sunlight catching a fish-scale, except the light moves with purpose. A head breaks the surface: small, angular, green-skinned, with large dark eyes and hair the colour of waterweed. She treads water effortlessly and regards you with the frank curiosity of something that owns this lake and considers you a...

name: Kodama
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- the deep, slow earth-resonance of a living tree's connection to the soil
environment: Great Forests (the interior of the oldest forests -- wherever a tree has stood long enough and grown deep enough to develop a kodama; never in young growth, plantations, or recently-cleared areas)
organization: Solitary (one per tree, always) or grove (3-20, one per tree in a kodama-grove)
advancement: 1-3 HD (Tiny -- larger kodama inhabit larger, older trees)
description: The sound is not a sound. It is a vibration -- a resonance that comes from the tree itself, from the ring of wood inside the bark, from the sap moving through channels that have been open for five hundred years. If you press your ear to the trunk and listen with more than your ears, you hear it: a pulse, slow and patient, like a heartbeat that measures its time in seasons rather than seconds. The kodama is not in the tree. The kodama is the tree's awareness of itself.

name: River Serpent
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- warden; the resonance of the living river, water that is healthy and watched and protected, the Lady of the Sea's freshwater domain maintained through her eldest servants
environment: Great River (the full length of the Great River and its major tributaries -- from the headwaters in the Giant's Teeth foothills through the Eruskan river-cities to the delta where the Great River meets the Apelian Sea; also present in the Twin Rivers region and Kinsweave lake systems)
advancement: 6-10 HD (Large); 11-15 HD (Huge -- transition to Great Serpent)
description: The Great River is deep here -- deep enough that you cannot see the bottom from the barge's railing, deep enough that the Pekakarlik boatman navigates by current-reading and memory rather than by sight. And then, in the deep green below, something moves. It is long -- longer than the barge -- and it moves with the effortless sinuosity of a creature for which the river is not habitat but home in the most fundamental sense. You catch a flash of iridescent scale, blue-green and silver, and a head t...

name: Treant
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- ancient forest-foundation; the resonance of the earth beneath a forest's oldest roots, soil that has been forest-floor for so long that the trees and the earth have become one system -- not air (though the canopy moves in wind) but earth, the foundation from which all growth proceeds
environment: Great Forests (the old-growth hearts of the Great Forests -- specifically the zones where trees have grown undisturbed for centuries, where the root-networks are deep and interconnected, and where the forest's elemental identity is strongest)
description: The oak in the clearing is old -- older than the settlement downstream, older than the Pasua patrol-routes that pass its roots, old enough that its trunk is wider than three people standing shoulder to shoulder. You have walked past it twice today. You did not notice the face. You notice it now because the face is looking at you, and the eyes -- formed from knots in the bark, framed by ridges that were not there this morning -- express a patience so vast and so slow that your entire life is, to ...

name: Tsukumogami
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- object-soul; the resonance of an object that has been used, loved, and maintained for so long that the earth-element within its material has developed a rudimentary awareness -- clay remembering hands, metal remembering forge-heat, wood remembering the tree
environment: Tanuki Groves (the communal lodge-complexes and craft-workshops of Tanuki settlements -- specifically the older groves where objects have been in continuous use for decades, creating the conditions of sustained attention and care that produce tsukumogami)
advancement: 2-3 HD (Tiny); 4-6 HD (Small -- tsukumogami of larger objects: wardrobes, looms, forge-bellows)
description: The teapot is dancing. You did not expect the teapot to dance. It is a perfectly ordinary ceramic teapot -- well-made, well-used, chipped at the spout from decades of daily service -- and it is executing a small, precise, delighted dance on the shelf, its lid clapping in rhythm. The Tanuki grandmother beside you is unsurprised. 'That's Katsu,' she says. 'He was a teapot for forty years before he decided to be a person too. Best tea in the grove. Don't ask about his opinions on water-temperature...

name: Zashiki-Warashi
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- hearthstone warmth; the resonance of a well-built house, a foundation laid true, earth-in-its-sheltering-aspect -- not the deep bedrock of the yer-sub but the domestic, nurturing earth that makes a house a home
environment: Tanuki Lodges (the longhouses, inns, and communal halls of Tanuki communities -- specifically well-maintained, long-established structures where generations of care have created the conditions for fey habitation; zashiki-warashi never appear in new buildings, temporary shelters, or neglected structures)
advancement: 2-3 HD (Small -- longer residence deepens the blessing)
description: You will not see it. That is the first thing to understand. You will hear it -- small feet on wooden floors, a child's giggle behind a screen, the sound of something being moved in a room where no one is standing. You will feel it -- the house is warm, the food tastes better, the neighbours are kind. The garden grows. The baby sleeps through the night. The family prospers. And you will never, not once, see the zashiki-warashi -- but it is there, has always been there, and the moment it leaves, y...

name: Noppera-Bō
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- aberrant; a creature that registers on Kin-sense as a blank space in the shape of a person, like a silhouette cut from the world
environment: Great Forests (Tanuki groves and the surrounding old-growth forest -- particularly the twilight-zones between Tanuki settlements and the deeper forest, where the lantern-light ends and the shadows begin)
description: The path through the Tanuki grove is well-lit -- paper lanterns strung between the trees, their warm light turning the forest-floor into a patchwork of gold and shadow. You have been walking for twenty minutes and you are beginning to suspect that the path is longer than it should be. The figure ahead of you on the path is a Tanuki woman -- round-faced, small, carrying a basket of mushrooms. She has been walking ahead of you since you entered the grove. She has not turned around. You call out. ...

name: Water Mephit
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- mist-mischief; the Lady's domain at its most trivial and annoying
environment: Great River (river-banks, bridge-hollows, canal-sides, and anywhere the Great River generates mist)
description: The river-mist thickens -- unnaturally, in a column that resolves into a grinning, dripping, knee-high figure with wings like wet sails and a face that is all malicious delight. It spits a stream of acid-tinged water at your boot and cackles.

name: Roc
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- sovereign; the most massive expression of air-resonance in Oreka's living creatures, wind given the weight and authority of a mountain, the sky's own apex predator
environment: Mountain Peaks (the highest peaks of the Giant's Teeth, the Scorchspires, and the Ashen Crown -- above the snow-line, above the cloud-line, in the thin air where nothing else flies and the wind is the only law)
description: The shadow passes over the caravan and everything goes dark -- not like a cloud, because clouds don't move that fast and don't have that shape. You look up. The wingspan blots out the sun. It is a bird -- your brain insists it is a bird, because it has wings and feathers and a beak -- but it is a bird the way a mountain is a hill. It is the size of a ship. Its talons, each one longer than a man is tall, are open and reaching, and the thing it is reaching for is the lead pack-elephant, which weig...

name: Cloud Giant
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- overwhelmingly strong, the air-attunement of a creature that was shaped when the Youngest Brother's power was undivided
environment: Giant's Teeth (the highest summits above the permanent snowline, cloud-wrapped peaks accessible only by climbing or flight; a handful are known to lair in the upper reaches of the Emberfangs and the northern Ashen Crown)
organization: Solitary, pair (mated), or family (2 adults plus 1d4 young)
description: The summit is not empty. What you took for a formation of ice-covered boulders is a chair -- an enormous chair, carved from the living stone of the peak -- and sitting in it is a figure thirty feet tall, wrapped in fog as though fog were a garment. Its skin is pale as glacier-melt. Its hair is white and moves in a wind that does not touch you. Its eyes, when it opens them, are the blue of a sky seen from a height where the air runs out.

name: Simurgh
flags: elemental_affinity:All four Elemental Lords -- harmonised; the simurgh carries all four elemental signatures in deliberate, conscious balance -- not the absolute unity of the qilin (which is an event) but the studied, wise harmony of a creature that has learned to hold all four elements in equilibrium through millennia of existence
environment: Giant's Teeth Peaks (the highest, most remote peaks of the Giant's Teeth -- above the roc nesting-zones, above the ccoa territories, in the thin air where only the wind and the simurgh remain)
description: The mountain is silent -- the kind of silence that exists only above the snow-line, where the wind has scoured the rock clean of everything that could make a sound. And then the silence breaks, not with noise but with presence. Something is above you -- something vast, slow-moving, and radiating an awareness that feels like standing in sunlight after years indoors. You look up. The wingspan covers the sky. It is a bird -- or it was a bird, once, before it became something else: feathered in bron...

name: Otyugh
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- aberrant; otyughs register on Kin-sense as a faint, unpleasant signal that doesn't map to any elemental framework
environment: City Sewers and Dungeons (the sewage-systems, refuse-pits, and underground waste-channels of Eruskan cities, Vestri mining-camps, and any settlement large enough to generate concentrated organic waste)
description: The sewer smells exactly as bad as you expected, but the thing in the sewer smells worse. It occupies the junction where three tunnels meet -- a bulbous, three-legged mass of mottled brown-grey flesh, waist-deep in the effluent, with two long tentacles rising from its body like the antennae of something that evolved specifically to live in places no other creature would tolerate. Its third appendage -- a thick, eyestalk on a muscular neck -- swivels toward you with the calm, unsurprised attentio...

name: Doppelganger
flags: elemental_affinity:None native -- doppelgangers are alien to Oreka; see Breach Resonance
environment: Any settlement (doppelgangers are urban predators found wherever Kin populations are dense enough to provide cover -- primarily Kinsweave border-kingdoms, Eruskan river-cities, Tidebloom Reach towns, and the larger Pekakarlik oasis-settlements)
description: It looks like you. It looks exactly like you -- your face, your build, your clothes (not real, just shaped flesh mimicking fabric), and the way you hold your shoulders when you are tired. It even smells like you, or close enough that a casual observer would not notice. But its thoughts are not your thoughts, and behind the mask of your face, something alien is calculating how long it can wear you before someone notices.

name: Mimic
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- mimics exist outside the elemental framework entirely; their physiology registers as an absence that is not Dómnathar void but simply nothing
environment: Giant Ruins (the interior rooms, corridors, and vaults of Giant-era ruin-complexes throughout the Infinite Desert -- any structure where there are objects to mimic and prey to attract; secondary populations in Vestri deep-mines and Dómnathar-adjacent tunnel-systems)
description: The chest is the only thing in the room that is not covered in dust. You notice this, but you notice it too late -- your hand is already on the lid, and the lid is already on your hand. The surface beneath your fingers is warm and wet, and the chest is opening in a direction that chests do not open, and the inside of the chest is full of teeth.

name: Domovoi
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- fire dominant; the domovoi is a hearth-spirit, born where fire meets stone
environment: Scorchspires (Rarozhki dwarf forges, hearthstones, and workshops -- always inside a structure with an active fire source); rare secondary presence in Eruskan river-settlements and Pekakarlik oasis-forges
description: The forge-fire is banked for the night, the embers glowing low, and something moves in the warm space between the anvil and the hearthstone. It is small -- no taller than a dwarf-child -- and shaped like a very old dwarf who has been compressed by time until everything non-essential has been squeezed out, leaving only beard, eyes, and an expression of severe domestic judgment. It checks the fire. It checks the tools. It picks up a hammer that has been left on the wrong hook and places it on the...

name: Griffon
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- the griffon is sky-and-mountain, the Youngest Brother's wind beneath the Lord of Stone's weight
environment: Giant's Teeth Foothills and temperate highlands (rocky hills and upland meadows with clear sight-lines, nesting on cliff-faces and high ledges -- the transition zone between the Giant's Teeth mountains and the lower grasslands)
description: The shadow passes over you first -- wide, fast, and shaped wrong for a bird. You look up and see something impossible: a lion's body, golden and muscular, suspended beneath an eagle's wings that span thirty feet. The front half is pure raptor -- hooked beak, taloned forelegs, golden feathers merging seamlessly into tawny fur at the shoulders. It banks, catches the thermal rising from the hillside, and circles once, looking down at you with a golden eagle's eye that misses nothing.

name: Griffon
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- the griffon is sky-and-mountain, the Youngest Brother's wind beneath the Lord of Stone's weight
environment: Giant's Teeth Foothills and temperate highlands (rocky hills and upland meadows with clear sight-lines, nesting on cliff-faces and high ledges -- the transition zone between the Giant's Teeth mountains and the lower grasslands)
description: The shadow passes over you first -- wide, fast, and shaped wrong for a bird. You look up and see something impossible: a lion's body, golden and muscular, suspended beneath an eagle's wings that span thirty feet. The front half is pure raptor -- hooked beak, taloned forelegs, golden feathers merging seamlessly into tawny fur at the shoulders. It banks, catches the thermal rising from the hillside, and circles once, looking down at you with a golden eagle's eye that misses nothing.

name: Chimera
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) dominant, with traces of Lord of Stone (Earth) and Youngest Brother (Air) -- a creature of three natures, none fully integrated
environment: Steppe-Mountain Border (the rocky highlands where the Eternal Steppe meets the Giant's Teeth foothills; secondary presence in the Scorchspire foothills and the dry ridgelines between the Kinsweave and the Infinite Desert)
description: Three heads. That is the first thing you register -- three heads on a body that should not be able to support even one of them. The lion's head roars. The goat's head screams. The dragon's head, dark-scaled and smouldering, simply opens its mouth and breathes fire across the rocks where you were standing a moment ago. The body is massive, a fusion of lion and goat with bat-wings spreading wide enough to blot out the pass behind it. It does not look like something that was designed. It looks li...

name: Ettin
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- fragmented, faint; the ettin carries the residual earth-resonance of the Giant heritage, corrupted by degeneration into something dull and heavy
environment: Giant's Teeth (mountain caves, rocky overhangs, and abandoned Giant ruin-margins in the lower peaks; occasionally found in the Scorchspire foothills and the dry badlands between the Giant's Teeth and the Infinite Desert)
organization: Solitary, pair, gang (3-4), troupe (1-2 plus 1-2 brown bears), or band (3-5 plus 1-2 brown bears)
description: Two heads argue over which way to go. This is what you hear first -- the voices, deep and guttural, snapping at each other in a language that is almost Giant but not quite, the way a parrot's speech is almost language but not quite. Then the owner of the voices steps around the boulder and you see both heads on a body that is thirteen feet tall, hunched, broad as a cart, and carrying a morningstar in each hand. Both heads notice you simultaneously. They stop arguing. For the first time in minu...

name: Pishtaco
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- dead cold; mountain-earth drained of warmth, the resonance of stone that should be alive with geological heat but has been hollowed out, leaving only cold mass
environment: Mountain Passes (the high passes, narrow defiles, and single-track trails of the Giant's Teeth and surrounding ranges -- specifically the remote, infrequently-travelled passes between Orean foothill communities where travellers are isolated and rescue is days away)
advancement: 8-10 HD (Medium); 11-14 HD (Large -- pishtaco that have fed extensively grow bloated and swollen)
description: The mountain pass smells wrong. You have been climbing for six hours through good clean air -- pine-resin, cold stone, the metallic scent of altitude -- and now something has changed. It smells like a butcher's shop on a warm day: blood and fat and the sweetish tang of meat beginning to turn. The Orean guide has stopped walking. He is looking at the path ahead, where the rocks narrow to a single-file passage between two cliff-faces, and he is looking at the ground, where something glistens in t...

name: Derro
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- derro are alien to Oreka's elemental system; see Breach Resonance
environment: Deep Underground (natural cavern systems beneath any region, particularly the deep strata beneath the Kinsweave and Giant's Teeth where derro warrens intersect Vestri mining tunnels)
organization: Team (2-4), squad (5-8 plus 1 3rd-level sorcerer), or band (11-20 plus 30% noncombatants plus 3 3rd-level sorcerers plus 1 5th-level savant leader)
description: Small. Blue-skinned. Mad. Those are the three things every Vestri miner knows about derro, and they are listed in order of importance. Small enough to fit through cracks you wouldn't think twice about. Blue-skinned, the colour of deep-water bruise, which means you can lose them in torchlight shadow. Mad, which means they will do things no sane creature would do -- attack a party of armed dwarves with stone knives, set traps in tunnels they themselves use, experiment on captured prisoners with ...

name: Ogre
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- dim; a faint, dull earth-resonance that reads as 'rock that doesn't know it's alive'
environment: Temperate Hills (the foothills and hill-country surrounding the Giant's Teeth, Scorchspire margins, and the hilly transition-zones between forest and steppe -- anywhere with caves for shelter and livestock within raiding distance)
description: The campfire was not built by someone who knows how to build a campfire. The logs are too big, stacked wrong, and the fire is more bonfire than cooking-fire -- throwing light and smoke a hundred yards in every direction. The bones around the fire-pit are mixed: deer, sheep, dog, and something disturbingly humanoid. The smell is appalling. Then the ground trembles, and the thing that built the fire comes back through the tree-line carrying a cow under one arm. It is nine feet tall, pot-bellied,...

name: Ogre
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- dim; a faint, dull earth-resonance that reads as 'rock that doesn't know it's alive'
environment: Temperate Hills (the foothills and hill-country surrounding the Giant's Teeth, Scorchspire margins, and the hilly transition-zones between forest and steppe -- anywhere with caves for shelter and livestock within raiding distance)
description: The campfire was not built by someone who knows how to build a campfire. The logs are too big, stacked wrong, and the fire is more bonfire than cooking-fire -- throwing light and smoke a hundred yards in every direction. The bones around the fire-pit are mixed: deer, sheep, dog, and something disturbingly humanoid. The smell is appalling. Then the ground trembles, and the thing that built the fire comes back through the tree-line carrying a cow under one arm. It is nine feet tall, pot-bellied,...

name: Troll
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- feral vitality; the resonance of earth-as-regeneration, the Lord of Stone's domain expressed as the relentless, unkillable persistence of stone -- break it and it reforms, chip it and it grows back, erode it and it replaces itself
environment: Cold Mountains / Marshes (the northern Giant's Teeth foothills, the cold marshes bordering the Eternal Steppe's northern reaches, and the fog-wrapped mountain-valleys where trolls establish lairs -- anywhere cold, wet, and remote enough to support a large predator that eats constantly)
description: The thing comes out of the marsh at a lope -- six limbs of disproportionate length, a body like a collection of angles that someone forgot to soften, and a face that is all mouth. It is green, in the way that rot is green, and it smells like a compost-heap in summer, and it is very fast for something its size. But the thing about the troll -- the thing that every survivor mentions first -- is not the speed or the claws or the jaw. It is the arm you cut off that grows back. It is the wound that c...

name: Earth Elemental, Medium
description: The ground buckles. A section of stone floor -- ten feet across, solid granite -- rises, cracks, and reshapes itself into something upright, something that has arms and legs and a suggestion of a face carved into what might be a head. It smells of deep stone. It sounds like a quarry. When it moves, the ground shakes.

name: Water Elemental, Small
environment: Any body of water; most common in the Great River and its tributaries, the Kailian and Apelian Seas, and the Deepwater Marches
description: The river rises. Not floods -- the water level does not change -- but a column of river-water pulls itself upright, shaping limbs and a torso and something that might be a head from the current itself. It stands on the river's surface like a person stands on a floor, and within its translucent body you can see river-debris turning slowly: pebbles, weeds, a small fish swimming in confused circles.
