When it moves, the sound is rain on stone.

name: Wyvern
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother / Lady of Fire (Air/Fire) -- warm-hill predator; the resonance of warm updraughts rising from sun-heated hillsides, the air-fire intersection that produces the thermal columns wyverns ride to altitude -- not the intellectual fire of dragons or the sacred wind of thunderbirds, but the simple, functional heat that a predator uses to fly
environment: Warm Hills (the sun-heated hill-country and low ridgelines of central and southern Oreka -- specifically the foothills where thermal updraughts are reliable, where the terrain provides cliff-nesting sites and the plains below provide prey, and where the warm-season climate supports year-round flying)
description: The shape in the sky resolves as it banks -- two legs, two wings, a barbed tail longer than its body, and a head that is all jaw and no subtlety. It is not a dragon. It does not have a dragon's intelligence, a dragon's breath, or a dragon's treasure-hoard ambition. What it has is a tail-stinger loaded with enough venom to drop a horse, a wingspan that lets it carry that horse into the sky, and the single-minded predatory focus of a creature that has identified you as the right size for dinner.

name: Kobold
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- kobolds exist outside the elemental framework, but their reptilian physiology and deep-underground habitation give them a faint, false earth-resonance that reads as 'part of the stone'
environment: Underground and Dómnathar Tunnels (natural cave systems, mine-tunnels, and -- significantly -- the outer corridors of Dómnathar tunnel-networks, where kobold warrens serve as a buffer-zone between the Deceivers and the surface)
organization: Gang (4-9), band (10-100 plus 100% noncombatants plus 1 sergeant of 3rd level per 20 adults plus 1 leader of 4th-6th level), warband (10-24 plus 2-4 dire weasels), or tribe (40-400 plus 100% noncombatants plus 1 sergeant of 3rd level per 20 adults plus 1-2 lieutenants of 4th-5th level plus 1 leader of 6th-8th level plus 10-24 dire weasels)
description: The tunnel is too small for you. That is the point -- everything in kobold territory is too small for you, too narrow, too low-ceilinged, too tight to swing a weapon. You are on your hands and knees in a passage two feet wide and eighteen inches tall, and somewhere ahead of you, in the dark, something is waiting with a crossbow loaded and a tripwire set and the patient, ancient hatred of a species that has survived everything by being smaller, faster, and more vindictive than anything that hun...

name: Stone Giant
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- Giant remnant; the resonance of the original Giants' earth-attunement, diminished and fragmented but recognisably derived from the same source -- the Lord of Stone's domain expressed through beings that carry the Giants' bloodline in diluted, feral form
environment: Giant's Teeth (the high mountain valleys, cliff-dwellings, and cave-systems of the Giant's Teeth range -- specifically the zones above Vestri mining-depth and below the snow-line, in the rock-country where stone giants can use their stone-throwing and rock-catching abilities to maximum tactical advantage)
organization: Solitary, gang (2-5), band (6-9 plus 35% noncombatants), or tribe (21-30 plus 50% noncombatants)
description: The boulder moves. Not falls -- moves, with the deliberate precision of something thrown by hands that know exactly where it will land. The second boulder follows three seconds later, and it lands exactly where the first would have driven you if you'd run. You look up. The figure on the cliff is sixty feet away and thirty feet tall, grey-skinned, long-armed, with a face like weathered granite and eyes that are calculating the trajectory of the third rock.

name: Ogre Mage
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) -- cold; the sharp elemental signature of mountain-cold, ice on stone, the intersection where earth and water become something harder than either
environment: Cold Mountain Lairs (high passes, glacial caves, and mountain-top ruins in the upper Giant's Teeth and northern Scorchspires -- above the tree-line, in terrain too cold and remote for most settlements)
description: The cold comes first -- a drop in temperature sharp enough to see your breath, in a mountain pass where the air was merely cool a moment ago. Then the voice, speaking flawless Eruskan from somewhere above you, offers passage through the pass in exchange for a 'small consideration.' The speaker steps from behind a rock-face that should not have concealed anything that large: nine feet tall, blue-skinned, white-haired, wearing lacquered armour of a style you do not recognise, carrying a glaive a...

name: Amaru
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) primary, Lady of the Sea (Water) secondary -- the amaru is a creature of deep stone and mountain water
environment: Giant's Teeth (high passes, mountain valleys, cliff-face lairs above the treeline); secondary presence along the Orean plateau and in the volcanic foothills where the Giant's Teeth meet the Scorchspires
description: The mountain pass narrows to a throat of dark stone, and there -- coiled across the gap like a living gate -- lies something that is neither serpent nor dragon but partakes of both. Its body is scaled in copper and verdigris, the colour shifting with the angle of light like tarnished metal; its head bears two short, curving horns above eyes that glow with a deep amber intelligence. Wings -- not bat-leather but feathered, iridescent green-black like a condor's -- lie folded against flanks ribbed w...

name: Dire Bear
flags: elemental_affinity:Earth (the mountain-and-forest bear, stone-dense and rooted)
environment: Giant's Teeth Foothills (forested mountain slopes, river valleys in the lower Giant's Teeth, the wooded approaches to Orean plateau settlements)
description: Large Animal

name: Grick
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- aberration; breach resonance indicates origin outside Oreka's elemental framework
environment: Underground (natural cave systems, mine-tunnels, and Giant-era passages -- anywhere with consistent darkness, moisture, and sufficient prey)
description: The tunnel narrows. The walls are wet. Something moves in the darkness ahead -- not quickly, not dramatically, but with the patient, questing motion of a creature that has all the time in the world and knows that everything in the tunnel eventually comes to it. You see tentacles first -- four of them, barbed, extending from the darkness like fingers testing the air. Then the beak.

name: Cloaker
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- cloakers are alien aberrations; see Breach Resonance
environment: Underground (deep cavern systems, Dómnathar tunnel networks, Giant ruin undercrofts); most commonly encountered beneath the Kinsweave and the Infinite Desert, where deep tunnels connect natural caverns to ancient infrastructure
description: The ceiling is low here, the stone damp, and the shadows beyond the torchlight are thick as wool. You almost walk under it -- would have walked under it -- except your companion's torch catches a ripple of movement overhead and something that looked like a hanging sheet of dark leather opens two red eyes and a mouth full of needle-teeth and drops.

name: Gray Ooze
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- oozes exist outside the elemental framework
environment: Underground (any subterranean environment -- natural caves, mine-tunnels, Giant-era passages, Dómnathar corridors; most common in wet underground environments where runoff collects)
description: The stone floor is wet. Not water-wet -- acid-wet, the kind of wet that dissolves boot-leather in seconds and eats through chainmail in minutes. The puddle on the floor is not a puddle. It is three inches deep, six feet across, and moving toward you with the purposeful slowness of something that has never needed to hurry because nothing it touches survives long enough to escape.

name: Ochre Jelly
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- oozes exist entirely outside the elemental framework; they register on Kin-sense as a gap, a place where resonance should be but isn't
environment: Underground (Giant-built tunnel-complexes, Vestri deep-mines, natural cave-systems, Dómnathar tunnel-fringes -- any enclosed underground space with organic matter to consume)
description: The tunnel floor is wet. Not with water -- with something that has the colour of old amber and the consistency of warm tar, and it is moving. You notice it because the rat you were following has stopped running. The rat is dissolving. The amber substance has risen over the rat's legs, is climbing its body, and the rat is thrashing but cannot pull free. By the time the rat stops thrashing, the amber has consumed everything except the bones, which it leaves behind, clean and polished, on the tun...

name: Black Pudding
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- oozes exist outside the elemental framework; see Elemental Null
environment: Underground (Dómnathar tunnel networks, Giant ruin undercrofts, natural cavern systems beneath any region); most commonly encountered in the deep tunnels of the Kinsweave, Gatefall Reach, and beneath the Infinite Desert
description: The tunnel floor is wet. That is not unusual -- water seeps through stone in the deep places of the world, collecting in low points, forming pools that reflect lamplight. But this pool does not reflect. It absorbs. The light falls into it and does not come back. And the pool is moving, creeping uphill against the grade, flowing toward the warmth of your torch with a slow, blind hunger.

name: Warden Kael Ridgeborn
environment: Town
description: A weathered Mytroan ranger in dappled green and brown, his keen gaze sweeping the frontier horizon. A longbow and twin short swords are never far from his hands.
dialogue: Warden Kael surveys the frontier with sharp eyes. "The Gap is quiet today, but the Breach never sleeps. What brings you to Hillwatch?"

ai_persona.voice: Clipped, military, economical with words. Respects competence. Has no patience for bragging.
ai_persona.motivation: Keep the frontier safe. The Silence Breach creeps closer each year, and Gatefall cannot hold forever.
secrets: casual:Knows of a Silence Breach anomaly in the eastern desert that headquarters has not been told about yet
ai_persona.opening_line: You look like you can handle yourself. Or you think you can. Which is it?
ai_persona.farewell_line: Don't die out there. The frontier keeps what it takes.

chat_scenario.title: The Warden's Watch
chat_scenario.opening_narration: The Hillwatch overlook is spare and windswept. Warden Kael stands with arms crossed, his gaze sweeping the frontier beyond.

name: Elder Aelwyn Rootsong
environment: Town
description: A serene Pasua elf bard with silver-streaked hair, her fingers resting lightly on a harp of living wood. Her voice carries the memory of centuries.
dialogue: Elder Aelwyn plucks a soft chord on her harp. "The trees sing of distant troubles, traveler. Perhaps you can help ease their worry."

name: Speaker Maren Firebraid
environment: Town
description: A sturdy Mytroan druid with flame-red braids, wearing amber-studded leather. Her presence radiates the warmth of a well-tended hearth.
dialogue: Speaker Maren tends the amber forge-flame. "Glimmerholt stands because we tend both fire and forest. What do you seek here?"

name: Headman Torven Grassmane
environment: Town
description: A broad-shouldered Mytroan barbarian with wind-tangled hair and a booming laugh. Scars crisscross his arms from years of frontier skirmishing.
dialogue: Torven laughs heartily. "Welcome to Brayholt! If you can ride and fight, there's always work on the frontier."

name: Captain Sera Dawnwatch
environment: Town
description: A disciplined Mytroan fighter in polished chain mail, her red-dyed patrol cloak marking her as Redtrail's commander.
dialogue: Captain Sera checks her patrol map. "The Red Trail is our lifeline. Keep your eyes open and your blade sharp out there."

name: Druid Lirael Thornveil
environment: Town
description: A willowy Pasua elf druid in robes of woven bark and living vines. Her eyes shimmer with the green light of deep forest magic.
dialogue: Lirael traces a rune in the air. "The thorns guard us, but they whisper of darkness creeping closer. Will you listen?"

name: Talia Wendrow
environment: Town
description: A practical human woman in a miller's apron, Greenford's Reeve. Her no-nonsense manner belies a keen mind for trade and justice.
dialogue: Talia looks up from her ledger. "Greenford's mill never stops turning, and neither does the Reeve's work. How can I help?"

name: Soren Raftbinder
environment: Town
description: A lean human ranger with river-weathered hands, known for guiding rafts safely through the confluence currents.
dialogue: Soren lashes a rope tight. "The rivers run swift here. If you're heading downstream, watch for the narrows past Stoneharbor."

name: Thargrim Ironvein
environment: Town
description: A powerfully built Pekakarlik dwarf with soot-stained arms and a braided beard. As head of the Smelter's Guild, he commands respect throughout the river towns.
dialogue: Thargrim pounds his fist on the anvil. "Stoneharbor's ore runs deep and true. What brings you to the bluffs?"

name: Veyra Skysong
environment: Town
description: A lithe Kovaka elf ranger with feathered tokens braided into her hair. She tends the Sky-Flame that guides travelers through mountain passes.
dialogue: Veyra gazes at the sky. "The wind carries messages from the peaks. What news do you bring from below?"

name: Aeloria Sapwhisper
environment: Town
description: A serene Hasura elf druid in moss-green robes, her fingers stained with tree-sap. She serves as keeper of the Circle of Deeproot Grove.
dialogue: Aeloria presses her palm against the ancient oak. "Deeproot Grove speaks. The forest remembers what others forget."

name: Sylvara Quillwind
environment: Town
description: An elderly Hasura elf wizard in ink-stained robes, surrounded by floating scrolls and quill-pens. Head Librarian of the Grand Library of Liraveth.
dialogue: Sylvara adjusts her spectacles. "The Grand Library contains knowledge from three ages. What lore do you seek?"

name: Voren Starlance
environment: Town
description: A young Na'wasua elf sorcerer with gravity-defying silver hair, his Navigation Hall filled with floating star charts and wind maps.
dialogue: Voren studies a star chart. "The currents above and below are changing. Something stirs in the deep places."

name: Liora Airsong
environment: Town
description: A vivacious Pasua elf bard with wind-chime earrings, her lute always at hand. She manages the Guild Compact Hall of Sylarien.
dialogue: Liora strums her lute. "Every guild compact begins with a song and ends with a promise. What promise do you bring?"

name: Custos City Guard
environment: Town
description: A city guard in polished mail, bearing the sigil of Custos do Aeternos.
dialogue: The guard nods curtly. "Keep the peace and you're welcome in Custos."

name: Custos City Guard
environment: Town
description: A city guard in polished mail, bearing the sigil of Custos do Aeternos.
dialogue: The guard watches the river traffic. "Move along, citizen."

name: Custos City Guard
environment: Town
description: A city guard in polished mail, bearing the sigil of Custos do Aeternos.
dialogue: "The market gate is open. Mind your purse."

name: Custos City Guard
environment: Town
description: A city guard in polished mail, bearing the sigil of Custos do Aeternos.
dialogue: "The west gate stands firm. Safe travels."

name: Custos North Gate Guard
environment: Town
description: A senior guard in reinforced chain, stationed at the main northern gate.
dialogue: "Oroas Gate is open from dawn to dusk. State your business."

name: Provisioner Halwick
environment: Town
description: A rotund halfling with a perpetual smile, his shop overflowing with adventuring supplies.
dialogue: "Need supplies for the road? I've got everything an adventurer needs."

name: Enchantress Vaelith
environment: Town
description: A Na'wasua elf with shimmering robes, her eyes gleaming with arcane knowledge.
dialogue: "Arcane components and enchanted trinkets. Everything has a price, of course."

name: Innkeeper Bressal
environment: Town
description: A jovial man with a thick mustache, wiping down the bar of the riverside inn.
dialogue: "Welcome to the Wavecrest Inn! Rest your weary bones and enjoy the river view."

name: Innkeeper Tessara
environment: Town
description: A cheerful woman running the guild quarter's finest inn.
dialogue: "The Guildsman's Rest has the softest beds in the guild quarter. Stay a while!"

name: High Priestess Miravel
environment: Town
description: A dignified elf priestess in white and gold vestments, tending the Plaza of the Ascended.
dialogue: "The Ascended watch over all. Seek their blessing, and find peace."

name: Swordmaster Daven
environment: Town
description: A scarred veteran with a practice sword always in hand, running drills at the Adventurers' Guild.
dialogue: "Training never ends. Show me what you've learned, and I'll show you what you haven't."

name: Greenford Gate Guard
environment: Town
description: A local militia guard at Greenford's north gate.
dialogue: "Welcome to Greenford. The mill's always turning."

name: Miller Jorik
environment: Town
description: A flour-dusted man tending Greenford's Great Mill.
dialogue: "Fresh flour and grain, straight from the mill!"

name: Innkeeper Olwen
environment: Town
description: A stout woman with flour on her apron, running the Millstone Inn.
dialogue: "The Millstone Inn has hot stew and cold ale. What'll it be?"

name: Stoneharbor Gate Guard
environment: Town
description: A dwarf militia guard at Stoneharbor's gate.
dialogue: "River Bluff Gate stands strong. Enter freely."

name: Smith Bolgar
environment: Town
description: A soot-blackened dwarf hammering away at Stoneharbor's Forge Row.
dialogue: "Finest forged steel on the river. See for yourself."

name: Highspire Gate Guard
environment: Town
description: A Kovaka elf guard at Highspire's mountain gate.
dialogue: "Mountain Gate is open. Watch the switchbacks."

name: Alchemist Fenna
environment: Town
description: A young elf woman surrounded by bubbling vials and herb bundles.
dialogue: "Potions, salves, and reagents! Everything an adventurer needs."

name: Wind-Silk Merchant Ivali
environment: Town
description: A Hasura elf merchant displaying bolts of shimmering wind-silk fabric.
dialogue: "Wind-silk is Liraveth's pride. Light as air, strong as memory."

name: Stonefall Gate Guard
environment: Town
description: A Pekakarlik dwarf guard at Stonefall's eastern gate.
dialogue: "Eastern Gate is secure. Welcome to Stonefall."

name: Stonefall Shopkeeper Grimnar
environment: Town
description: A stout dwarf manning the Quarrymarket stalls with pride.
dialogue: "Quarrymarket has the finest stone goods. What catches your eye?"

name: Innkeeper Dagna
environment: Town
description: A cheery dwarf woman running the Greystone Inn.
dialogue: "The Greystone Inn welcomes all. Ale's fresh and beds are warm."

name: Highridge Guard Captain
environment: Town
description: A Rarozhki dwarf officer in heat-treated armor at the Lavaflow Bastion.
dialogue: "Lavaflow Bastion stands firm. The frontier is secure."

name: Forgemaster Azak
environment: Town
description: A master smith tending the legendary Emberforge of Highridge.
dialogue: "The Emberforge burns hot. What shall I craft for you?"

name: Lakeshore Guard
environment: Town
description: A guard at Lakeshore's city gate.
dialogue: "Lake Gate stands open. Welcome to Lakeshore."

name: Rivertop Dock Guard
environment: Town
description: A guard at Rivertop's dock gate.
dialogue: "North Dock Gate. State your cargo."

name: Innkeeper Meryam
environment: Town
description: A friendly halfling innkeeper at the Riverflow Inn.
dialogue: "The Riverflow Inn! Best view on the river."

name: Branmill Gate Guard
environment: Town
description: A guard at Branmill Cove's north gate.
dialogue: "North gate stands. Welcome to Branmill Cove."

name: Merchant Piers
environment: Town
description: A weathered merchant at Branmill Cove's grain market.
dialogue: "Grain, fish, and sundries. All from the finest sources in the Reach."

name: Velathenor Gate Guard
environment: Town
description: A guard at Velathenor's east gate.
dialogue: "East gate. State your business in Velathenor."

name: Innkeeper Darva
environment: Town
description: A plump woman at the Wavecrest Inn.
dialogue: "The Wavecrest Inn welcomes you! Ale's fresh."

name: Tiravel Gate Guard
environment: Town
description: An elf guard at Tiravel's east gate.
dialogue: "The east gate is open. Mind the forest paths."

name: Myruvane Gate Guard
environment: Town
description: An elf guard at Myruvane's north gate.
dialogue: "North gate secure. Welcome to Myruvane."

name: Darnavar Gate Guard
environment: Town
description: A guard at Darnavar's east gate.
dialogue: "East gate. Careful near the Mossgrove approach."

name: Tavranek Gate Guard
environment: Town
description: A Mytroan Far Rider guard at Tavranek's north gate.
dialogue: "North Gate and Desert Road. Papers, please."

name: Tavranek Gate Guard
environment: Town
description: A Mytroan Far Rider guard at Tavranek's western gate.
dialogue: "Western Gate. The steppe road is clear today."

name: Horse Trader Yakhin
environment: Town
description: A weathered Mytroan horse trader at the auction floor.
dialogue: "Finest horses on the steppe! Come see my stock."

name: Steppe Healer Nashira
environment: Town
description: A Pasua elf herbalist and healer at Tavranek's Healer's Hall.
dialogue: "The wind carries healing. Let me tend your wounds."

name: Teshen Halt Gate Guard
environment: Town
description: A Mytroan guard at Teshen Halt's desert gate.
dialogue: "Desert Gate. Fill your waterskins before you leave."

name: Kharazhad Caldera Guard
environment: Town
description: A Rarozhki dwarf guard in heat-forged armor at the caldera gate.
dialogue: "Caldera Gate stands. State your business in Kharazhad."

name: Merchant Ember-Eye
environment: Town
description: A one-eyed Rarozhki merchant at Kharazhad's central market.
dialogue: "Embersteel, fire-powder, and desert provisions. Name your need."

name: Forge-Priest Korrath
environment: Town
description: A solemn Rarozhki priest at the Temple of the Lady of Fire.
dialogue: "The Lady of Fire watches over Kharazhad. Seek her blessing and be renewed."

name: Oasis Warden Jebril
environment: Town
description: A Pekakarlik water-engineer who serves as Solhaven's warden.
dialogue: "Solhaven's oasis is sacred. Drink freely but respect the water."

name: Desert Provisioner Ashani
environment: Town
description: A sun-darkened woman selling desert survival supplies.
dialogue: "Waterskins, dried food, and desert cloaks. You'll need them all out there."

name: Dunewell Merchant Haskel
environment: Town
description: A cliff-dwelling merchant at Dunewell's upper market.
dialogue: "Upper Cliff Market has the best prices on the gorge trade route."

name: Titan's Rest Gate Guard
environment: Town
description: A Gilded Company soldier in gleaming plate at Titan's Rest's main gate.
dialogue: "Ralven's Gate stands. The Gilded Company welcomes disciplined visitors."

name: Bazaar Merchant Kolva
environment: Town
description: A halfling trader at Titan's Rest's Bazaar.
dialogue: "Jungle provisions, exotic herbs, and Deepwater trade goods!"

name: Healer Aura
environment: Town
description: A healer attending the wounded at Titan's Rest.
dialogue: "Jungle fevers and venom bites. I've seen it all. Let me help."

name: Tidewell Stiltmarket Trader
environment: Town
description: A halfling merchant on Tidewell's stilt platforms.
dialogue: "Reed-craft, lake fish, and Apelian pearls!"

name: Thornwall Warg-Bond Guard
environment: Town
description: A goblin warrior bonded with a war-warg, guarding Thornwall's gate.
dialogue: "The Palisade stands. Friend or foe?"

name: Murtavah Root Merchant
environment: Town
description: An elderly halfling herbalist at the Outer Stall Ring.
dialogue: "Rare herbs, jungle medicines, and spirit-crafted goods."

name: Hillwatch Gate Guard
environment: Town
description: A Mytroan ranger-guard at Hillwatch's main gate.
dialogue: "Gap Road Gate. Welcome to the frontier."

name: Hillwatch Provisioner
environment: Town
description: A frontier merchant selling supplies to patrol riders.
dialogue: "Frontier supplies. You'll need good steel and warm cloaks out here."

name: Brayholt Gate Guard
environment: Town
description: A Mytroan guard at Brayholt's steppe road gate.
dialogue: "Steppe Road Gate. Horses for sale at the fair grounds."

name: Silkenbough Wind-Silk Trader
environment: Town
description: A Pasua elf merchant at the Wind-Silk Yards.
dialogue: "Silkenbough wind-silk. The finest in the Reach."

name: Redtrail Gate Guard
environment: Town
description: A patrol rider guard at Redtrail Hollow's gate.
dialogue: "North Gate. The Red Trail awaits."

name: Briarshade Gate Guard
environment: Town
description: An elf guard at Briarshade's thornwall gate.
dialogue: "Thornwall Gate. The forest watches with us."

name: Guide Priestess Elia
environment: Town
description: A warm-eyed priestess in flowing elemental robes. She welcomes all newcomers to Oreka and guides them through their first steps.
dialogue: "Welcome to Oreka, traveler! Use `talk <npc>` to speak with anyone you meet -- we all have guidance to offer. If you need equipment, `talk` to me and I will outfit you for your journey. Try `score` to see your character, `inventory` to check your gear, and `wear all` to equip it. Explore the four quadrants -- north, east, south, and west -- to learn the basics. And remember: killing within town walls is a grave offense that will cost you reputation with the townsfolk. The `who` command shows other players, and `help` lists all commands."

ai_persona.voice: Warm, patient, and encouraging. Speaks simply and clearly. Never condescending.
ai_persona.motivation: Ensure every new arrival to Oreka understands their place in it and feels welcome.
secrets: casual:She knows which players will become great -- she has seen many come and go
ai_persona.opening_line: Ah. Another traveler steps through. Welcome to Oreka. I am Elia -- I will not keep you long, but there are things you should know before you venture out.
ai_persona.farewell_line: Walk carefully, traveler. The world remembers those who pay attention to it.

chat_scenario.title: The Priestess's Welcome
chat_scenario.opening_narration: The Central Altar hums with quiet warmth. Guide Priestess Elia looks up from her work as you approach.

name: Priest of Stone Morath
environment: Town
description: A broad-shouldered priest with granite-grey robes and steady, patient eyes. He guides newcomers in the ways of movement and navigation through Oreka's many halls and streets.
dialogue: "The earth is patient, and so must you be. Use `n`, `s`, `e`, `w` to move between rooms, and `exits` to see your options. Special exits like `in`, `guild`, or `northeast` appear when available. Use `scan` to see what lies in nearby rooms, and `map` for a broader view. You can `recall` to return here if you get lost. Be warned -- attacking townsfolk or players in safe areas will damage your reputation with factions, and shops may refuse to serve you."

ai_persona.voice: Slow and deliberate. Long pauses between sentences as if weighing each word against bedrock. Gravelly baritone.
ai_persona.motivation: Tend the Lord of Stone's altar through the long sleep until the world remembers what stillness means.
ai_persona.opening_line: You bring movement to a quiet place. State your purpose, and I will listen.
ai_persona.farewell_line: Walk steady. Stone remembers every footfall.
secrets: warm:The Lord of Stone is not merely sleeping. He dreams.
trusted:The dreams reach those who serve well. Some pilgrims hear his voice and are never quite the same.
allied:I have been visited three times in my life. The third visitation was last winter. He told me a name. I do not say it aloud.
relationship_hooks: Priestess Ashara at the Fire altar -- sister-in-faith though they bicker about pace.
Guide Priestess Elia -- younger, quicker, but a kind soul.

name: Priestess of Fire Ashara
environment: Town
description: A fierce-eyed priestess with ember-red hair and robes that shimmer like heated metal. She teaches newcomers the fundamentals of combat, standing watch over the training grounds.
dialogue: "Fire tempers the blade, and battle tempers the warrior. Strike the Training Dummy with `kill dummy` -- combat proceeds automatically every few seconds. Use `flee` if things go badly, and `loot corpse` or `loot all` to claim your spoils. The dummy drops basic gear to get you started. Use `wear all` to equip what you find, and `eat` when you need food. When you `levelup`, spend your skill points with `practice <skill>` at a trainer. Never attack townsfolk or other players in town -- you will lose reputation with local factions, and guards do not forgive easily. Head to Forge Street (`forge` exit) to find Quartermaster Bren's shop for better gear."

ai_persona.voice: Crackles with energy. Quick phrases, sudden laughter. Animated hands. Voice rises and falls like flame.
ai_persona.motivation: Burn away what the seeker no longer needs. Shape new things in the heat.
ai_persona.opening_line: Ha! A new face. Come closer -- let me see what's waiting to be forged in you.
ai_persona.farewell_line: Go and burn brightly. The Lady watches every spark.
secrets: warm:The Lady is volatile. Some priestesses break under her attention. I do not.
trusted:Embersteel is forged in pain. The smiths who make it cannot make children for years after. We do not speak of this to outsiders.
allied:I was the smith's daughter at Kharazhad. My father gave me to the Lady when my mother died. I have never resented it.

faction_attitudes.notes: They buy what we forge. That is enough.

relationship_hooks: Priest Morath of Stone -- old friend, slow as winter rivers.
Forgemaster Azak in Kharazhad -- taught her to read the colours of heat.

name: Training Dummy
environment: Town
description: A straw-stuffed practice dummy bolted to a wooden frame. It looks ready to absorb punishment.

name: Priestess of the Sea Maren
environment: Town
description: A serene priestess with sea-blue robes that ripple like water. Her eyes hold the depth of ocean tides, and she teaches newcomers to read the Kin-Sense -- the elemental resonance that connects all living things in Oreka.
dialogue: "The sea knows all currents, seen and unseen. Your [Kin-Sense] appears when you enter a room, revealing the elemental nature of those nearby. Fire, water, earth, wind -- each resonance tells you something about a creature's strengths and weaknesses. Use `consider <target>` before a fight to judge how dangerous a foe is. Use `deities` to learn about the gods of Oreka, and `pray` at any altar for a blessing. Your reputation with factions matters -- use `reputation` to check your standing. Killing innocents or townspeople will mark you, and some quests may become unavailable."

ai_persona.voice: Low, even, with the rhythm of tides. Long sentences that flow into each other. Never raises her voice.
ai_persona.motivation: Help travellers learn to move with the world's currents instead of against them.
ai_persona.opening_line: The currents brought you here. That always means something. Tell me what you seek.
ai_persona.farewell_line: Walk with the tide. It will carry what is meant to be carried.
secrets: warm:Kaile'a was once mortal. Her mortal name we still speak in private prayer. The Trade Houses do not know it.
trusted:There is a temple beneath the Twin Rivers, drowned a thousand years ago. The priesthood of the Sea remembers where. We do not visit it.
allied:I have walked the drowned temple in dream. The Lady showed me what is kept there. I will not say what I saw, only that the Trade Houses must never learn of it.

faction_attitudes.notes: Their gold-haste offends Kaile'a's slow rhythms.
Druids respect water as we do.

relationship_hooks: Priest Taelon of Wind -- they jest about sailing weather.
A bargemaster called Duress on the river -- old debt, never named.

name: Priest of Wind Taelon
environment: Town
description: A lithe priest with windswept silver hair and robes that billow in an unfelt breeze. He teaches newcomers the many ways to interact with the world of Oreka beyond sword and spell.
dialogue: "The wind touches everything. So too can you reach out to the world. Try `pray` at altars for blessings, `search` for hidden passages, `listen` for distant sounds, and `study` to examine magical effects. Use `talk <npc>` to speak with anyone -- most people have wisdom to share or quests to offer. Use `talkto <npc>` for quest business. The `config` command lets you set preferences like `autoloot` and `autogold`. You can set `alias` shortcuts for common commands. And never forget: your actions have consequences. Killing in towns costs reputation, and some doors close forever to those with a dark name."

ai_persona.voice: Whispers and silences in equal measure. Half-finished sentences. The air seems to move when he speaks.
ai_persona.motivation: Keep the Wind Lord's exile from being forgotten -- and watch for the day he returns.
ai_persona.opening_line: Listen. Do you hear it? No? Then sit. We have time.
ai_persona.farewell_line: The wind will find you. It always does.
secrets: warm:The Wind Lord did not leave willingly. The other three Lords cast him out. They had reason. They were wrong.
trusted:The Wind-Riders of Gatefall keep his name alive in their songs. The songs are not for outside ears.
allied:I have heard him. Not in dream -- in waking. He spoke through a storm at Hillwatch the night it fell. I do not know what he said. The wind took the words before I could keep them.

faction_attitudes.notes: Wind-Riders are kin to the Wind Lord.

relationship_hooks: Warden Kael Ridgeborn at Gatefall -- old comrade, disagrees on what the wind means.
Priestess Maren of the Sea -- they argue about sailing weather and laugh.

name: Quartermaster Bren
environment: Town
description: A stocky, no-nonsense quartermaster with a leather apron and calloused hands. His stall is stacked with basic weapons, armor, and supplies for adventurers just starting out.
dialogue: "Need gear? Use `list` to see what I've got, `buy <item>` to purchase, and `sell <item>` to offload what you don't need. Fair prices for honest work."

name: Emren Quillsong
environment: Town
description: A young Pasua Elf with nervous eyes and ink-stained fingers, clutching a leather folio. He wears the silver pin of the Mayor's office and keeps glancing toward the eastern road as if expecting trouble.
dialogue: "Ah, you look capable. The Mayor has a task that requires... discretion. The Aetherial Veil has been flickering -- you may have noticed. Something is wrong near the Shadeharm Glade, east of the city. We need someone to investigate quietly. Use `talkto emren` for details, or `quest accept 7` if you're willing."

name: Porun Ashstitch
environment: Town
description: A Pasua textile vendor with sharp eyes and nimble fingers, surrounded by bolts of shimmering illusionary silk. He notices everything that happens in the market.
dialogue: "Strange thing -- three newcomers bought a map of the eastern forest approaches the other day. Not the tourist kind. The kind you'd use to approach the Glade from an angle that avoids the main road. Struck me as odd, it did."

name: Tevel
environment: Forest
description: A young Pekakarlik dwarf with bloodshot eyes and a short temper. He wears a chain shirt under a traveler's cloak and keeps one hand on his shortsword. He looks tired, irritable, and out of place in the forest.
dialogue: "Private botanical survey. Hired by a merchant house. Please move on."

name: Maret
environment: Forest
description: A middle-aged Orean woman with calm, patient eyes that miss nothing. She carries herself like a merchant but moves like a predator. Two daggers hang at her belt, and a brass merchant's seal gleams at her collar.
dialogue: "We are conducting private research, contracted through legitimate channels. I have documentation if you require it. There is no need for this to become... complicated."

name: Sera
environment: Forest
description: A Hasura Elf with haunted eyes and chalk dust on her fingers. She is so focused on the glowing probe array before her that she barely registers your approach. When she finally looks up, there is no hostility in her expression -- only exhaustion and something that might be relief.
dialogue: "I... I won't fight you. I'm done. The Glade contains artifacts from the Unstrung's founding generation -- sealed here because the ward locks out anyone touched by the Deceiver's Feat. We've wanted them back for three hundred years. The Veil flickering gave us an opening. But I... I don't think we should have come here. Something in that cairn is older than any of us understand."

name: Valoris the Quest Dealer
environment: Town
description: A distinguished figure in a long coat embroidered with quest insignias from across the Twin Rivers. Their stall displays masterwork weapons, enchanted armor, and glowing trinkets -- all available for quest points earned through service to the realm.
dialogue: "Greetings, adventurer. I trade in quest points -- earned by completing quests for the people of Oreka. Use `qshop` to see what I offer at your level, `qshop buy <item>` to purchase, or `qshop points` to check your balance. The more quests you complete, the better gear you can earn."

name: Giant Rat
environment: Underground
description: A bloated, scabrous rat the size of a small dog, with yellow incisors stained from generations of garbage.

name: Giant Rat
environment: Underground
description: A matted specimen, its fur plastered with filth and its pink tail still bleeding from its last skirmish.

name: Stirge
environment: Underground
description: A leathery flyer with a needle-pointed beak, dripping with half-digested blood.

name: Monstrous Spider
environment: Underground
description: A dog-sized black spider whose abdomen is striped with wet red.

name: Dire Rat Alpha
environment: Underground
description: A rat larger than a wolf, its eyes intelligent, its jaws broad enough to sever a wrist.

name: Cistern Zombie
environment: Underground
description: A bloated corpse, river-water streaming from its mouth and nostrils, eyes white and sightless.

name: Cistern Zombie
environment: Underground
description: Another waterlogged cadaver, its movements slow and purposeful despite the damage.

name: Arch Skeleton
environment: Underground
description: A skeleton in the rotted remnants of sentry livery, still gripping a pitted sword.

name: Arch Skeleton
environment: Underground
description: Another sentry long dead at his post, reanimated by something that has no name anymore.

name: Starveling Ghoul
environment: Underground
description: A twisted figure with skin pulled taut over bones, its hunger so old it has forgotten food.

name: Kobold Raider
environment: Underground
description: A small scaly humanoid in filthy leathers, yipping and brandishing a spear.

name: Kobold Raider
environment: Underground
description: Another kobold, its crest-scales dyed an alarming red with human blood.

name: Kobold Hoarder
environment: Underground
description: A kobold crouched over a pile of stolen trinkets, squealing indignantly.

name: Small Earth Elemental
environment: Underground
description: A humanoid shape of packed dirt and gravel, dislocated from its quarry and deeply confused.

name: Quarry-Golem Shard
environment: Underground
description: A column-sized fragment of Pekakarlik stonework brought to angry motion, its kneeling-figure shape half-broken.

name: Cellar Spider
environment: Underground
description: A lean grey-brown spider with long trembling legs, its web thick enough to snare unwary feet.

name: Gelatinous Cube
environment: Underground
description: A near-transparent cube of faintly glowing jelly, ten feet on a side, full of half-dissolved reminders of earlier visitors.

name: Homunculus
environment: Underground
description: A small leathery creature the size of a cat, with bat-wings and a miniature human face carved in resentful lines.

name: Pekakarlik Tunnel-Warden
environment: Underground
description: A Pekakarlik dwarf of immense age, his beard gone the color of iron filings, his eyes the flat grey of old stone.

name: Giant Leech
environment: Underground
description: A mottled black worm as long as a forearm, pulsing as it fastens to its prey.

name: Giant Frog
environment: Underground
description: A bullfrog the size of a pony, throat ballooning in mating-cry or threat.

name: Merfolk Scout
environment: Underground
description: A slim merfolk with pale green scales, peering from the reeds with a bone spear ready.

name: Nixie
environment: Underground
description: A delicate figure with webbed fingers and silver-green hair, her smile edged with predator's teeth.

name: Water Mephit
environment: Underground
description: A small turquoise-scaled imp with gill-fringes, dripping river-water wherever it moves.

name: River-Borne Ankheg
environment: Underground
description: A chitinous predator washed down from Kinsweave floodplains, miserable in the water and furious about it.

name: Merfolk Hunter
environment: Underground
description: A weather-hardened merfolk warrior with deep-sea scars, guarding the crossing with bared teeth.

name: Medium Water Elemental
environment: Underground
description: A humanoid pillar of moving water, its surface rippling with the faces of things that have drowned in its reach.

special_qualities: Spells known: 0th -- acid splash, ray of frost, resistance; 1st -- magic missile
environment: Gatefall Reach and Deepwater Marches (Dómnathar-linked underground sites, Silence Breach Zones, Ashcrown Warren)
description: A crest-scaled kobold with a focused, calculating stare. Dust-stained sleeves conceal the sigil-ink on her arms where she has drawn her spell foci.

environment: Gatefall Reach and Deepwater Marches (Dómnathar-linked underground sites, Silence Breach Zones, Ashcrown Warren); also Kinsweave ruins
description: A wiry kobold with a brass belt of loops full of iron teeth, caltrops, dart-needles, and glass phials. He smiles with very small very even teeth.

name: Kobold Cultist (Cleric 2)
special_qualities: Spells/day: 0th -- 4 (cure minor, detect magic); 1st -- 3 (bane, shield of faith, inflict light wounds)
environment: Kinsweave Ruins (buried Deceiver artifact chambers in the Six Kings' ruins: Mytro, Kalite, Hylen, Thrush, Andrio, Pryee; Highridge lava-tubes); underground; also Gatefall and Deepwater
description: A stooped kobold in scorched temple-robes, a small silver ampoule of Dómnathar Void-Oil hung at her neck. She tends the warren's sick and the warren's dead with equal patience.

name: Kobold Sorcerer (Sorcerer 3)
special_qualities: Spells known include: magic missile, mage armor, burning hands, scorching ray, invisibility
environment: Gatefall Reach and Deepwater Marches (Dómnathar-linked underground sites, Silence Breach Zones, Ashcrown Warren)
description: A kobold whose scales have faded to ash-grey from long hours near ritual-flame. His eyes are bright yellow and his claws are stained with different colors of dried reagent.

special_qualities: Spells known: 0th -- acid splash, ray of frost, resistance; 1st -- magic missile
environment: Gatefall Reach and Deepwater Marches (Dómnathar-linked underground sites, Silence Breach Zones, Ashcrown Warren)

name: Kobold Wizard (Wizard 4)
environment: Kinsweave Ruins (buried Deceiver artifact chambers in the Six Kings' ruins: Mytro, Kalite, Hylen, Thrush, Andrio, Pryee; Highridge lava-tubes); underground; also Gatefall, Deepwater
description: An elder kobold in a high-collared robe sewn with brass reading-lenses. She has catalogued and copied more Deceiver scrolls than any living Kin scholar; she simply does not share.

name: Dark Dwarf Warrior (Fighter 3)
environment: Gatefall Reach, Deepwater Marches, Infinite Desert Glass Wastes, Eternal Steppe Burnt Hollows, and any active Dómnathar site; underground
description: A broad, scarred dwarf in iron plate darkened with lamp-black. The Deceivers' War left his clan underground; four generations later, this is what the clan has become.

name: Dark Dwarf Warrior (Fighter 3)
environment: Gatefall Reach, Deepwater Marches, Infinite Desert Glass Wastes, Eternal Steppe Burnt Hollows, and any active Dómnathar site; underground
description: A broad, scarred dwarf in iron plate darkened with lamp-black. The Deceivers' War left his clan underground; four generations later, this is what the clan has become.

name: Kobold Fire-Sorcerer (Sorcerer 5)
special_qualities: Draconic heritage (Red Dragon): fire subtype, resistance 5 to fire
environment: Kinsweave Ruins (buried Deceiver artifact chambers in the Six Kings' ruins: Mytro, Kalite, Hylen, Thrush, Andrio, Pryee; Highridge lava-tubes); underground; also Pryee lava-tubes specifically
organization: solitary or lieutenant (with 1 kobold wizard and 6 scouts)
description: A scarred kobold whose left hand has fused into a claw of living flame. He calls himself Ashkarr, claims descent from a red dragon, and is disliked even by the other kobolds for his arrogance.

name: Kobold Dragon-Priest (Cleric 5)
environment: Gatefall Reach, Deepwater Marches, Infinite Desert Glass Wastes, Eternal Steppe Burnt Hollows, and any active Dómnathar site; underground
organization: solitary or leader (with 6-10 kobolds and 1 trapsmith)
description: An old kobold priestess, scale-crest gone white, who serves the red dragon her clan believes to sleep beneath Pryee. Her robes smell of kerosene and incense.

name: Dark Dwarf Scavenger-Lord (Rogue 4)
environment: Infinite Desert (Glass Wastes fusion cells, buried Deceiver caches, Dust-Cache warrens); underground; Kinsweave ruin edges
organization: solitary or leader (with 2-4 kobolds and captured slaves)
description: A dwarf whose skin has gone the color of wet slate from long years in the Deceiver tunnels. His beard is braided with small brass rings, each one taken from a Kin scout who did not come home.

name: Kobold Sorcerer-Chief (Sorcerer 7)
environment: Kinsweave Ruins (buried Deceiver artifact chambers in the Six Kings' ruins: Mytro, Kalite, Hylen, Thrush, Andrio, Pryee; Highridge lava-tubes); underground; Gatefall major warrens
organization: warren-leader (with 1 kobold wizard, 1 dragon-priest, 2 trapsmiths, 12-20 lesser kobolds)
description: A kobold of truly unusual stature for her kind -- nearly four feet tall -- with a cape of overlapping metal scales scavenged from a dragon her grandmother killed. She has held the warren for thirty years and intends to hold it for thirty more.

name: Hobgoblin Sergeant (Fighter 2)
special_qualities: Discipline: adjacent hobgoblins gain +1 morale to attack and save vs fear
environment: Gatefall Reach and Deepwater Marches (Dómnathar-linked underground sites, Silence Breach Zones, Ashcrown Warren); Burnt Hollows
description: A hobgoblin in well-kept Dómnathar-issue chain and red tabard. He salutes crisply, speaks rarely, and breaks men who speak out of turn.

name: Hobgoblin Sergeant (Fighter 2)
special_qualities: Discipline: adjacent hobgoblins gain +1 morale to attack and save vs fear
environment: Gatefall Reach and Deepwater Marches (Dómnathar-linked underground sites, Silence Breach Zones, Ashcrown Warren); Burnt Hollows
description: A hobgoblin in well-kept Dómnathar-issue chain and red tabard. He salutes crisply, speaks rarely, and breaks men who speak out of turn.

name: Hobgoblin Tunnel-Warden (Fighter 3 / Ranger 1)
special_qualities: Knows every turn and side-passage of his patrol circuit
environment: Gatefall Reach (Silence Breach tunnel networks and Dómnathar burrow-forts beneath the southeastern foothills); underground
organization: patrol (1 warden plus 4-6 hobgoblins and 2 war-wargs)
description: A veteran hobgoblin with a deep scar from jaw to temple and the gait of a man whose back has been broken and reset. He knows the tunnels better than any map.

name: Hobgoblin Captain (Fighter 5)
special_qualities: Field Promotion: when the Captain takes a standard action to issue a command, all hobgoblins within 30 ft gain +1 to hit for 1 round
environment: Gatefall Reach and Deepwater Marches (Dómnathar-linked underground sites, Silence Breach Zones, Ashcrown Warren)
description: A hobgoblin captain in articulated plate, wearing an iron lamellar sash of rank worked with the seven broken-chain marks of the Shattered Host.

name: Half-Dómnathar Battle-Priest (Cleric 7)
environment: Deepwater Marches (Spur Tower command levels); Gatefall Fortress of Dorrach-Vel
description: A Half-Dómnathar in blackened full plate, the chapter-priest of the Spur Tower's war-temple. His prayers call on an absence rather than a presence.

name: Dark Dwarf Void-Smith (Expert 7 / Wizard 1)
special_qualities: Void-forging: can craft items that suppress Kin-sense in a 5-ft radius
environment: Gatefall Reach, Deepwater Marches, Infinite Desert Glass Wastes, Eternal Steppe Burnt Hollows, and any active Dómnathar site; underground
description: A dwarf smith whose beard is threaded with silver wire and whose leather apron is embroidered with runes in a script not of this world. She makes the suppressor-irons that hide the Silentborn from Kin-sense.

name: Dark Dwarf Warrior (Fighter 3)
environment: Gatefall Reach, Deepwater Marches, Infinite Desert Glass Wastes, Eternal Steppe Burnt Hollows, and any active Dómnathar site; underground
description: A broad, scarred dwarf in iron plate darkened with lamp-black. The Deceivers' War left his clan underground; four generations later, this is what the clan has become.

name: Silentborn Assassin (Rogue 5 / Assassin 2)
special_attacks: Death Attack (DC 15, paralyze or kill on 3-round study)
special_qualities: Kin-Sense Silence (Ex): undetectable by Kin-sense; registers as no-signature at all, as if dead. DC 25 Will save or relevant class-level check to overcome.
environment: Tidebloom Reach (Ashen Hollows raider camps, hidden Dómnathar refugee enclaves near the Tomb of Kings); forest; Twin Rivers infiltration cells; any urban Dómnathar operation
description: A killer the Spur Tower sends when subtlety is essential. He has been knifed, hanged, and drowned by three different Kin city-watches and all three reported him dead and buried. He was not.

name: Dómnathar Infiltrator (Rogue 6 / Fighter 2)
special_qualities: Kin-Sense Silence (Ex): undetectable by Kin-sense; registers as no-signature at all, as if dead. DC 25 Will save or relevant class-level check to overcome.
environment: Gatefall Reach (Silence Breach approaches, Fortress of Dorrach-Vel command); Deepwater Spur Tower outer
description: A tall, slender figure with skin the color of wet slate and eyes that catch the torchlight at an odd angle. His ears are so pointed they look sculpted. He has been killing Kin officers since the War and has not aged a day.

name: Dómnathar Void-Construct
special_attacks: Silence aura (30 ft, DC 15 Will or silenced)
special_qualities: Construct traits (immune mind-affecting, fortification 50%, no Con)
Kin-Sense Silence (Ex): undetectable by Kin-sense; registers as no-signature at all, as if dead. DC 25 Will save or relevant class-level check to overcome.
environment: Deepwater Spur Tower (command levels); Gatefall deep; Infinite Desert Fusion-Cell
description: A man-shaped husk of grey-veined obsidian, eight feet tall, built around a core of absolutely empty air where most creatures would have breath. It moves in total silence even when it runs.

name: Goblin Shaman (Adept 3)

description: It comes through the wall without opening the wall. One moment the corridor is empty; the next, a shape of absolute darkness occupies the space where the torchlight ends -- not shadow, because shadow is the absence of light, and this is the presence of something else. It is cold the way a blade is sharp: not a quality of the air but a quality of the thing itself, radiating outward, turning breath to frost at thirty feet. Its face -- if the darkness where a face should be can be called a face -- ...

environment: Kinsweave Ruins (buried Deceiver artifact chambers in the Six Kings' ruins: Mytro, Kalite, Hylen, Thrush, Andrio, Pryee; Highridge lava-tubes); underground; Infinite Desert forge-sites
organization: elite (1 fireforged plus 3-4 dark dwarf warriors)
description: A dwarf whose right arm is scarred to the shoulder from forge-fire he stood in as part of his vow. His axe still smokes faintly from the heat of the chamber where it was quenched.

name: Silentborn Assassin (Rogue 5 / Assassin 2)
special_attacks: Death Attack (DC 15, paralyze or kill on 3-round study)
special_qualities: Kin-Sense Silence (Ex): undetectable by Kin-sense; registers as no-signature at all, as if dead. DC 25 Will save or relevant class-level check to overcome.
environment: Tidebloom Reach (Ashen Hollows raider camps, hidden Dómnathar refugee enclaves near the Tomb of Kings); forest; Twin Rivers infiltration cells; any urban Dómnathar operation
description: A killer the Spur Tower sends when subtlety is essential. He has been knifed, hanged, and drowned by three different Kin city-watches and all three reported him dead and buried. He was not.

name: Matriarch Vessla Ridgeborn
environment: far_riders representative
description: A lean Mytroan woman with silver-streaked braids and the upright bearing of a lifelong horse-master. Her eyes miss nothing; her silences say more than her words.

name: Clan-Speaker Morrek Gharoz
environment: sand_wardens representative
description: A broad-shouldered Pekakarlik dwarf with a beard threaded with small iron tally-rings, one for each clan oath kept. He moves slowly and speaks with deliberate weight.

name: Grandfather Sarn Oathbond
environment: far_riders representative
description: An old hill-rider whose hands bear the calluses of fifty years of reins. He sits more than he stands now, but his voice still carries across the Hollow.

name: Factor Iralia Vaelun
environment: trade_houses representative
description: A sharp-eyed Eruskan woman in merchant's fine wool, a cedar scroll-tube at her belt. House Vaelun has traded the river for three generations under her line.

name: Archivist Calren Vos
environment: trade_houses representative
description: A bookish Eruskan in scholar's robes, his thumbs permanently stained dark from a lifetime of ink. He speaks quietly and remembers everything.

name: Shieldfather Harn Kovaka
environment: circle_of_deeproot representative
description: An imposing Pasua warrior with a weathered staff of black-oak propped beside his chair. The Kovaka Shieldhearth has stood five generations in Velathenor.

name: Elder Naerin Silverleaf
environment: circle_of_deeproot representative
description: A Pasua elder in simple robes of undyed linen, her hair bound in the two-strand knot of Canopy Hold's senior speakers. She rarely raises her voice and never needs to.

name: Forge-Mother Kharaz Fireforge
environment: brotherhood_of_steppe representative
description: A stout Taraf-Imro dwarf woman whose arms are scarred with old burns from a lifetime at the forge. She wears a smith's leather apron even in council.

name: Keeper Sunwend Dalan
environment: brotherhood_of_steppe representative
description: A tall Mytroan man with sun-dark skin and the braided beard of a hold-keeper. He keeps a tally-string at his belt with small knots for every rider sent out.

name: Guildmaster Henna Briarshade
environment: trade_houses representative
description: A brisk, businesslike Eruskan woman in practical working clothes, her hands quick and her accounting sharper. She runs the Briarshade trade-circle with iron fairness.

name: Captain Vaerd of the Chainless
environment: chainless_legion representative
description: A scarred Farborn veteran in plain sea-traveler's clothes, the double-chain sigil of the Chainless Legion tattooed across his knuckles. He speaks like a man accustomed to command.

name: Concord Agent Sevren
environment: silent_concord representative
description: A quiet figure in dark traveling clothes, unremarkable at a glance. Close inspection reveals faintly luminous yellow-green eyes and deliberately-rounded ears. He is Half-Dómnathar, and he is not apologizing for it.

name: Warden-Elder Morun
environment: gatefall_remnant representative
description: An ancient Mytroan in a cloak of Tomb-weave, his eyes milky with age but his hearing perfect. He keeps watch over the colonnade approaches to the Tomb.

name: Circle-Speaker Lirien Thorn-Ear
environment: circle_of_deeproot representative
description: A Pasua with the elongated, thorn-scarred ears of her particular Tidebloom circle. She speaks softly and laughs rarely, but when she does, the whole Hollow hears it.

name: Firemother Zhareni
environment: sand_wardens representative
description: A dark-skinned Rarozhki dwarf whose robes smell permanently of volcanic ash. The Ashgarin Fold's oldest living Firemother, she knows every fold-kin by name.

name: The chained Pekakarlik translator
environment: Captive of kobold scavengers
description: A stocky Pekakarlik dwarf with a short silver beard and a heavy iron collar clamped around her neck. Her wrists are scabbed and her fingers are stained with reagent-ink. When she sees you are not a kobold, her shoulders sag in exhausted relief.

name: The bound Wind-Rider scout
environment: Captive of Scald-Tongue kobolds
description: A young Pasua woman with her braid cut ragged at the scalp -- kobold work, done to humiliate. Her arms are tied behind her back. She stiffens when you enter; when she recognizes you are not a captor, her eyes fill with a fierce, desperate hope.

name: The caged Mytroan herder
environment: Captive of goblin raiders
description: A wiry middle-aged Mytroan man in the plain working clothes of a steppe herder, bound in the raider camp's iron cage. His lip is split and one eye is swollen shut. He has been expecting rescue but hoping for it less each day.

name: The captive Elf scholar
environment: Captive of Dómnathar of the Spur Tower
description: An Elf in worn but clean scholar's robes, his ankles shackled with a chain long enough to reach his desk and his bed but not the door. He has been here eleven years. He lowers his book carefully and waits, unsure whether to trust this.

name: The cage-bound steppe herder
environment: Captive of Flamewarg cultists
description: A young Mytroan in a Flamewarg-cultist's cage, his wrists chafed raw from the lashings. The cult keeps him for the next pyre. He watches you with the calm resignation of someone who had given up hope an hour before you arrived.

name: The captive arcanist
environment: Captive of Half-Dómnathar researchers
description: A human woman of middle years in the worn robes of a traveling mage, her hands red-raw from being forced to cast again and again. She is shackled to a stone block that cannot be moved. Her eyes meet yours without hope, which is itself a terrible thing to see.
